races.race_name: Yorkshire Equine Practice Handicap

runners.name: Captain Parma
runners.spotlight: Consistent record on AW features two wins; far less solid on turf form (finishing positions read 790) and can be opposed in a competitive affair kept to this sphere.
runners.comment: Consistent on AW; still has something to prove on turf

runners.name: Montezin
runners.spotlight: Raced only four times, all over 5f/6f, showing solid AW promise (221) last autumn then posting a duck egg in Newbury handicap on reappearance; may still do better but has enough to prove upped in trip and kept to turf.
runners.comment: May still do better but has enough to prove upped in trip and kept to turf

runners.name: Mereside Diva
runners.spotlight: Versatile as regards ground; form this year features an AW success in February and close second at Wetherby (7f) last time; five-time winner on turf and C&D record includes two placed efforts; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Close second at Wetherby last time; C&D record includes two placed efforts

runners.name: Zarathos
runners.spotlight: Finally got the job done in Windsor contest (1m, good to firm) last week and did it comfortably, opening his account at the 18th attempt; great chance under a penalty, provided he's in the same form back down in trip; due to go up 4lb in future.
runners.comment: Comfortably opened his account in Windsor contest (1m) last week; 4lb well in
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Style Of Life
runners.spotlight: Broadly progressive record on turf for current yard features good-ground wins at Chester (7.5f) and Pontefract (1m) last June; may still have more to offer and remains of interest, despite an absence since July; market confidence would cement her claims.
runners.comment: Has a broadly progressive record on turf for current yard; remains of interest
runners.owner: Rise One Racing - Style Of Life

runners.name: Highland Olly
runners.spotlight: Has done all his winning on good ground, including over 1m at Nottingham and Ripon in last two starts; this front-runner switches to a much bigger field, which may not be ideal, but is otherwise at the top of his form; effective at 7f.
runners.comment: Big field may not be ideal but he's otherwise in top form and bidding for hat-trick

runners.name: Gressington
runners.spotlight: Campaigned exclusively on AW for current stable; out of sorts since December win; well handicapped on best turf form for previous yard, so has a good chance if the return to this sphere sparks a revival.
runners.comment: Campaigned on AW for current stable; well handicapped on best turf form
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Leadman
runners.spotlight: Has an underwhelming strike-rate, given his ability, and brings a doubt over current form; otherwise attractively treated off a mark 3lb lower than the one he defied at the 2025 Newmarket July festival.
runners.comment: Brings a doubt over current form but otherwise is on an attractive mark

runners.name: Aisling Oscar
runners.spotlight: Has done well on AW for current yard, completing a remarkable eight-timer along the way; failed to transfer the form back to turf last time, taking record in this sphere to 0-10; bit to prove off same mark.
runners.comment: Failed to transfer his AW form back to turf last time; 0-10 in this sphere

runners.name: North View
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful handicapper whose AW form this year for new yard features a 7f success in January and a creditable effort last time; effective on turf in the past but seems weighted to the hilt back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Switches back to turf from AW; looks weighted to the hilt

runners.name: Moon Beginnings
runners.spotlight: Record of 888 in handicaps (1m-1m2f) reflects his lack of progress since the novice performances that included a debut success in sole 7f attempt; however, this drop back in trip may prompt a resurgence.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 4yo whose sole attempt over 7f resulted in debut win
runners.owner: Nbr30, Mike Owen & Rasio Cymru Racing

runners.name: Harry The Rogue
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 5yo who has maintained his consistency in two starts for new stable, creditable fourth at Musselburgh (1m) latest; still has a modest strike-rate but is likely to give his running and again holds frame possibilities.
runners.comment: Has maintained his consistency for new yard; again holds frame possibilities

runners.name: Ormolulu
runners.spotlight: Has done all of her winning over 6f, mostly on Southwell AW; perhaps needed her Leicester reappearance effort but she's not the percentage call returned to 7f and kept to this sphere.
runners.comment: All wins over 6f, mostly on Southwell AW; not the percentage call

runners.name: Havana Prince
runners.spotlight: Couple of sound performances on Newcastle AW this year, winning over 7f latest; back on career-high mark but he's a five-time turf scorer who has form over C&D and appears to hold solid claims.
runners.comment: Scored on AW last time; five-time turf winner who has form over C&D; solid

runners.name: War Howl
runners.spotlight: Well treated on his warm spell (finishing positions 112) last summer; not in anything like the same form this year for new yard, however, and is best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Yet to show anything like his best form since joining new yard
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

runners.name: Dingle
runners.spotlight: Seven-time AW scorer, mainly over 1m, most recently in January; latest effort (failed to beat a rival) took turf record to 0-16; enough to prove back over 7f for first time in five years.
runners.comment: Latest effort took his turf record to 0-16; others preferred

runners.name: Nyman
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo whose form for new stable features a success at Newmarket (7f, good to soft) last autumn and creditable fourth-place finishes in last two outings (7.5f/1m); again has frame possibilities.
runners.comment: Creditable fourth in last two outings; again has frame possibilities

runners.name: Quest For Fun
runners.spotlight: Has plenty of C&D form, notably wins in 2023 and 2025 (good to soft) and respectable fourth (off current mark) in this race 12 months ago; thereabouts provided he improves on efforts elsewhere this term.
runners.comment: Has plenty of C&D form, including respectable fourth in this race last year

runners.name: Orne
runners.spotlight: Useful on his day for previous yard but is far from solid on form, mostly over 6f, for current stable; it remains to be seen whether this step back up in distance makes a difference.
runners.comment: Far from solid on his form, mostly over 6f, for current stable

runners.name: Stratocracy
runners.spotlight: On a career-high mark but he won well from the front at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in November when last seen and may remain competitive; dual scorer on turf and doesn't necessarily need to dominate.
runners.comment: Won well on AW when last seen; dual scorer on turf; may remain competitive
runners.owner: Total Asbestos Solutions Ltd & A Frost

runners.name: Obelix
runners.spotlight: Lacks both consistency and recent match practice (absent for 200 days) but holds a fighting chance provided he's in top form; has gained both handicap wins off 2lb higher, including over C&D (good) last July.
runners.comment: Has gained both handicap wins off 2lb higher, including over C&D last July
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho

runners.name: Spirit Genie
runners.spotlight: Ended winless 2025 campaign with a placed effort in an above-average seller; starts his 2026 season off 8lb below his last winning mark but chance partly depends on the weather as he's best on ground softer than good.
runners.comment: 8lb below last winning mark; best on ground softer than good

races.race_name: Sky Bet Fillies' Stakes (Registered As The Michael Seely Memorial Fillies' Stakes) (Listed Race)

runners.name: Act Of Kindness
runners.spotlight: Won at Newmarket (7f, good) last October on second start and kept on for third on reappearance in the Group 3 Nell Gwyn there (7f, good) last month; this half-sister to Group 1 Fillies' Mile winner Ylang Ylang is open to further improvement now tackling 1m and could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Kept on for third in the 7f Nell Gwyn and she's open to improvement now tackling 1m

runners.name: Pacific Mission
runners.spotlight: Won at Kempton (7f, AW) last August on second run and, despite slow starts, posted fine efforts when runner-up on next two outings, in the Group 2 May Hill at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) and the Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies Turf at Del Mar (1m, firm); leading form claims on return.
runners.comment: Runner-up at the Breeders' Cup last October and has leading form claims on her return

runners.name: Botagoz
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Kempton (7f, AW) last December and made it 2-2 with 2l win in Nottingham novice (8.3f, good) last month; this is a much stiffer test of her credentials but she's a half-sister to a Listed winner, and her dam won a Group 2, and it remains to be seen where her limitations lie.
runners.comment: 2-2; up in grade today but bred for the task; remains to be seen where her limitations lie

runners.name: So Regal
runners.spotlight: Promising third on sole 2yo start (7f, AW) and 2-2 this year following wins in a maiden at Newmarket (1m, good) and novice at Ascot (1m, good to firm); this well-bred filly is in excellent hands to continue to progress (one of two for the stable) and may well take this rise in grade in her stride.
runners.comment: 2-2 this year and this well-bred filly may well take today's rise in grade in her stride

runners.name: Fashion People
runners.spotlight: Odds-on for both starts and impressive each time, at Newcastle (7f, AW) last November and at Yarmouth (7f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; she could do with settling better now at 1m and others have more substance to their form, but she's in top hands (one of two for the yard) and it's hard to identify where the ceiling of her ability will sit; not ruled out.
runners.comment: 2-2; others have stronger form but not ruled out for top stable (one of two for yard)

runners.name: Lilt
runners.spotlight: 7-2, set a steady pace when making all on debut at Doncaster (7f, soft) last October; prevailed by just a neck and is bottom of this pack on RPRs but she's a half-sister to a 7f/1m Listed winner for connections, and her dam was a US 9.5f/1m2f Grade 1 winner; she could have lots more to offer.
runners.comment: Had just a neck to spare on sole 2yo start but well bred and could have lots more to offer

runners.name: Synchronicity
runners.spotlight: 900,000gns yearling who is a sister to 1m1f/1m2f Group 1 winner Ombudsman and she was sent off the 6-4 favourite when winning a newcomers' race at Newbury (7f, good) last month; she drew nicely clear in the closing stages and the runner-up, fourth and fifth have all won since; looks set to be suited by the step up to 1m and she's an exciting prospect.
runners.comment: Sister to Group 1 winner Ombudsman; won on debut last month and she's an exciting prospect

races.race_name: Support Macmillan Charity Raceday On 13 June Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Financer
runners.spotlight: Runner-up three times last season, including in a big field over C&D (good), and he could improve for last month's reappearance run at Ripon; however, this is a tough contest in which to try to snap a 13-race losing run.
runners.comment: Some good runs last season, including over C&D, but his losing sequence is up to 13
runners.owner: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor

runners.name: Thunder Run
runners.spotlight: Disappointing in the Cambridgeshire at Newmarket on final outing last season and same story in Listed race at Kempton on last month's reappearance; however, he's back down to the same mark as when winning this last year (good to firm) and can have a big say if he's back on song.
runners.comment: A return to form is needed but he races off the same mark as when winning this last year

runners.name: Cadarn
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row at Redcar (1m, good to firm) last spring for George Boughey and he's just 1lb higher than when last successful; effective at 1m2f earlier in career; however, he hasn't been at his best on his last four starts, for this yard on the last two occasions, and he has to raise his game.
runners.comment: Won two in a row last spring but hasn't been at his best on his four starts since

runners.name: Warrant Holder
runners.spotlight: Built upon his previous promise (including on turf) to win in good style in a small field at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) last October on his second handicap start; gelded since and this lightly raced 4yo could have lots more to offer this season for his top stable; wears a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Impressive when last seen and this lightly raced 4yo could have lots more to offer
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Regal Ulixes
runners.spotlight: Two novice wins on turf and he made it 3-6 when scoring at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in February on his second handicap start; solid fourth to The Glen Rovers at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) last month and, while improvement is necessary today, this is just his ninth run and he may have more left in the tank.
runners.comment: Solid fourth on AW last time; lightly raced and he may have more left in the tank

runners.name: Have Secret
runners.spotlight: 6yo who is on a career-high mark but he looked better than ever when winning by just over 2l at Pontefract (1m2f, good) last month; close third in this last year (good to firm) and could again be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Better than ever when winning at Pontefract latest; third in this last year; thereabouts

runners.name: Double Parked
runners.spotlight: Prominent-racer who won twice during last season's 3yo campaign (1m, good to soft/good to firm) and returned to form when third at Redcar (1m, good) last Thursday on his second start of the season; it's possible this new trip will be within range and he has an each-way shout if seeing it out.
runners.comment: Good third over 1m at Redcar last week; each-way shout if seeing out this new trip

runners.name: Castle Stuart
runners.spotlight: Close third of 15 on stable debut over C&D (good to firm) last August when 3lb higher; he hasn't kicked on subsequently but last month's reappearance sixth at Doncaster gives him something to build on; not discounted.
runners.comment: Has failed to kick on since his good run over C&D last August but he's not discounted

runners.name: Per Contra
runners.spotlight: Neck second of 15 over C&D (good to firm) last August and his seventh of 12 at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) last month can be upgraded as he was slowly away in a race which favoured those ridden more prominently; he's one to consider back up in trip.
runners.comment: Neck C&D 2nd last August and last month's Musselburgh run can be upgraded; one to consider

runners.name: Empire Of Light
runners.spotlight: Made all at Chester (1m2f, heavy) last September and did likewise to follow up on reappearance at Haydock (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago; he had just a head to spare last time and is up 4lb in a higher grade today, but this 5yo is clearly in fine heart and could make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Front-runner who has won his last two; up in grade but this 5yo is clearly in fine heart

runners.name: Ashnak
runners.spotlight: French 2yo winner in 2024 and remains lightly raced; ran well when third of six at Ripon (1m4f, good) last month on his second stable start, when shaping as though this drop back in trip would be a plus, and he could have more to offer for a yard that has excelled with similar types over the years.
runners.comment: Ex-French 4yo; shaped when third over 1m4f at Ripon as though this shorter trip would help

runners.name: Zryan
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins in the winter (9.4f/1m2f); it's possible he's gone off the boil but when beaten 8l in ninth of 13 at Newmarket a fortnight ago he was denied a clear passage before keeping on nicely; not ruled out each-way back up in trip.
runners.comment: It's possible he's gone off the boil but the step back up in trip could prove a positive

runners.name: The Glen Rovers
runners.spotlight: 7yo who is lightly raced for his age and made it 6-12 on AW when winning a competitive 1m2f handicap at Newcastle last month; achieved very little on turf at the start of his career but he's a different model now and he could be in the shake-up if the return to grass isn't an issue.
runners.comment: Won competitive AW h'cap last time; could be in shake-up if return to grass isn't an issue

runners.name: Tony Montana
runners.spotlight: Beaten on his last 12 starts going back to May 2024 but he's run some big races in the meantime, including when beaten a head in this last year (good to firm) off the same mark as today; reappeared with solid sixth of 24 on stable debut in the Spring Cup at Newbury (1m, good); can be in the mix.
runners.comment: Has the same mark as when a head runner-up in this last year and he can be in the mix

runners.name: Altareq
runners.spotlight: Improved upon his first two runs when going close for the Gosdens in Newbury maiden (1m2f, good) last May; gelded subsequently and sold for 95,000gns last October; this well-bred 4yo is a very interesting recruit for Jim Goldie but he makes his handicap/stable debut in a hot race following a year off, and it's hard to know what to expect.
runners.comment: Ex-Gosden 4yo; sold for 95,000gns; makes handicap/stable debut in hot race after absence

runners.name: Chillingham
runners.spotlight: Promoted to first in 1m4f handicap here (good to firm) last June and he races off 2lb lower today; however, he wasn't at his best last year on his final few runs for Edward Bethell, and failed to threaten over hurdles in the winter on his first two starts for this yard; may be best watched.
runners.comment: Course winner who is on a handy mark but he failed to shine over hurdles in the winter

races.race_name: Design Tonic Out Of This World Spaces At The Races EBF Marygate Fillies' Stakes (Listed) (IRE Inc')

runners.name: Lover Girl
runners.spotlight: Amo runner who is two from two, namely 5f wins in Wolverhampton maiden (AW) and Salisbury conditions race (good to firm); leading player on the figures and may continue to make hay while the iron's hot.
runners.comment: Amo runner who is two from two, latest win in Salisbury conditions race; leading claims

runners.name: Princesse d'Orange
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism with quite an impressive 4l success from the front in Beverley contest (5f, good to soft) and may well prove Listed class or even better; promising contender who commands respect.
runners.comment: Quite impressive at Beverley and may well prove Listed class or even better; respected

runners.name: Lady Blanche
runners.spotlight: Persian Force filly who made all for an authoritative success at Bath (5.7f, firm) to build on her Beverley debut (5f, good to soft); looks potentially useful and helps to give Amo Racing a strong hand.
runners.comment: Recorded an authoritative win at Bath last time and helps to give Amo Racing a good hand

runners.name: Angels Passing
runners.spotlight: 130,000euros yearling; No Nay Never half-sister to six winners, notably Duneflower (7f/1m including 2yo/Listed; RPR 107); dam Group-placed 7f 2yo scorer (101); sole debutante in the field; check the betting.
runners.comment: 130,000euros yearling; by No Nay Never; sole debutante in the field; check the betting

runners.name: Lake Muritz
runners.spotlight: Backed up 50-1 effort behind Niewiadoma to go one better at Beverley (5f, good to soft) where she beat four rivals; however, looks short of Listed standard and is unlikely to follow up.
runners.comment: Went one better at Beverley but looks unlikely to follow up in this grade

runners.name: Lauralynn
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly whose consistent but modest form comprises placed efforts at Beverley and a success at Southwell (5f, AW); has the worst chance on the figures upped in grade.
runners.comment: Has the worst chance judged on her consistent but modest form
runners.owner: Ger O'Neill & Unique Retreats Stays Ltd

runners.name: Love A Giggle
runners.spotlight: First foal of a 5f 2yo winner and duly showed precocity in beating three opponents at Southwell (5f, AW); goes into deeper waters today but is in excellent hands and could be set for further spring gains.
runners.comment: Southwell AW winner who is in excellent hands and could be set for further spring gains

runners.name: Margaret's Pearl
runners.spotlight: Prevailed narrowly at Pontefract (5f, good) to build on her Wolverhampton debut behind Lover Girl, who has since confirmed she's the better of the pair; likely to fall short at this level.
runners.comment: Prevailed narrowly at Pontefract last time; likely to fall short at this level

runners.name: Armor Supreme
runners.spotlight: Bargain purchase at 4,000euros having recouped that amount fourfold at Goodwood (5f, good) two weeks ago when she made all; however, this looks a much stiffer task.
runners.comment: 4,000euros purchase who made all at Goodwood but this looks a much stiffer task
runners.owner: Team Valor International, D Dias,M Eves

runners.name: Niewiadoma
runners.spotlight: Speedily bred filly who prevailed at Thirsk (5f, good) last month where she beat Lake Muritz and contributed to her stable's bright start with 2yos this season; the type to go well in this bigger prize.
runners.comment: Won at Thirsk, contributing to her stable's bright start with 2yos this season

races.race_name: Boodles Yorkshire Cup Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Rahiebb
runners.spotlight: Broadly progressive on a range of ground in his first season last year, despite being 0-6 after debut success; beaten a neck from off the pace in the St Leger at Doncaster (14.5f, soft) on final start; that makes him one of the form picks in today's field and, although this trip may eventually turn out to be shorter than ideal, he should be a big player.
runners.comment: Neck 2nd from off the pace in the St Leger (14.5f, soft) makes him one of the form picks

runners.name: Amiloc
runners.spotlight: Seven races and improved with each one; raised to Group 1 last autumn for his only defeats, when second in the 1m6f Irish St Leger and fourth in the 1m4f Breeders' Cup Turf; one of the top two on form and should have a big shout.
runners.comment: Improved with each race; 2nd in Irish St Leger and 4th in Breeders' Cup Turf; big shout

runners.name: Furthur
runners.spotlight: Best form winning the Group 3 Geoffrey Freer at Newbury (1m5f, good to firm) last August in good style from Epic Poet; creditable sixth in the St Leger at Doncaster (14.5f) and not disgraced when mid-division for the 2m Melbourne Cup in November when last seen; gelded since; could resume his progress and that would bring him back into serious contention.
runners.comment: Gelded; could resume his progress and that would bring him back into serious contention

runners.name: Tarriance
runners.spotlight: Won the C&D Melrose Handicap (good to firm) last August, before the St Leger proved too tough; gelded soon after and creditable third in Saudi Arabia (1m7f) in February, but he needs to bounce back with a big career best after his heavy defeat in 2m Ascot Group 3 (9-1) two weeks ago.
runners.comment: Won the C&D Melrose Handicap; heavy defeat in 2m Ascot Group 3 (9-1) two weeks ago

runners.name: Al Nayyir
runners.spotlight: While form since has not quite reached the heights of 2024, he's still been pretty reliable and got back on the scoresheet at Meydan (1m6f Group 3) in February; third in a 2m Group 2 there in March when last seen; wore cheekpieces on last four starts; should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Pretty reliable, including a Meydan win (1m6f Group 3) in February; should be thereabouts

runners.name: Epic Poet
runners.spotlight: Beaten a head in this last year, despite a highly tactical race; has held his form well (back in Dubai and Saudi Arabia this January-March) and should again be seriously involved, but to win this may require a career best.
runners.comment: Head 2nd last year; should be seriously involved but to win this may require a career best

runners.name: Deira Mile
runners.spotlight: Fourth in the 2024 Derby and St Leger; however, well held in Saudi Arabia and Dubai on his only two starts last year, left Owen Burrows for 16,000gns last October, gelded and never dangerous in 2m Ascot Group 3 (150-1) on return, so he seems to be swimming against the tide in this good race.
runners.comment: Fourth in the 2024 Derby and St Leger; very opposable judged on light career since

races.race_name: Irish Thoroughbred Marketing Handicap

runners.name: Old Is Gold
runners.spotlight: Displayed plenty of ability in his five-race 2yo campaign and well backed ahead of last month's reappearance at Sandown (5f, good), where he was a 3l sixth of nine having been slowly away and denied a clear passage; could have a part to play if things pan out more favourably today.
runners.comment: Well backed on comeback at Sandown where not much went right; could have a part to play

runners.name: Naana's Shadow
runners.spotlight: 5f AW novice winner for Alice Haynes last October; suffered interference on stable debut last month then comfortably made all in a handicap at Catterick (5f, good) subsequently; today's company is considerably hotter but she could still be well treated up 5lb; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Impressive at Catterick, albeit in a lower grade; could still be well treated up 5lb

runners.name: Von Trotter
runners.spotlight: Runner-up late last year on first two starts (6f/5f, AW) and filled the same position in a maiden at Southwell (5f, AW) two months ago; has improved with each run and further progress is possible but necessary in a warm contest on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Runner-up on all three starts; further improvement is needed on handicap debut

runners.name: Storm Esme
runners.spotlight: Well-backed winner on debut at Newcastle (6f, AW) last September and bounced back from Listed defeat at Redcar to make all in Leicester novice (6f, good); soundly beaten on last month's seasonal and handicap debut at Ripon (6f, good to soft) but may have needed the run and is not written off.
runners.comment: 2-3 last year; soundly beaten on reappearance last month but may have needed the run

runners.name: Schrodinger's Cat
runners.spotlight: Promising second of 14 on seasonal/stable debut at Thirsk (5f, good) last month and filled the same position at Chester (5f, good) last Friday, when inconvenienced by early interference before finishing off strongly; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Runner-up in handicaps on first two starts for this yard and he's firmly in calculations

runners.name: Believeinmenow
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but he's gone close on his last two starts, most recently a head second of 14 at Beverley (5f, good to soft); also effective on good ground; up in grade today but has each-way possibilities once more.
runners.comment: 0-8 but has gone close on his last two starts and holds each-way possibilities once more

runners.name: Desert Treasure
runners.spotlight: Promising win on debut at Yarmouth (5f, good) last July and followed up at 1-4 in Windsor novice (5f, good) ten days later; absent since and she could do with breaking better on this handicap debut but it remains to be seen where her limitations lie and she has potential off her opening mark.
runners.comment: 2-2 last year, both wins in July; absent since but has potential off her opening mark

runners.name: Chairmanfourtimes
runners.spotlight: Last season's standout performance was a 12-1 Listed win at Ayr (5f) in September on his sole encounter with soft ground; however, that performance has been an outlier and he's on a tough mark judged on the rest of his form; gelded since latest start last month.
runners.comment: Listed winner last September but he's on a tough mark judged on the rest of his form

runners.name: Hanney Girl
runners.spotlight: Comfortably made all on nursery debut at Ripon (5f, good to firm) last August and second off this 9lb higher mark at Chester (5f, good to soft) next time; however, hasn't kicked on subsequently and needs something extra.
runners.comment: On a competitive mark on best nursery form but has to take step forward from comeback run

runners.name: Eternal Solace
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year from first three starts (including on turf); some good efforts over 6f on AW this spring and William Buick has been booked by her in-form trainer, but she's looking exposed; others preferred.
runners.comment: Some good efforts in defeat on AW this year but she's looking exposed; others preferred

runners.name: Fortification
runners.spotlight: Pretty consistent across her six starts for Archie Watson (including on turf), winning a 5f AW novice last November; however, improvement is needed on stable debut, having changed hands for 26,000gns last month.
runners.comment: Consistent for Archie Watson but improvement needed on stable debut; sold for 26,000gns

runners.name: Shes Got A Brother
runners.spotlight: Comfortably won on debut at Southwell (5f, AW) last month and again made all in a novice there (5f) since; she's a likeable prospect and it will be no surprise if she proves to be on a good mark on handicap debut.
runners.comment: 2-2, both wins at Southwell last month; no surprise if she's on a good mark on h'cap debut

runners.name: Call Margot
runners.spotlight: She's improved with each of her three runs for this yard and did well to defy a tricky passage to win at Southwell (5f, AW) last month; based on that evidence a 6lb rise may well not stop her this afternoon, and the expected strong gallop will be a positive for this headstrong filly.
runners.comment: Did well to defy a tricky passage at Southwell and a 6lb rise may well not stop her

runners.name: Kinnalargy
runners.spotlight: Ran five times last year, winning a novice at Redcar (5f, good to firm) last June and showing good pace when fourth of 15 on nursery debut at Doncaster (6f, good) when last seen in September; gelded since; the drop back to 5f could be a positive on his reappearance and he's a possible player.
runners.comment: Showed good pace when fourth in 6f nursery at Doncaster when last seen; gelded since

runners.name: Arduis Invicta
runners.spotlight: It's possible 6f suits better but some of last autumn's good nursery/Listed form came at 5f and she was beaten only 2l from a wide draw when sixth of 12 at Chester (6f, good) last Wednesday; not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: Given her wide draw it was a positive performance at Chester recently; not ruled out e-w

runners.name: Entailed
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in both nurseries for Andrew Balding and sold for 27,000gns last October; however, she was an encouraging third of 14 at 50-1 on her stable and seasonal debut at Thirsk (5f, good) four weeks ago and could build on that run today; one of two fillies for the yard; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Encouraging third on stable debut at Thirsk and could build on that run today; interesting

runners.name: Sir Alfie
runners.spotlight: Two wins last summer for Ger Lyons; however, he's been a long way below his best on his last four starts, the first two for Charles Byrnes and the latest two for this yard; transformation needed in first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Two wins last summer for Ger Lyons but he's struggled more recently; a tongue-tie goes on

runners.name: Stargazed
runners.spotlight: Close fourth at Beverley (5f, good to firm) last July on second start and comfortable win at Ripon (5f, good to soft) later that month; fifth in Group 3 at Longchamp (5f, very soft) in August and off since; her attractive pedigree provides optimism that she will have more to offer for her top yard.
runners.comment: Ripon maiden win last July; attractive pedigree provides optimism she has more to offer

runners.name: Nuevo Slovo
runners.spotlight: Won at Ayr (5f, good) last April on second start before creditable sixth in the Windsor Castle at Royal Ascot (5f, good to firm); just one run since, when mid-division in Listed race at Fairyhouse (6f, yielding) in September, and sold for 34,000gns since; may be best watched on stable debut.
runners.comment: Promise in light 2yo campaign but he may be best watched on this seasonal/stable debut

races.race_name: Darley EBF "Confined" Novice Stakes (For Horses With No More Than Two Runs) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Weheedd
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns yearling who was well backed into 100-30 when runner-up on last month's debut at Yarmouth (1m, good); that form reads very well in view of the subsequent handicap exploits of the first and third and he looks to have a very bright future; one to consider.
runners.comment: Well backed on last month's debut at Yarmouth and that form reads very well

runners.name: Portcullis
runners.spotlight: Belied a market drift, slow start and evident inexperience to win by just over 5l at 7-1 on debut in the Wood Ditton at Newmarket (1m, good) four weeks ago; leading claims under a 6lb penalty for top stable that has won the last three runnings of this race.
runners.comment: Impressive in the Wood Ditton on debut and has leading claims under a 6lb penalty
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Always Blue
runners.spotlight: Sister to a Swedish winner; 12-1 when 1l second of five on debut at Ripon (1m2f, good) three weeks ago; could settle better today in a first-time hood and she's open to improvement, but this tough task may be beyond her.
runners.comment: Promising second on debut at Ripon but today's tough task may be beyond her

runners.name: Ervani
runners.spotlight: 4-1 when runner-up to the experienced, 1-4 favourite on recent debut at Wetherby (1m, good to firm); his attractive pedigree suggests that he will progress and he's in good hands to do just that.
runners.comment: Well-bred colt who was second at Wetherby on debut and is in good hands to progress

runners.name: Pearl
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; sister to French 1,000 Guineas/French Oaks winner Avenir Certain, half-sister to two winners; dam Group-placed 7f 2yo winner; with a top trainer but this 4yo is in a tough race against males on her belated debut.
runners.comment: Sister to French Guineas/Oaks winner; in a tough race against males on belated debut

runners.name: Celtic Chariot
runners.spotlight: Displayed ability when third of five at 33-1 on debut at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) three weeks ago; open to improvement but a sizeable step forward is needed if he's to threaten in this hot contest.
runners.comment: Displayed ability on debut at Doncaster three weeks ago but needs a sizeable step forward

runners.name: Lord d'Or
runners.spotlight: His dam was a German 1m Group 3 winner and he could be much sharper today than at Kempton (7f, AW) last August on sole 2yo start, when slowly away and soundly beaten; however, others have far more obvious claims.
runners.comment: Possible he'll leave his sole 2yo run well behind but others have far more obvious claims
runners.owner: RC Syndicate X and Gary Gillies

runners.name: Test Run
runners.spotlight: Late headway when fourth of ten, beaten just over 7l, at 80-1 on debut in a warm maiden at Doncaster (1m, soft) last September; others have much stronger form but there's plenty of potential in his pedigree and he could be capable of considerably better; not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: His sole 2yo run wasn't devoid of promise and there's plenty of potential in his pedigree

races.race_name: Starlight Friends: Don't Bet The Ranch Charity Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Gone Rogue
runners.spotlight: Regained the winning thread at Brighton (1m, good to soft) last October and has remained competitive in two outings since, close second at Windsor (1m2f) on reappearance latest; due to go up 1lb in future; solid.
runners.comment: Has remained competitive since autumn win; close second at Windsor on reappearance

runners.name: Pay Attention
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-16 but he's attractively treated, having gained the success (1m, good) off 2lb higher; tally could be doubled if he takes well to first-time visor with Bath reappearance under his belt.
runners.comment: Gained sole win off 2lb higher; tally could be doubled if he takes well to visor

runners.name: Jet Packer
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but he has performed encouragingly in two runs, albeit both on Lingfield AW in December, since having wind surgery; returns to turf for this reappearance.
runners.comment: Maiden; needs to show he can transfer December AW form back to turf

runners.name: Pearly Squirrel
runners.spotlight: AW scorer for current stable but form dipped sharply returned to turf last time, despite being previously effective in this sphere; similar type to Dark Rosa.
runners.comment: Form dipped back on turf last time; similar type to Dark Rosa

runners.name: Time After Time
runners.spotlight: Rather disappointing in hat-trick attempt when last seen, but this low-mileage 4yo may still have more to offer and can't be written off; gained her two wins over 1m on good ground.
runners.comment: Rather disappointing when last seen but this low-mileage 4yo may do better still

runners.name: Virtue Patience
runners.spotlight: Has a stronger record on AW than on turf but produced best effort in this sphere when third off 7lb higher over C&D last June; good chance if that form is repeated.
runners.comment: Stronger record on AW than on turf but good chance on C&D effort last June

runners.name: Hello Cotai
runners.spotlight: Three wins (8.5f/9.5f) for Adam West; ran encouragingly (close third) in 1m2f AW contest at Lingfield on stable/seasonal debut and has possibilities provided he builds on that effort.
runners.comment: Ran encouragingly on stable/seasonal debut; possibilities if building on that effort

runners.name: Dark Rosa
runners.spotlight: AW winner for current stable but her form has dipped in two starts since returned to turf, despite being previously effective in this sphere; bit to prove at present.
runners.comment: AW winner for current stable; form has dipped in two starts since returned to turf

runners.name: Miss Chester
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent filly who has raced mainly on AW; posted one of her better efforts when close third in Class 6 at Kempton (1m) last week but isn't guaranteed to back up the form.
runners.comment: Posted one of her better AW efforts last week but isn't sure to back up the form

runners.name: Tilani
runners.spotlight: Has failed to shine in two runs this season for new yard but is interesting assuming a turnaround occurs; well treated on peak Irish form, namely a win off 2lb higher at the 2025 Galway festival (8.3f, soft).
runners.comment: Well treated on his win at the 2025 Galway festival; third start for new stable

runners.name: Toralou
runners.spotlight: Sole effort this year took her AW record to 0-6; has form figures of 26221 on turf, the success in Musselburgh maiden (1m, good); return to this sphere is the angle with her.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 26221 on turf; return to this sphere is the angle with her

runners.name: Riyadh Gem
runners.spotlight: Not solid on his AW form for current yard and he has regressive results on turf, so chance hinges on physical tweak (gelded since last run) and addition of tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Chance hinges on physical tweak (gelded) and addition of tongue-tie

runners.name: Spirit Lead Me
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner but holds dismal claims on his 2026 form in that sphere and his unconvincing record on turf adds doubt.
runners.comment: Holds dismal claims on 2026 AW form and his turf record adds doubt

races.race_name: Titan Wealth Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Al Durry
runners.spotlight: Has raced on only the AW, with her wins at Southwell (1m) in October and on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (9.5f) in March both coming after five-month layoffs; it's unclear how she will cope with turf but she is undoubtedly progressive.
runners.comment: Has raced on only AW; unclear how she will cope with turf, but undoubtedly progressive

runners.name: Clouds Hill
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed the run of the race when making all to beat four rivals on his Yarmouth return (1m2f, good) 15 days ago; has a 5lb penalty to carry but remains unexposed after just five starts, so makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: 5lb higher than for his successful Yarmouth reappearance but still unexposed; shortlisted
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Alcarath
runners.spotlight: Bolted up from the front in an Epsom novice (8.5f, good) last September but twice unplaced in handicaps, including on his reappearance at Newmarket (1m1f, good to firm) 13 days ago; should be better for that run, but more is needed.
runners.comment: Should be better for his recent Newmarket reappearance but more is needed

runners.name: High Degree
runners.spotlight: Narrowly landed skinny odds in a Ffos Las maiden (1m, good) last July and although out of the first three in three big-field handicaps (1m/1m2f) subsequently, he was far from disgraced; gelded since last seen seven months ago but looks the type with more to offer this year.
runners.comment: Gelded since last seen seven months ago but looks the type with more to offer this year

runners.name: Something Splendid
runners.spotlight: 2-13, his latest success coming off this mark at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) last September after the first past the post was disqualified for a banned substance; off seven months but has gone well fresh before; each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Off seven months but has gone well fresh before; each-way possibilities

runners.name: Celeborn
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who was returning from seven months off when winning at Epsom (8.5f, good) in April last year; off again since and 9lb higher, but he is clearly proven fresh and his sire should ensure that this longer trip is within range; respected.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo; off for over a year but is proven fresh and should stay this far; respected
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Hand Of God
runners.spotlight: Talented 5yo, but has endured some lengthy absences in his career and was returning from another 280 days off when seventh of 24 in the Spring Cup here (1m, good) last month; return to this trip is not a problem and he's interesting down in grade.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo; return to this trip is not a problem and he's interesting down in grade

runners.name: Oursin
runners.spotlight: Has raced only three times, all on the AW and winning two 1m novices in the autumn; makes his handicap/turf debut after 200 days off, but the longer trip should suit on breeding.
runners.comment: AW record reads 211; handicap/turf debut after 200 days off; the longer trip should suit

runners.name: Al Aali
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in France (9.5f, AW) as a 2yo; didn't show enough on his stable debut at Goodwood (1m2f, good; first start since being gelded) 13 days ago to make him of immediate interest.
runners.comment: Didn't show enough on his stable debut at Goodwood 13 days ago to make him of interest

runners.name: Mustazeed
runners.spotlight: 4-9 over C&D including landing this race three years ago, but his starts have increasingly become a problem, flatly refusing to do so here in October; too risky.
runners.comment: 4-9 over C&D including this race in 2023, but his starts have become a big problem

runners.name: Parole d'Oro
runners.spotlight: Sole success came at Epsom (7f, good to firm) as a 2yo, though he twice finished runner-up last year including at York (1m2f, good; first start since being gelded) in October; off since but he has gone well fresh in the past; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Off since finishing second at York in October but has gone well fresh in the past

races.race_name: ARC Proud To Support Starlight Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: State Of War
runners.spotlight: March foal by Too Darn Hot; 35,000gns foal; 82,000gns yearling; dam well held at 7f/1m 2yo (RPR 42), half-sister to winners Walk Of Stars (9.5f UAE dirt Group 1) and Moon Shimmer (French 1m5f), out of Listed-placed 7f-1m4f winner; something to like on breeding and it will be interesting to see how he fares in the market compared to stablemate Pure Grit.
runners.comment: 82,000gns yearling; something to like on breeding; one of two from the yard

runners.name: Pure Grit
runners.spotlight: February foal by Pinatubo; 90,000gns foal, 230,000euros yearling; half-brother to winners American Sonja (6f-1m2f including 2yo/Group 3; RPR 113), Love You Darling (1m2f; 79) and Kodi Dream (7f-10.5f including AW; 70); dam unplaced at 5.7f-8.6f (58), half-sister to useful 1m-2m winner Island Brave; his price tag and breeding make him worth a second look; stable, which took this last year, also runs State Of War.
runners.comment: His price tag and breeding make him worth a second look; one of two from the yard

runners.name: Down To You Kid
runners.spotlight: April foal by Starman; 48,000euros foal; £82,000 yearling; dam 7f winner (RPR 90), half-sister to winners Arctic Sound (7f 2yo Group 3) and Deep Snow (1m/1m2f), out of 6f 2yo Listed-winning half-sister to 5.5f 2yo Group 2 winner Ocean Ridge; enough on pedigree to make him of interest and his stable has taken three of the last seven runnings, two of them with newcomers.
runners.comment: Enough on pedigree to make him of interest; stable won three of the last seven runnings

runners.name: Asgar
runners.spotlight: January foal by Supremacy; 65,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Sacred Angel (6f 2yo including Group 3; RPR 102) and Anotherfortheroad (French 1m2f AW); dam 5f (Listed) and 6f 2yo winner (93) out of unraced sister to 6f Group 3 winner Tiger Royal; early foal whose stable often gets them ready first time and he makes plenty of appeal on pedigree.
runners.comment: Early foal; stable often gets them ready first time; makes plenty of appeal on pedigree

runners.name: God Given Talent
runners.spotlight: February foal by No Nay Never; 95,000gns yearling; closely related to winners Vincent Rocks (1m including AW; RPR 89) and Must Be Ten (6f AW 2yo; 65); dam placed at 1m2f (65), half-sister to winners Blooming Stars (9.5f) and Nijoom Star (1m 2yo), out of 6f 2yo Group 3 winner; others make more appeal on breeding and stable's 2yos are usually better for a run.
runners.comment: Others make more appeal on breeding and stable's 2yos are usually better for a run

runners.name: Kamaal
runners.spotlight: Apiril foal by Minzaal; 100,000gns yearling, 90,000gns breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to winners North Coast (7f-1m including Group 3; RPR 108) and Super Goldendragon (Hong Kong 1m3f); dam US 7.5f-1m1f turf winner, half-sister to useful winners Cook Islands (1m 2yo) and Silky (1m2f); appealing pedigree and stable can get a 2yo ready first time.
runners.comment: Appealing pedigree and stable can get a 2yo ready first time

runners.name: Menhaal
runners.spotlight: April foal by Minzaal; 260,000gns yearling; half-brother to six winners including Queen Of Mougins (Group-placed 6f including 2yo; RPR 108), Pretty Baby (6f/7f including 2yo/AW/Group 3; 105) and Tamaska (6f/7f; 88); dam unraced half-sister to Group 2 winners at 1m3f and 1m4f; his pedigree and the stable's fine record with 2yo newcomers put him high on the list.
runners.comment: His pedigree and the stable's fine record with 2yo newcomers put him high on the list

runners.name: Oceans Six
runners.spotlight: March foal by Cotai Glory; £55,000 yearling; half-brother to 7f/1m winner Assaranca (including 2yo; RPR 70); dam unraced half-sister to useful 11.5f/1m4f Flat and 2m5f hurdle winner Hamsiyann, out of 7f 2yo Group 3 winning half-sister to Derby winner Harzand; stable's newcomers often better for a run and he may be one for later on.
runners.comment: Stable's newcomers often better for a run and he may be one for later on

runners.name: Sakowin
runners.spotlight: April foal by Sioux Nation; 30,000euros foal; 70,000euros yearling; half-brother to three winners including Ventura Ocean (GB/Australia 6f/7f including 2yo; RPR 99) and Spirit Of Eagles (5f; 72); dam unraced half-sister to Italian Listed-placed 7f-8.5f winner Saint Steven; stable has a good record with 2yo newcomers, so he's one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Stable has a good record with 2yo newcomers, so he's one for the shortlist

runners.name: Wilbur
runners.spotlight: March foal by Kingman; 125,000euros foal; 160,000euros yearling; first foal; dam 1m 2yo winner (RPR 93), half-sister to winners Biographer (1m6f Listed) and Petitioner (1m4f), out of unraced close relative of top 2yo/miler Hector Protector and 1m-1m2f Group 1 winner Bosra Sham; stable doesn't have many 2yos go in first time and he may be one for further in due course.
runners.comment: Stable doesn't have many 2yos go in first time and he may be one for further in due course

races.race_name: Trade Nation Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Extremely Zain
runners.spotlight: 185,000euros breeze-up 2yo; sent off 5-2 when coming home 7l clear on his Newcastle debut (6f, AW) in December; the form hasn't really worked out and he has a 7lb penalty, but this St James's Palace Stakes entry shouldn't be underestimated.
runners.comment: Bolted up on his Newcastle debut in December; holds a St James's Palace Stakes entry

runners.name: Erudition
runners.spotlight: 8-1 when a promising third of 12 on his Leicester debut (7f, good) last month; the runner-up won easily back there last Saturday; high on list.
runners.comment: Promising third of 12 on his Leicester debut last month; form franked; high on list

runners.name: Oceti
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in both starts, but not disgraced when eighth of 25 on his reappearance here (1m, good) last month (form working out well); may still be one for handicaps after this, though.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on his reappearance here last month, but may still be one for handicaps

runners.name: Petworth
runners.spotlight: 260,000euros foal; 400,000euros yearling; first foal; dam German 1m-1m3f winner (including German Oaks; RPR 110), sister to French 1m 2yo Listed winner Penny Lane, half-sister to winners Peace Royale (1m Group 3), Peaceful Love (1m Group 3) and Phiz (Group-placed 1m5f); makes plenty of appeal on pedigree and interesting to see what the market says about him; stable also runs Stormy Music.
runners.comment: Makes plenty of appeal on pedigree and interesting to see what the market says about him

runners.name: Odogwu
runners.spotlight: 110,000gns foal; 125,000gns yearling; half-brother to 7f AW 2yo winner Char (RPR 87); dam placed 1m AW in France only run (74), half-sister to winners Rahiyah (6f 2yo/French 1,000 Guineas third) and League Champion (useful 5f/6f); stable, also responsible for Race To The Edge, gets plenty of 3yo winners first time out; market useful.
runners.comment: Stable gets plenty of 3yo winners first time out; market useful

runners.name: Race To The Edge
runners.spotlight: 25,000gns foal; 135,000euros yearling; half-brother to winners Texas Starlight (7f AW 2yo; RPR 87), Al Alaali (French 1m1f) and First Smiling (German 1m3f); dam French 10.5f winner, half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Barsanti; already gelded; market informative on debut.
runners.comment: Already gelded; market informative on debut

runners.name: Sponsor
runners.spotlight: Both starts over 1m, stepping up from last month's debut here (good) when beaten 2l into fourth of 11 at Windsor (good to firm) 11 days ago; each-way claims if taking another step forward.
runners.comment: Improved from his debut when fourth at Windsor 11 days ago; each-way claims
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing, Miller And Day

runners.name: Guitar Solo
runners.spotlight: 16-1 when fifth of seven on her Salisbury debut (7f, good to firm) 12 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Fifth of seven on her Salisbury debut 12 days ago; needs to take a big step forward
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Binmalk
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam Listed-placed 7f 2yo/1m AW winner (RPR 106), sister to winners Benbatl (1m1f/1m2f Group 1) and Modaara (useful 1m2f/1m4f), half-sister to 1,000 Guineas winner Elmalka (by Kingman), out of 1m2f Group 1/Grade 1 winner; makes plenty of appeal on breeding and stable has a fine record with 3yo newcomers.
runners.comment: Makes plenty of appeal on breeding and stable has a fine record with 3yo newcomers

runners.name: Big Snoopy
runners.spotlight: 300,000gns yearling; 12-1 when a tailed-off last of eight on his Newmarket debut (7f, good) last month; bred to be much better than that, but needs the tongue-tie to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Tailed off on his Newmarket debut last month; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Stormy Music
runners.spotlight: Sent off 11-2 when sixth of 11 on her debut here (6f, good) last August; plenty of winners have come out of that race; worth a second look.
runners.comment: Sixth of 11 on her debut here last August; plenty of winners have come out of that race

runners.name: Anton Mauve
runners.spotlight: 250,000gns yearling, but well held in two 7f AW novices last month; needs a good deal more.
runners.comment: Well held in two AW novices last month; needs a good deal more

runners.name: English Time
runners.spotlight: 260,000gns yearling; some promise when finishing in midfield on his Newmarket debut (7f, good) in October; bred to be better than that, but he may need more time.
runners.comment: Midfield on his Newmarket debut in October; may need more time

runners.name: Debrief
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns foal; finished a well-beaten fourth of six on his Yarmouth debut (6f, good; 100-30) in October; major improvement required.
runners.comment: Well-beaten fourth of six on his Yarmouth debut in October; major improvement required

runners.name: Red Moon
runners.spotlight: 28-1 when a well-beaten sixth of eight on his Kempton debut (7f, AW) in December; a couple of those ahead of him won next time, but much more is still needed.
runners.comment: Well-beaten sixth of eight on his Kempton debut in December; much more is needed

races.race_name: Coolmore City Of Troy EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Hatteen
runners.spotlight: Has improved with each start, finishing second of nine on his Nottingham reappearance (1m2f, good) last month (third has since won easily); big player.
runners.comment: Second of nine on his Nottingham reappearance last month; form franked; big player

runners.name: Point Of Law
runners.spotlight: Sent off 11-2 when second of five on his Yarmouth debut (1m2f, good) last month, running a 101-rated rival to half a length with the third (who has since won a Class 3 handicap) over 3l behind; major player.
runners.comment: Ran 101-rated rival to half a length on Yarmouth debut; third has won since; major player
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Glory Road
runners.spotlight: 110,000euros yearling; green when second of six on his Nottingham debut (1m, good to soft; 7-2) in October (form worked out well); should appreciate this longer trip and high on the list.
runners.comment: Second of six on his Nottingham debut in October; should appreciate the longer trip

runners.name: Phantom Recon
runners.spotlight: 340,000euros yearling; closely related to Group-placed French/German 10.5f-12.2f winner Tres Rock Women (including AW; RPR 110), half-brother to French 1m4f winner Tiger Rock; dam 1m4f winner (including Listed; 98), sister to Listed-placed 1m2f/1m3f winner; nicely bred, but already gelded and may need a bit more time.
runners.comment: Nicely bred, but already gelded and may need a bit more time

runners.name: Siwa Oasis
runners.spotlight: Second of eight on his Kempton debut (7f, AW) in October and not disgraced when seventh of 25 on his reappearance here (1m, good) last month; this trip should be within range, but he may be one for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Has shown ability in both starts, but he may be one for handicaps after this

runners.name: Steamroller
runners.spotlight: 200,000euros yearling; half-brother to winners Queen Of The Pride (1m2f-1m4f including Group 2 Lancashire Oaks; RPR 115) and Delta (1m4f; 91); dam 1m4f-14.5f winner (including St Leger; 118), half-sister to 1m4f Group 2 (Ribblesdale) winner Even Song; attractive pedigree, but already gelded and this looks a warm race in which to make his debut.
runners.comment: Attractive pedigree, but already gelded; this looks a warm race in which to make his debut

runners.name: Zynak
runners.spotlight: 100,000euros yearling; half-brother to five winners including Zyzzyva (6f-7.5f including 2yo/AW/France/Qatar; RPR 107) and Zarisk (French 2m1f-2m5f hurdle/chase); dam unraced half-sister to smart South African/UAE 7f-1m2f winner Zanzamar; enough to like on breeding, but already gelded and would need to be above-average to make a winning debut in this field.
runners.comment: Enough to like on breeding, but already gelded and would need to be above-average

runners.name: Blue Hercules
runners.spotlight: 33-1 when beaten a long way into last of eight on his debut here (1m3f, good) last month; a transformation is required.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way into last of eight on his debut here last month; transformation required

runners.name: Much
runners.spotlight: 40-1 when a well-beaten eighth of ten behind Derby contender Maltese Cross on his debut over C&D (good; tongue tied) four weeks ago; looks one for middle-distance handicaps in due course.
runners.comment: Eighth of ten on debut over C&D four weeks ago; looks one for middle-distance handicaps

runners.name: El Loco
runners.spotlight: Both runs at Doncaster (1m, soft) last autumn, faring slightly better when fourth of five on the second occasion; gelded since; not crying out for the longer trip on the dam's side of his pedigree.
runners.comment: Unplaced in two starts last autumn; not crying out for the longer trip on pedigree

races.race_name: Greenshields JCB EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Heyzoom
runners.spotlight: Started favourite when placed in both starts, the latest when second of six at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) last month; finished over 4l clear of the third there and very likely to play a big part.
runners.comment: Finished clear of the third when second at Leicester last month; likely to play a big part

runners.name: Tambora
runners.spotlight: 120,000gns foal; out of the frame here in his two starts, but he showed ability on both occasions as when sixth of 25 over 1m last month (second and third have won since); stamina not assured on breeding, but still interesting given the stable won this with a twice-raced colt last year.
runners.comment: Out of the frame here in his two starts, but showed ability on both occasions; interesting

runners.name: Echo Of Stars
runners.spotlight: 100,000euros yearling; showed ability in both starts over 1m last autumn, the latest when second of seven at Newmarket (good); should stay at least this far and considered if ready to roll.
runners.comment: Showed ability in both starts last autumn; should stay and considered if ready to roll

runners.name: Besieged
runners.spotlight: 8-1 when beaten a length into second of nine on his Kempton debut (1m, AW) last month; the longer trip shouldn't be an issue and he's a player.
runners.comment: Second of nine on his Kempton debut last month; longer trip shouldn't be an issue; player

runners.name: Brocklesby Bill
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Slovakian 8.7f/1m2f winner Prufry; dam placed 6f/7f 2yo (RPR 66), half-sister to Group-placed 5f 2yo winner Ahlan Emarati, out of Listed-placed 6f/7f winner; already gelded and a hood is given a go; withdrawn after running loose on his intended debut at Salisbury in October (25-1 at the time); best watched.
runners.comment: Already gelded and a hood is given a go; best watched

runners.name: North Beach
runners.spotlight: 390,000euros yearling; 4-1 second-favourite when seventh of ten in the Wood Ditton at Newmarket (1m, good) last month; should stay this far, but improvement will be needed.
runners.comment: Seventh of ten in the Wood Ditton at Newmarket last month; improvement will be needed

runners.name: Knight Of Storms
runners.spotlight: 25,000euros foal; 110,000gns yearling; half-brother to 1m1f 2yo winner Hippodrome (RPR 91); dam 6f 2yo winner (runner-up in 1,000 Guineas and Irish 1,000 Guineas; 111), half-sister to 2m Group 3 winner Sweet Selection and smart 6f/7f winner Gulliver; something to like on pedigree, but he may need a bit more time.
runners.comment: Something to like on pedigree, but may need a bit more time

runners.name: Round The Table
runners.spotlight: 48,000gns foal, 50,000gns yearling; half-brother to 1m AW winner Celestias Comet (RPR 75); dam unraced sister to winners Tobann (7f Group 3), Team Decision (useful 1m-1m2f) and Theophile (7f/1m), half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed winner Vertiginous; probably best watched on debut unless popular in the market.
runners.comment: Probably best watched on debut unless popular in the market

runners.name: Tropbien
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 7f/1m winner (including Group 2; RPR 111), half-sister to winners Twisting Physics (7f/1m), Marquis Pompeo (7f/1m) and Toussarok (6f/7f), out of 6f/7f winner; plenty to like on pedigree and respected if the market speaks in her favour on debut.
runners.comment: Plenty to like on pedigree and respected if the market speaks in her favour on debut

runners.name: Salgados
runners.spotlight: 300,000gns yearling; sent off 15-2 when fourth of ten on his Southwell debut (1m, AW) in March (held a Derby entry at the time); that was something to build on and the trip shouldn't be an issue, but this will require more.
runners.comment: Fourth of ten on his Southwell debut in March; this will require more

runners.name: Electric Dreams
runners.spotlight: 475,000gns yearling; 3-1 when fourth of nine on his Kempton debut (1m, AW) last month; likely to improve, especially now up in trip, but he has 4l to find with Besieged so he will need to.
runners.comment: Has 4l to find with Besieged on last month's Kempton running; will need to improve

races.race_name: Coolmore City Of Troy Handicap

runners.name: Kalokalo
runners.spotlight: Form figures of 2142 in handicaps, competitive on turf and AW; should be much more exposed than plenty of these after seven races, but there's enough in his pedigree and performance to give encouragement for this longer trip.
runners.comment: Form figures of 2142 in handicaps (turf/AW); encouragement for this longer trip

runners.name: Madjid
runners.spotlight: Four races and marked improvement every time, making his handicap debut when second of nine at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm; 7-1) three weeks ago; still looked a bit green and further progress should make him interesting despite 2lb rise.
runners.comment: Four races and marked improvement every time, handicap debut when 2nd at Doncaster (1m2f)
runners.owner: Dr Jamal Ahmadzadeh & Mrs D Swinburn

runners.name: Kakirra
runners.spotlight: Hood off when upped markedly in trip for handicap debut at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW; 16-1) seven days ago, showing his first worthwhile form to win in good style, coming from the back; defied a penalty on turf debut at Bath (1m2f, firm) on Wednesday, making all at 6-5, but he was pressed all the way and a double penalty in this bigger field may present a tricky task.
runners.comment: Won well last week (1m4f, AW) and made all at Bath (1m2f, firm) Wednesday; double penalty

runners.name: Armstrong
runners.spotlight: Second over 1m at Kempton (strong finish), Bath (hung left) and Yarmouth (rallied) in all three of his handicaps; 1m2f now looks worth a go.
runners.comment: Second over 1m in all three of his handicaps; 1m2f now looks worth a go

runners.name: Berkshire Schmokin
runners.spotlight: Won at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in February in his second handicap; backward steps in his three starts since, admittedly clipping heels early on latest (Madjid's race at Doncaster; 11-2) but doubts are creeping in.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in February but three backward steps since
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Extraterrestrial
runners.spotlight: Third in his first two handicaps, including at Nottingham (1m2f, good; visored) in his only turf race; better form when beaten a neck at Southwell (1m3f, AW; cheekpieces) latest outing, which he'll need to build on.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck at Southwell (1m3f, AW; cheekpieces) last time, which he'll need to build on

runners.name: Grand Pier
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability as 2yo, without being that competitive, but tailed off on reappearance in a 1m handicap at Bath (good to soft; 12-1) four weeks ago; dam French 1m6f winner.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a 1m handicap at Bath (good to soft) four weeks ago; dam French 1m6f winner

runners.name: Tough Date
runners.spotlight: All four starts were over 1m; third in a novice at Southwell (AW) and second of six in maiden at Bath (good to soft); never dangerous when 13-2 for handicap debut at Windsor (good to firm) but this new trip could help.
runners.comment: Never dangerous when 13-2 for handicap debut (1m, good to firm) but this trip could help

runners.name: Flash Kozo
runners.spotlight: Finished last in his only turf race (40-1); getting closer on AW, finishing on the heels of Extraterrestrial when front-running third of seven on handicap debut at Southwell (1m3f) 16 days ago; needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Getting closer on AW, on heels of Extraterrestrial on handicap debut at Southwell (1m3f)
runners.owner: D Bevan And Mrs D Bevan

runners.name: Glenna
runners.spotlight: 12-1, creditable fourth of nine on handicap/seasonal debut at Nottingham (1m2f, good) five weeks ago; dropped just 1lb, so needs marked improvement but these are early days.
runners.comment: 12-1, creditable fourth of nine on handicap/seasonal debut at Nottingham (1m2f, good)

runners.name: Three Socks On
runners.spotlight: A different proposition since handicapping, with wins at Chelmsford (1m; dead-heated) in January and Wolverhampton (9.4f) five weeks ago; asserted in good style in the final 1f last time, which has earned him an 8lb rise but this longer trip looks a plus if he proves effective on turf debut.
runners.comment: 2-2 in AW handicaps; up 8lb since asserting in good style in final 1f over 9.4f last time

runners.name: Dijon Gone
runners.spotlight: Behind over 1m at Bath, Kempton and Newbury; lacks solid claims for this handicap debut but her family gives hope for middle distances.
runners.comment: Lacks solid claims for this handicap debut but her family gives hope for middle distances

runners.name: Brighton View
runners.spotlight: Fifth of nine on debut at Haydock (good to soft) in September has been followed by two lesser shows on AW, all at 1m; has a run under his belt this season and makes handicap debut over new trip.
runners.comment: Best show on debut; has a run under his belt in 2026 and makes handicap debut at new trip

runners.name: Virtue Chastity
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two AW maidens over 1m at Kempton and one over 1m2f at Lingfield; absent three months and lacks solid claims but going handicapping offers her more of a chance to show her merit.
runners.comment: Three months off and lacks solid claims but going handicapping offers her more of a chance

runners.name: Rossa Raheen
runners.spotlight: Eye-catcher from the rear at Ascot (1m, soft) in the first of two outings as 2yo for David Menuisier; 80-1 for new yard (same owner) on reappearance in maiden at Newbury (1m, good) four weeks ago and raced far too freely held up in rear, before some headway into mid-division; an interesting handicap newcomer but with plenty to learn, including if she's to stay 1m2f.
runners.comment: An interesting handicap newcomer but with plenty to learn, including settling for 1m2f

runners.name: La Peregrina
runners.spotlight: Well held at 33-1, 18-1 and 80-1 in his three novice races (1m1f, 1m3f and 1m; last two on AW) but he was a 150,000gns yearling and is well related; needs a market check on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well held; 150,000gns yearling, well related; needs a market check on this handicap debut

races.race_name: Coolmore Auguste Rodin Handicap

runners.name: One Million Dreams
runners.spotlight: Won two handicaps (1m5f/1m4f) last summer for Nick Littmoden and back with him when partially revived in third of four at Yarmouth last month; sold for 6,000gns eight days ago and a case can be made for him.
runners.comment: Partially revived last month; sold for 6,000gns eight days ago and a case can be made

runners.name: Educator
runners.spotlight: Won twice over about 1m4f (AW/good to firm) last spring and he's thrown in judged on that form; twice close up on AW in January and a respectable third at Bath (2m) in April, before Ascot 2m race last week proved too tough; there's a rethink on trip today and cheekpieces (last used in June 2024) return.
runners.comment: Down the weights; a rethink on trip today and cheekpieces (last used in June 2024) return

runners.name: Autumn's Breeze
runners.spotlight: Quickfire double at Lingfield (2m/1m5f; good) last August when the now-regular cheekpieces first went on; she's fired only once (last September) from her four subsequent starts, however.
runners.comment: Quickfire double at Lingfield (2m/1m5f; good) last August; fired once in four starts since

runners.name: Yokohama
runners.spotlight: Has slipped to a career-low mark and well-backed fifth of 15 when returned to turf at Doncaster (1m2f) in March last time wasn't too bad; however, his record over that sort of trip is far more appealing than his two runs at 1m4f and his turf tally is 0-11.
runners.comment: 1m2f record is far more appealing than his two runs at 1m4f, and his turf tally is 0-11

runners.name: Kimeko Glory
runners.spotlight: Bagged three 1m2f turf handicaps last April-July for other yards and started well for her new handler with two 1m4f seconds this winter; two lesser displays have followed, however, and she now wears headgear first time.
runners.comment: Lesser displays on last two starts mean that she now wears headgear first time

runners.name: Rose Cotton
runners.spotlight: Improved with the switch to handicaps and AW with a win at Wolverhampton (9.4f) in January and seconds there and at Lingfield (1m4f) this spring; player if that form is transferred to turf.
runners.comment: Improved with the switch to handicaps and AW; player if that form is transferred to turf
runners.owner: Hasmonean Racing, C Fahy And Marchants

runners.name: Amancio
runners.spotlight: Won this off 4lb lower last year (good) not long after a success over hurdles; lightly raced since, with a novice hurdle win in November before well beaten on handicap hurdle debut in December; major player if he's in the same form as 12 months ago and he has run well after a similar break before.
runners.comment: E-w player if in same form as 12 months ago (won this race); this time it's after a break

runners.name: Dust Cover
runners.spotlight: AW record since November reads 12112 and he was beaten a nose at Kempton 11 days ago; however, his record on turf is 85000, including a tailed-off run at Musselburgh (1m4f, good) last month, so there are loud warning bells today.
runners.comment: AW record since November reads 12112 but his record on turf is 85000

runners.name: Simiyann
runners.spotlight: Dual 2m winner last year; two heavy defeats over hurdles before perking up somewhat on return to Flat in January, but absent since; well handicapped on plenty of turf and AW form, so needs a close look, but the last time he raced over a trip this short was February 2024.
runners.comment: Well handicapped but the last time he raced over a trip this short was February 2024

runners.name: Patrol
runners.spotlight: Consistency seems far less evident in the last year or so but one trend has developed, as he's 3-13 on AW compared to 0-11 on turf; AW second last time was his best effort for a while but he's not the percentage call here.
runners.comment: AW second last time was his best effort for a while but he's not the percentage call

runners.name: Perfect Scoundrel
runners.spotlight: Fair form all five starts but well held in his two handicaps (1m2f) and may not yet have come far enough down the weights.
runners.comment: Well held in both handicaps (1m2f) and may not yet have come far enough down the weights
runners.owner: Mrs F Cotton, Mr & Mrs P Conway

runners.name: Vilaine Verlaine
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m2f winner on AW late as 2yo and runner-up in that sphere last May, but turf record is 0-9 and most turf form makes her opposable.
runners.comment: Runner-up on AW last May but turf record is 0-9 and most turf form makes her opposable

runners.name: Curran
runners.spotlight: Won two 1m6f turf handicaps last June but inconsistent afterwards and unraced since a flop in October; left Charlie Johnston for 6,000gns 16 days later and had wind surgery in March; well handicapped (2lb above last winning mark) if he returns revived for new stable.
runners.comment: Left C Johnston for 6,000gns in October; wind surgery March; well handicapped if revived

runners.name: Possible
runners.spotlight: Siyouni half-sister to four winners, three with peak Flat RPRs of 95, 100 and 105; dam French 1m4f winner (including Listed; 101); placed over 2m, 1m3f and 1m4f (touched off) last May/June on the last three of her four starts in France for Francis Graffard; sold for 80,000euros last July and 30,000euros last November, but worth a market check on this British/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Placed over 2m, 1m3f and 1m4f last May/June on the last three of her four starts in France

runners.name: Percy Jones
runners.spotlight: All four wins have come on Wolverhampton AW, the latest off this mark last August (1m4f); absent since November and 0-21 on turf but that includes six seconds, one of those behind Amancio in this race last year after a break (3lb higher today); has to be in each-way calculations.
runners.comment: 0-21 record on turf contains six 2nds, one of them behind Amancio in this race last year

races.race_name: EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Rocket Boots
runners.spotlight: March foal by Too Darn Hot who took a small step forward from his debut here when second of six at Doncaster (6f, good to firm; Lion O behind) a fortnight ago; offers a fair standard to aim at but faces a couple of potentially tasty newcomers; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Second (6f, good to firm; Lion O behind) 13 days ago sets a fair standard; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Al Hudaiba
runners.spotlight: January foal by Dark Angel; dam 7f 2yo (August debut) winner (RPR 89), sister to winners Wuheida (1m1f Grade 1 BC Filly & Mare Turf) and Winter's Song (1m2f), out of 1m 2yo Group 1 and US 1m2f Grade 1 winner; encouraging his dam went in first time as a juvenile and he's handed the honour of being the first 2yo representative of the season for powerful yard.
runners.comment: January foal by Dark Angel related to G1 winners; dam won 2yo debut; powerful yard

runners.name: Imperial Nation
runners.spotlight: March foal by Sioux Nation; 150,000gns yearling; brother to 5f-8.5f winner Brave Emperor (including 2yo/AW/Italian Group 2; RPR 116), half-brother to 7.4f winner Puella Law (79); dam 6f-1m maiden (67), half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Salouen; leading yard and market should provide some clues.
runners.comment: March foal by Sioux Nation; 150,000gns yearling; leading yard and market can guide

runners.name: Mrair
runners.spotlight: April foal by Mehmas; half-brother to six winners including Fan Club Rules (5f 2yo; RPR 101), Barrington (5f 2yo/6f; 99), Hastalavistababy (5.5f Listed; 97) and Mia Mento (6f 2yo; 91); more importantly he was £620,000 clear of his nearest pursuer when topping the Goffs breeze-up sale at £880,000 last month; likely to be some long faces if he can't make his presence felt.
runners.comment: £620,000 clear of nearest pursuer when topping breeze-up sale at £880,000 last month

runners.name: Jiro
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns March foal by first-season sire Perfect Power; 68,000euros yearling, 75,000gns breeze-up 2yo; dam placed 7f/1m1f (RPR 70), half-sister to French 1m2f winners Canndal (useful) and Clariyn (grandam of Calandagan), out of Listed-placed 1m winning close relative to French 2,000 Guineas Clodovil; first juvenile runner of the year for his stable and worth a market check.
runners.comment: March foal by Perfect Power; 75,000gns breeze-up 2yo; worth a market check

runners.name: Lion O
runners.spotlight: Early April foal by Mehmas; 150,000gns yearling; fairly weak 11-2 shot before dropping away when last of six at Doncaster (6f, good to firm; Rocket Boots second) a fortnight ago; some signs of inexperience that day so may do better; clearly needs to.
runners.comment: Last of six at Doncaster (6f, good to firm; Rocket Boots second) a fortnight ago

races.race_name: Debenhams Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tales Of Wisdom
runners.spotlight: Justified short odds in some style on Southwell AW debut (7f) in December but he had nearly the worst of the draw at Kempton recently (first-time hood, now discarded) and was never getting there in time; this 1m trip should suit and he still has potential.
runners.comment: Impressive 7f debut on AW; poorly drawn latest; step up to 1m can suit; has potential

runners.name: Quartermaster
runners.spotlight: 140,000gns yearling; dam won Listed race at 1m; raced freely but stayed on well to beat three rivals (headed by a Haggas runner) on Chelmsford AW debut over 1m in December; has more to come for yard that's won two of the last five runnings; now hooded.
runners.comment: 140,000gns yearling; stayed on well to beat 3 rivals on 1m AW debut; can find more; hooded

runners.name: Bemersyde
runners.spotlight: Respectable C&D debut behind a smart-looking winner and followed that with a keeping-on close third at Windsor (1m, good to firm) last week; the return to a stiff finish may well suit but some tough opposition awaits.
runners.comment: Respectable C&D debut; keeping-on 3rd at Windsor (1m) last week; toughish task

runners.name: Bayaann
runners.spotlight: 410,000gns yearling; dam bred several winners including in Group 2 (1m) and Listed (7f) races; smooth start to career when winning 7f maiden on Lingfield AW in November (stewards enquired about the running of the second who has done better since) and he looks sure to improve; up the list.
runners.comment: 410,000gns yearling; smooth start to career when winning 7f AW maiden as 2yo; can improve

runners.name: Queen's Companion
runners.spotlight: Some promise on debut and she held her position for a long way at Newbury (1m, soft) next time when third to smart Haggas stablemates (winner holds multiple Group 1 entries; the second impressive since) in October; interesting to see what the market says on her return.
