runners.comment: Promising 2yo, chased home smart Haggas rivals at Newbury (1m, soft) in October
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Cuff It
runners.spotlight: £55,000 yearling; gelded son of City Light; first foal; dam 6f-1m2f maiden (RPR 66), half-sister to 1m5f AW Listed winner, out of 1m5f AW Listed winning half-sister to 5f Group 1 winner Total Gallery; worth a market check but this is a competitive start to his career.
runners.comment: £55,000 yearling; this looks a competitive start to his career but worth a market check

runners.name: I Got Rhythm
runners.spotlight: 46,000euros yearling; third foal; by Isfahan out of unraced half-sister to UAE 1m2f Group 3 winner Star Safari, out of useful French 1m 2yo/10.5f winner; has been gelded; market can guide on debut but he'll do well to overcome more experienced rivals.
runners.comment: 46,000euros yearling; will do well to overcome more experienced rivals on debut

runners.name: King Cherry
runners.spotlight: Has shown signs of ability in 6f and 7f events on the AW this spring but others have achieved more and handicaps are on the horizon after today; now tongue tied.
runners.comment: Signs of ability in AW events at 6f and 7f; others have achieved more; now tongue tied

runners.name: Anthena
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure prices and soundly beaten in two starts, over 7f on Southwell AW as 2yo for previous yard and 1m2f on this track two weeks ago; can only watch.
runners.comment: Triple-figure prices and soundly beaten in two starts, at 7f and 1m2f; can only watch

runners.name: Me An' All
runners.spotlight: Big prices and has finished remote in both starts this year, well behind Bemersyde over C&D on debut, then dropped to 7f at Yarmouth in April; handicaps more suitable after today.
runners.comment: Big prices and finished remote in two starts this year, over C&D then 7f; handicaps soon

races.race_name: Boodles Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Topteam
runners.spotlight: Normally reliable and gritty performer, as evidenced by his comeback win at Kempton (AW; made all) in April, so his major flop at Epsom (again 1m4f, good) came as quite the surprise three weeks later; maybe that track didn't suit and his previous body of work means he can easily be given another chance.
runners.comment: Normally reliable and gritty performer but flopped latest; can be given another chance

runners.name: Double Meaning
runners.spotlight: Made good progress handicapping for this yard from August onwards, scoring at Southwell (1m6f) then easily defying a penalty at Lingfield (2m, also AW) ten days later; fine effort when fourth in the Old Rowley Cup over C&D (good) in October and had soft-ground excuse on final start of the term; not fully exposed and he's respected on this return.
runners.comment: Won twice over further last term and fine fourth over C&D in October; not fully exposed

runners.name: Marnier
runners.spotlight: Can lead; chased home two good ones on his debut at Newbury last April but he hasn't built on that and become expensive to follow; did win three-runner maiden at Lingfield (1m4f) in November but his latest second of six at Southwell (1m3f, also AW) was another reversal for supporters; form chance off same mark but tempting to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Won three-runner maiden in November but expensive to follow; tempting to look elsewhere

runners.name: Asgard's Captain
runners.spotlight: Did well to win at York (good to firm) off just 1lb lower in August but only fourth of five in the Great Metropolitan at Epsom (also 1m4f, good, Topteam behind) 17 days ago; that was a shade underwhelming given he'd won the race in 2025 but was a little weak in the market so maybe better can be expected this time.
runners.comment: Won off 1lb lower in August; weak in market when fourth of five latest so might do better

runners.name: Ernst Blofeld
runners.spotlight: Form tailed off sharply for the Gosdens but previously looked promising, a novice win at Kempton (AW) in April preceding good handicap debut fourth in valuable London Gold Cup at Newbury (also 1m2f, good to firm) in May; didn't run at two and has been gelded, so not one to dismiss on this return for new yard.
runners.comment: Good h'cap debut before losing form; gelded and not one to dismiss on return for new yard

runners.name: Pride Of Donegal
runners.spotlight: Steady improver on AW who won his third race in that sphere when making all at Wolverhampton in April; bit below that under a penalty when third on his turf debut at Thirsk (also 1m4f, good) but that came just six days later; five-week break should have recharged his batteries and pedigree points to grass being no problem.
runners.comment: Steady improver on AW whose turf debut third came just six days after latest win; player

runners.name: Master Vintner
runners.spotlight: Promising third on last May's debut for Andrew Oliver then won first two starts for this yard, novices at Goodwood (1m2f, heavy) and Lingfield (1m4f, AW) in September; slightly underwhelmed with midfield finish in November handicap (1m4f, heavy) next time but retains bundles of potential ahead of this reappearance.
runners.comment: Two novices wins for this yard and h'cap debut came in gruelling event; retains potential
runners.owner: R A Pegum And Paul Anthony Henry

runners.name: Arqoob
runners.spotlight: Exposed 8yo but he's still capable of useful form and scored at Kempton (1m4f, AW) last March; also won over 1m6f on the July course (good to firm; first-time blinkers) in August; returns on a stiff-enough mark with no headgear but they might go a solid gallop which can help.
runners.comment: 1m6f winner (first-time blinkers) in August; no headgear today but a solid gallop can help

races.race_name: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: O'Gorman
runners.spotlight: Both starts at 6f; another who improved from his debut when taking ten-runner Kempton novice (AW) a fortnight ago; return to turf surely no problem and he's respected under the penalty.
runners.comment: Improved when taking ten-runner AW novice 15 days ago; respected under the penalty

runners.name: Ballisty
runners.spotlight: March foal; 12-1 and it was an encouraging start when third of six at Goodwood (5f, good) a fortnight ago; looked likely to appreciate an extra furlong that day so he's a big player up to this trip on this second go.
runners.comment: March foal; encouraging third (5f, good) a fortnight ago; 6f looks a positive; big player

runners.name: Mr Macartney
runners.spotlight: Good step forward from debut AW fourth when beating 11 others at Leicester (also 5f, good to firm; Turnstile third) a fortnight later; still looked a little raw that day and warrants respect up to 6f under 6lb penalty.
runners.comment: Step forward to win (5f, good to firm) 20 days ago; respected up to 6f under the penalty

runners.name: Turnstile
runners.spotlight: Evident inexperience on his debut at Thirsk (good) and did better when third to Mr Macartney at Leicester (good to firm) three weeks ago; 6lb pull in the weights gives him solid prospects of turning the tables on this first go at 6f.
runners.comment: Third to Mr Macartney three weeks ago; 6lb pull makes it interesting; first go at 6f

runners.name: Duidin
runners.spotlight: February foal; attracted support before promising third of 10 at Kempton (6f, AW) a fortnight ago; had a wide trip that day, and pedigree suggests turf will be fine, but Oisin Murphy has jumped ship; still firmly considered.
runners.comment: Promising third (6f, AW) a fortnight ago; had a wide trip that day; firmly considered

runners.name: Celtic Charioteer
runners.spotlight: Late January foal by Mondialiste; fifth foal; brother to UAE 9.5f dirt winner Withering (RPR 109); dam unraced sister to 5.5f-1m winner Predetermined, half-sister to winners Classic Blade (6f 2yo Group 2), Raadobarg (smart 7f-1m) and Captain Dunne (smart 5f); first juvenile runner of the year for this stable; watch the market.
runners.comment: Late January foal by Mondialiste; first juvenile runner of the year for yard; watch market

runners.name: Temple Court
runners.spotlight: Late January foal by Inns Of Court; 20,000euros yearling; half-brother to 7.4f winner Bowen Island (RPR 84); dam runner-up 10.6f AW (61), half-sister to winners Taniyar (7f Group 3) and Tanaza (7f/7.4f Group 3 including 2yo), out of 1m4f Listed winning half-sister to 1m2f Group 2 winner Takali; draw on the wing isn't helpful to a newcomer but yard always to be respected in these events; betting helpful.
runners.comment: Late January; 20,000euros yearling; yard respected in these events; betting helpful

runners.name: Mirzimaan
runners.spotlight: March foal by Mohaather; dam unraced sister to legendary bullet sprinter Battaash, who won four 5f Group 1s in these colours; that alone makes him of interest so interesting to see how he goes in the market.
runners.comment: March foal; dam unraced sister to legendary bullet sprinter Battaash; market helpful

runners.name: Agitator
runners.spotlight: Late February foal by Cracksman; 30,000gns yearling; half-brother to 7f 2yo/1m4f winner Meribella (RPR 96); dam ran once (7f 2yo; 46), half-sister to winners Poet's Word (1m2f/1m4f Group 1), Malabar (7f/1m Group 3) and Clowance Estate (useful 1m2f/1m3f); second juvenile runner of the year for this yard and any market clues should be heeded.
runners.comment: Late February foal by Cracksman; 30,000gns yearling; market clues should be heeded

runners.name: Fly Test
runners.spotlight: Late March foal by Bungle Inthejungle; 30,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 5.7f/6f 2yo winner (RPR 80), half-sister to prolific 5f-6.5f winner Something Lucky, out of 5f winning sister to multiple 5f-6f winner Saxonette; yard yet to strike from a handful of 2yo runners this term, but took this with a 14-1 newcomer in 2024 and that's a speedy pedigree.
runners.comment: Late March foal; 30,000gns yearling; yard took this with a 14-1 newcomer in 2024

runners.name: Regal Chita
runners.spotlight: March foal by Acclamation; 21,000gns yearling; second foal; dam 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 98), closely related to winners Encrypted Message (7f/1m3f) and Polished Rock (1m2f), half-sister to useful 1m winner Stay De Night, out of US Grade 3 1m1f winner; yard off the mark with its juveniles and betting can guide.
runners.comment: March foal; 21,000gns yearling; yard off the mark with its juveniles and betting can guide

runners.name: Zohar
runners.spotlight: January 20,000euros foal; 25,000euros yearling; already gelded half-brother to winners Ribee (7f-8.3f; RPR 102) and Signalman (1m1f-1m4f including Bahrain; 97); dam runner-up 1m2f/1m4f (77); yard going nicely with this term's juveniles (3-10) and this son of Kodiac has a foaling date advantage over the field.
runners.comment: January foal; 25,000euros yearling; yard 3-10 with this year's 2yos; betting helpful

runners.name: Last Dandelion
runners.spotlight: March foal who was 66-1 and always out the back when finishing last of ten at Kempton (6f, AW) a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: 66-1 and always out the back when last of ten at Kempton (6f, AW) a fortnight ago

runners.name: Holi Scarlett
runners.spotlight: April foal who was 40-1 for her debut here (5f, good to firm) when blowing the start 12 days ago; contender to step forward if getting away on terms.
runners.comment: Blew the start on debut 12 days ago; contender to step forward if getting away on terms

races.race_name: Turners Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Crazee Icon
runners.spotlight: Yet to score but she looked unlucky when going mighty close on nursery debut at Chester (7f, good to soft) in September; bettered that form, despite taking a firm hold and being a little mucked about, when narrowly denied again at Windsor (1m, good to firm; Tamzan second) last Monday; hood added and she's 2lb well-in; key player.
runners.comment: Unlucky 2nd on h'cap debut & again when 2nd last Monday; new hood and 2lb well-in; player

runners.name: Tamzan
runners.spotlight: Yet to score but she's been competitive on plenty of occasions and her third at Kempton (1m, AW) in October reads well; encouraging comeback when beaten 2l over that C&D last month and she didn't get the breaks when third at Windsor (1m, good to firm; Crazee Icon second) last Monday; draw possibly asks a question but she goes off the same mark and looks a leading contender.
runners.comment: Unlucky when third (1m, good to firm) last Monday; same mark and looks a leading contender

runners.name: Bearin Up
runners.spotlight: Third handicap start when she opened her account at Ripon (1m) in September; bettered that form when third of 12 at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) 19 days ago; same mark and she should go well again.
runners.comment: Bettered previous form when third (1m1f, good) 19 days ago; same mark and should go well

runners.name: Dream Illusion
runners.spotlight: Best form on AW and she's 0-7 on turf; exposed filly but she landed her fourth AW handicap when scoring at Southwell (1m) in March and was back in that career-best form when second over that C&D a fortnight ago; chance if she can transfer those efforts to grass.
runners.comment: Back in career-best form when 2nd on AW 16 days ago; chance if transferring that to turf

runners.name: Borderline Madness
runners.spotlight: Turf winner on good to soft in July who won her second AW race when scoring at Southwell (also 1m) a fortnight ago; 2lb nudge no reason to rule her out.
runners.comment: Turf winner on good to soft; won (also 1m, AW) a fortnight ago; 2lb nudge no barrier

runners.name: Daretobedifferent
runners.spotlight: Has finished third five times and that was the case again at Leicester (7f, good to firm) three weeks ago; step up in trip might help and will likely go well again while finding one or two too good.
runners.comment: Third for fifth time latest; steps up to 1m and can go well; vulnerable for the win though

runners.name: Safe Harbor
runners.spotlight: All starts on AW; similar form to two of her three 7f qualifying runs when third of five at Southwell (1m) on handicap debut three weeks ago; first-time cheekpieces will need to eke out more but it's still early days.
runners.comment: Third of five (1m, AW) on handicap debut three weeks ago; cheekpieces need to eke out more

runners.name: Khuskhas
runners.spotlight: Won Yarmouth maiden (6f, good) in June for Kevin Philippart De Foy but went backwards subsequently; first-time blinkers (retained) when fifth of nine at Southwell (7f, AW) on seasonal/stable debut last month; needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Went backwards for old yard; fifth on seasonal/stable debut last month needs building on

runners.name: India Gold
runners.spotlight: Belied market weakness to get off the mark in first-time hood at Musselburgh (7f, soft; fifth start) in November; didn't match that form off this mark when sixth on nursery debut at Southwell (1m, AW) the following month; no hood for this return but any softening of the ground should be in her favour.
runners.comment: Won in first-time hood (7f, soft) in November; h'cap debut not so good; no hood for return

runners.name: Wild Rosie
runners.spotlight: Didn't progress for Owen Burrows at around 1m last year and her stable/handicap/seasonal debut over 6f offered little encouragement last month; cheekpieces reached for.
runners.comment: Stable/handicap/seasonal debut offered little encouragement last month; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Blue Samuna
runners.spotlight: Best form on debut when sixth of ten in good July course novice (7f, good to firm) in August; hasn't kicked on from there but would have claims on this handicap debut if tapping back into that initial promise.
runners.comment: Not kicked on from solid start but claims on h'cap debut if tapping back into that promise

runners.name: Case Study
runners.spotlight: All starts at 6f; made inauspicious turf debut then modest form in two AW novices; respected yard and step up in trip on handicap debut the straws to clutch at; worth a market check ahead of her return.
runners.comment: Respected yard/step up to 1m on h'cap debut the straws to clutch at; market check advised

runners.name: Tardaff
runners.spotlight: Only made her debut just under a year ago; best effort on second start when runner-up in 7f novice on the July course (good to firm) in June; not badly treated on that form but didn't build on it and ended last term with a whimper when tailed off at Musselburgh in August; market might help on this seasonal/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Tailed off last start; fairly treated on earlier form; market a help on return/h'cap debut

races.race_name: JCB Handicap

runners.name: A Major Payne
runners.spotlight: 0-16 but he'd gone close at 1m and did so again over 1m2f when third of 13 at Yarmouth (good; Beach Point fourth) on his reappearance last month; returning cheekpieces did little harm when second to unexposed sort over that C&D (good to firm; Beach Point third) a fortnight ago; should be thereabouts again.
runners.comment: Narrowly ahead of Beach Point when placed both starts this term; tight between them again

runners.name: Caph Star
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 7yo who can race freely; off the mark for this yard when beating 13 others at Nottingham (1m2f, good) in April; backed that up with creditable third (AW) when 1m3f just looked to stretch him 11 days ago; player.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 7yo; beat 13 others (1m2f) in April; creditable third (1m3f) latest; player

runners.name: Beach Point
runners.spotlight: Two Yarmouth runs over 1m2f in April tie in closely with A Major Payne, including when a whisker behind that rival in third a fortnight ago; shouldn't be much between the pair again.
runners.comment: Just behind A Major Payne on last two starts; shouldn't be much between the pair again
runners.owner: John & Peter Seabrook And Tim Fisher

runners.name: Sansanetti
runners.spotlight: Had a fine time of it once handicapping over 1m2f last term, going back-to-back at Chelmsford (AW) and Leicester (good to firm) in the summer before repeating the feat in AW handicaps on her final two starts of the year; still looks competitively handicapped and no obvious reason why she shouldn't resume her progress on this return.
runners.comment: Prolific at 1m2f for this yard last term; still looks competitively handicapped on return

runners.name: Salaria
runners.spotlight: 1m2f winner who landed a fourth success for this yard when scoring at Lingfield (1m, AW) in March; step back in the right direction when sixth of 12 at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) last time and return to this trip could help.
runners.comment: 1m2f winner; step back in right direction last time and return to this trip could help

runners.name: Expressionless
runners.spotlight: Good comeback over 1m2f when third of 15 at Doncaster (good to soft) in March and made it win number six when going in at Leicester (1m4f, good) next time; never threatened over 2m at Ascot last Friday but not ruled out back down in trip.
runners.comment: No show at 2m latest but previously won at 1m4f and effective at 1m2f; not ruled out

runners.name: Caledonian
runners.spotlight: Scored on his AW/nursery debut in 2024 but wasn't competitive in just three starts last year; did gain plenty of market traction on the last of them, though, and last month's reappearance at Bath wasn't totally devoid of promise; tries 1m2f for just the second time and he's not fully exposed.
runners.comment: No impact in three starts last term; tries 1m2f for just second time; not fully exposed

runners.name: Sir Edward Lear
runners.spotlight: Wore cheekpieces when just holding on in six-runner July course handicap (1m2f, good to firm) in August; visored when he stopped quickly on good to soft at Nottingham in October and not seen since a hurdles debut the following month; cheekpieces back on for this return.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces when just holding on (1m2f) in August; gets that aid for the first time since

races.race_name: Behind The Bridle Racehorse Aftercare Handicap

runners.name: We're Goosers
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with an AW win over 7f (made most); thoroughly encouraging reappearance here (1m, good) in April on handicap debut, where he looked likely to reward market support but the race slipped from his grasp late on; major chance off the same mark back at 7f.
runners.comment: AW winner at 7f as 2yo; excellent return on handicap debut (1m); major chance back at 7f

runners.name: Magic Box
runners.spotlight: Two 7f wins last July, on AW and at Ayr (soft), but she went up 7lb for the latter success which seemed to put paid to her progress in handicaps; relatively exposed for a 3yo from this yard and likely needs to have done well over the winter to make a winning return.
runners.comment: Two 7f wins last July but went up 7lb for the latest of them and held on last 2 starts

runners.name: Deluded
runners.spotlight: Narrowly landed the odds in 7f maiden on AW last September; up against it on final 2yo start but he was gaining with every stride when only just failing on Wolverhampton AW (7f; handicap debut) in April; up 3lb; needs more for the win but should have some improvement in him.
runners.comment: Won 7f AW maiden as 2yo; just failed on handicap debut latest; up 3lb but can improve

runners.name: Wield
runners.spotlight: Was returning from a layoff when third of six on Chelmsford AW in March and was all the better for that run when hanging on grimly to his lead in a C&D maiden last month, until seen off late by a Listed-class rival; left Andrew Balding for 35,000gns since; form claims on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Saw off all but a Listed-class rival over C&D latest; left A Balding since; form chance

runners.name: Gizmo
runners.spotlight: Two runs last September, belying 25-1 odds when quite impressive over 7f on Lingfield AW; had a much stiffer task at Epsom after (about 1m, good), albeit ran as well as could have been expected in a three-runner conditions race; should have some handicap potential off his opening mark.
runners.comment: Two runs in September, belied 25-1 odds when winning on AW; tough task since; h'cap debut

runners.name: Just A Girl
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin winner of a Lingfield novice (extended 7f, good) last August but she's been vulnerable since, in handicaps and Listed company, and this looks like more hard work for her.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner of a novice last August; suffered in the weights since; hard work again

races.race_name: Horse Comes First Handicap

runners.name: Southern Warrior
runners.spotlight: Successful handicap debut on Newcastle AW (6f) in March for George Scott but he went up 3lb and was just a onepaced third of five at Kempton after; sold for 10,000gns since; still relatively early days but this might be needed for new trainer on first turf run.
runners.comment: Won h'cap debut on AW (6f); lesser run since; sold 10,000gns; turf debut for new yard

runners.name: Akabusi
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins on turf and AW for previous trainers and he ran right up to his best when a close fourth on Lingfield AW for this one in April; has had some excuses before and since and, while he's on the exposed side now, a good run can't be ruled out back on turf.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins on turf and AW as 2yo; had some excuses either side of a 4th on AW in April

runners.name: Daizen
runners.spotlight: Three good 6f runs since gelded, in two handicaps and when winning an Ayr maiden (soft); found little on his recent Pontefract return (6f, good) but that may well have been needed and he'd be of interest if the ground eases.
runners.comment: Three good 6f runs on final 2yo starts, winning on soft; should improve for recent return

runners.name: Quantum Power
runners.spotlight: Won Beverley maiden (5f, soft) in September; not much between him and Akabusi on an AW run later but he never reached a challenging position on his Bath return (50-1) in April and some weight off his back wouldn't go amiss; visor refitted.
runners.comment: Won 5f Beverley maiden as 2yo; never involved on Bath return (50-1); visor returns

runners.name: Ecclefechan
runners.spotlight: Maiden who had some useful 2yo form, including in a competitive 7f handicap on this course before going close against a big improver over 6f on Lingfield AW in November; satisfactory reappearance in March but was seriously hampered at Ripon since; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Maiden; had some useful 2yo form, including here; badly hampered latest; interesting

runners.name: Victor Cee
runners.spotlight: Made all in 6f Hamilton maiden for Karl Burke as 2yo; has run well in defeat for new yard since, if lacking the pace to get involved when a persevering third at Catterick (5f, good) last month; withdrawn at Yarmouth on Wednesday on account of unsuitable ground (good).
runners.comment: Made all for 6f maiden win as 2yo; some good runs for new yard at 6f and 5f

runners.name: King Of Chaos
runners.spotlight: Yard won this in 2024; off the mark when making all in 7f Lingfield AW maiden in February on third run for new trainer but he's rarely reached the same level since and has something to prove on the return to 6f.
runners.comment: Off the mark in 7f AW maiden in February; rarely run to the same level since; work to do

runners.name: Nebulon
runners.spotlight: All three runs at 6f, doing best when runner-up on Wolverhampton AW in March following a layoff; those either side haven't distinguished themselves since and he himself faded at Newcastle in April but he's been gelded since and handicaps are more realistic now.
runners.comment: Best 6f form when 2nd on AW return; not so good since but gelded and now goes handicapping

runners.name: Logi Bear
runners.spotlight: Busy 2yo for Richard Hannon, winning a C&D novice last May and sometimes acquitting himself well after, despite having stiff tasks; has dropped down the weights and he ran respectably on yard/AW debut at Newcastle recently (7-2), eased when beaten; may well do much better back on turf.
runners.comment: Busy 2yo, C&D winner last May for R Hannon; some good efforts since; dropped down weights

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden

runners.name: King Of Cloughan
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica colt a 110,000euros yearling; first foal; dam US 5.5f/6f turf 2yo winner (inc stakes; RPR 99), half-sister to US stakes-placed 1m/8.5f winner Tempers Flair; promising enough debut effort at Navan last month, bit green and challenge petering out inside final furlong; upped in trip here.
runners.comment: Promising enough Navan debut run; entitled to improve, will likely need to

runners.name: West Of Eden
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never colt a 500,000euros yearling; second foal; dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 81), half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Heliac, out of French 1m5f winning half-sister to winner Meteor Storm (US 1m2f Grade 1); odds-on for last month's Navan debut but never landed a meaningful blow, outpaced some way out when only fifth (3l off fourth-placed King Of Cloughan); interesting Ryan Moore remains loyal and upped in trip here.
runners.comment: Disappointing Navan debut run but Ryan Moore remains loyal and upped in trip

runners.name: Chesham
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars filly cost 150,000gns as a yearling; fourth foal; sister to winners Space Legend (Group-placed 1m2f; RPR 112) and Newton's Star (US 8.5f turf); dam unraced half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed winner Treasured Isle; yard has already introduced some smart 2yos this season and interesting this filly is pitched in against the colts; seems sure to be ready for a bold bid.
runners.comment: Yard have a strong team of 2yos already; filly seems sure to be ready for a bold bid
runners.owner: Valmont, Amo Racing & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Florida Bay
runners.spotlight: Mehmas colt cost 270,000euros as a foal and 375,000gns as a yearling; second foal; half-brother to French 1m winner Alhama River; dam French 1m1f winner (inc 2yo), half-sister to winners Reggane (Canadian 1m2f Grade 1) and Areen Heart (7f/7.4f inc 2yo), out of Group-placed 1m2f winner; rare runner by this sire for the yard, seemingly second-choice on jockey bookings.
runners.comment: 375,000gns son of Mehmas; seemingly second-choice on jockey bookings

runners.name: Nola Soul
runners.spotlight: Justify colt cost 220,000USD as a yearling; second foal; half-brother to US 1m dirt winner Equal Force; dam maiden half-sister to US 1m winner, out of close relative to US 1m1f Grade 3 winner Andromeda's Hero; market likely best guide on debut.
runners.comment: 220,000USD Justify colt; market best guide on debut

runners.name: Ted's Turn
runners.spotlight: New Bay half-brother to 7f 2yo/1m2f winner Sea Poetry (RPR 91); dam French Group-placed 1m 2yo winner (106), out of sister to French 10.5f Group 3 winner Albisola; likely all the better for the experience but worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: New Bay colt likely all the better for the experience but worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Telegraph Road
runners.spotlight: Night Of Thunder colt is second foal of a Group-placed 9.5f winner (RPR 106), a half-sister to French 1m4f Group 2 winner One Foot In Heaven, family of Champion Stakes winner Pride & 1,000 Guineas winner Speciosa; another nicely-bred debutant for whom market should tell a lot.
runners.comment: Night Of Thunder colt another nicely-bred debutant for whom market should tell a lot.

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden

runners.name: Suspicious Mindz
runners.spotlight: Sets a good standard on his Tipperary Listed third of last season and most recently when second in Cork maiden over this trip last month (third has won since); 6l ahead of Coasting then.
runners.comment: Sets a good standard on his Tipperary Listed third and recent Cork maiden second

runners.name: Matilda Joslyngage
runners.spotlight: Much improved on final 2yo start when upped to 1m, staying on well into second late on at Thurles (good-to-yielding);
runners.comment: Got 1m well at Thurles last season; one to consider

runners.name: Full Moon Tonight
runners.spotlight: Shaped promisingly on both AW runs over the winter, second occasion slightly hampered when beaten only 1l into fourth over 7f; more needed now upped in trip.
runners.comment: AW promise but more now needed on turf debut

runners.name: Coasting
runners.spotlight: Bated Breath gelding posted okay efforts last month at Dundalk and when slow-starting fifth (Suspicious Mindz second) in stronger Cork contest over today's trip; more required.
runners.comment: Fair form but good bit to find with Suspicious Mindz on recent Cork run

runners.name: Kirkland Sioux
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly is first foal of a 1m4f winner (RPR 83), a half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Heavens Gate, out of 1m winning half-sister to 1,000 Guineas winner Ghanaati; yard in red-hot form so market support for this newcomer has to be noted.
runners.comment: Sioux Nation filly; yard in red-hot form so market support for this newcomer noted

runners.name: Make The Tide
runners.spotlight: Make Believe half-sister to 7f 2yo winner Hopewell Rock (RPR 110); dam unraced sister to 1m 2yo Group 1 (Racing Post Trophy) winner Marcel; another debutante for whom market should be informative.
runners.comment: Make Believe filly; another debutante for whom market should be informative

runners.name: Saga Gaga
runners.spotlight: Lucky Vega half-sister to 7f winner Far Ahead (RPR 77); dam 1m2f winner (70), closely related to useful 1m-1m2f winner Sagaciously; yard yet to have a 2yo winner this season.
runners.comment: Lucky Vega filly; yard yet to have a 2yo winner this season

runners.name: Daring Heart
runners.spotlight: Kodi Bear half-brother to five winners inc Bobby Bennu (7f/7.6f inc AW; RPR 105), Mattymolls Gaga (7f inc 2yo/HK; 93) and Lord Clenaghcastle (5f-8.5f inc 2yo/AW; 87); dam maiden; definite promise on only 2yo start, staying on well after not clear run in decent Curragh maiden in August; improvement likely and one to consider.
runners.comment: Definite promise on only 2yo start; improvement likely and one to consider

runners.name: De Songbird
runners.spotlight: No real threat in maidens both here and at Naas last month; eligible for a mark after this.
runners.comment: No real threat in maidens both here and at Naas last month; eligible for a mark after this

runners.name: Mr Perth
runners.spotlight: Couple of modest enough runs this term on testing ground, more likely one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Couple of modest enough runs this term on testing ground, more likely one for handicaps

runners.name: Trustmore
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation colt never landed a blow on sole 2yo outing but did make late headway when beaten little over 4l in decent Curragh maiden; 1.5l off Daring Heart then so improvement needed and cheekpieces fitted here.
runners.comment: Bit to find with Daring Heart on Curragh run but cheekpieces on and don't rule out

runners.name: Avoca Woods
runners.spotlight: Sea The Moon colt reportedly didn't handle testing ground on last month's Curragh debut; better ground should suit.
runners.comment: Reportedly didn't handle testing ground on last month's Curragh debut

runners.name: Sunrush
runners.spotlight: Yard in form but this colt produced two moderate efforts in maidens last season at Tipperary (7f, yielding) and the Curragh (7f, yld-sft); probably best watched upped in trip on seasonal return.
runners.comment: Couple of ordinary 2yo efforts, handicaps likely more his thing

runners.name: Farewell To Arms
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to six winners inc Lord Riddiford (prolific 5f inc 2yo/AW/Listed; RPR 107), Mister Freeze (6f/7f AW; 76) and Hope Probe (5f/7f; 71); dam placed 1m1f, out of 1m winning half-sister to Middle Park winner First Trump; never sighted in late-season Gowran 2yo maiden; tongue-tie fitted for return but can only be watched.
runners.comment: Never sighted in Gowran 2yo maiden; tongue-tie fitted for return but best watched

runners.name: Thistle Cross
runners.spotlight: Couple of modest AW efforts late last year, easily overlooked now on turf debut.
runners.comment: Couple of modest AW efforts late last year, easily overlooked now on turf debut

races.race_name: Leopardstown Friday Night Social Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Bay Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Won twice for James Ferguson last year, including on turf at Nottingham; tailed off on stable debut at Bellewstown when badly hampered; suited by good ground.
runners.comment: Highly consistent in Britain last year, tailed off on stable debut when badly hampered

runners.name: Ohmali
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground winner over this trip at Gowran last September; fair form on better ground; below best on soft on seasonal debut at Gowran.
runners.comment: Heavy-ground winner last season, fair form on better ground, below best on seasonal debut

runners.name: Land Of The Giants
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win over 1m1f at Ayr ten days ago on his 18th start; more needed to win back on home territory, since he is now 6lb higher than when beaten a neck by Bucaneer's Spirit at Cork last month.
runners.comment: Breakthrough win over 1m1f at Ayr ten days ago, more needed to win back on home territory
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Nigel O'Hare & Philip Smith & Bart O'Sullivan

runners.name: Moyassr
runners.spotlight: Boasts a fine AW record (4-10, twice second), continues to struggle on turf (0-11); towards the rear in races at Limerick and the Curragh this term.
runners.comment: Boasts a fine AW record (4-10, twice second), continues to struggle on turf (0-11)

runners.name: Monvoe
runners.spotlight: Finished runner-up at Dundalk on final two starts last year when upped to 7f after two runs in sprint handicaps; built on that when scoring this trip at Limerick last month; can remain competitive.
runners.comment: Built on his useful AW form when winning at Limerick last month, can remain competitive

runners.name: Bucaneer's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Won a Ballinrobe claimer last summer before struggling in handicaps; took advantage of a reduced mark to beat Land Of The Giants at Cork; can feature again, though cut no ice in the charity race at Punchestown.
runners.comment: Took advantage of a reduced mark to beat Land Of The Giants at Cork, can feature again

runners.name: Rappell
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins, the latest at the Curragh in 2024; added a 6f AW win last September; needs to step up considerably on the form of this season's two outings at the Curragh.
runners.comment: Needs to step up considerably on the form of this season's two outings at the Curragh

runners.name: Battle Borne
runners.spotlight: Has done most of his racing at Dundalk, highlight provided by a 7f maiden win in February; struggled over C&D last month, last of 17; blinkered now.
runners.comment: Has done most of his racing at Dundalk, struggled over C&D last month, blinkered now

runners.name: Dynamic Force
runners.spotlight: Speedy in his early days, winning three times over 5f for Robert Cowell; sole Irish win gained at 1m in a claimer; on a long losing sequence and lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: On a long losing sequence and lacks a recent run, very capable rider claims 7lb

runners.name: God Knows
runners.spotlight: Twice second for this yard after being acquired from a claimer last summer; went close over this trip at the Curragh in October; never involved on her comeback there over 7f; needs to bounce back after a below-par run at Sligo; hooded now.
runners.comment: In good form last autumn, needs to bounce back after a below-par run at Sligo

runners.name: Lough Leane
runners.spotlight: All six wins in Britain came on the AW; fine run on the opening day of the season, when runner-up in a 26-runner Curragh handicap (1m, heavy); not quite at that level on next two starts before winning well over a longer trip at Ballinrobe last week; strong contender.
runners.comment: Won well over a longer trip at Ballinrobe last week, previously best known for his AW wins

runners.name: Faoladh
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a good spell last summer, winning over C&D off 2lb lower; moderate Dundalk run in March; fair return to turf over C&D when fourth of 17; has a bit to find with Monvoe on Limerick running.
runners.comment: C&D winner last June off 2lb lower, has a bit to find with Monvoe on Limerick running

runners.name: Java Wood
runners.spotlight: Modest form in turf maidens before 40-1 win on AW/handicap debut last August; made a pleasing return when staying on for fifth of 15 at the Curragh 11 days ago; extra furlong will suit; fair chance for rider with plenty of pony-racing experience
runners.comment: Long-priced winner on AW last August on handicap debut, pleasing return at the Curragh

runners.name: Proleek Prince
runners.spotlight: Useful form in 2024, winning over this trip at Punchestown and Dundalk; failed to prosper last year; not a bad 7f run here on seasonal debut and may improve over the extra furlong.
runners.comment: Useful form in 2024, failed to prosper last year, not a bad 7f run here on seasonal debut

runners.name: Perfect Judgement
runners.spotlight: Four AW wins; signs of a return to form at Dundalk last time; his record on turf is not very convincing (1-16), though his single success came at this venue in 2024.
runners.comment: Signs of a return to form at Dundalk last time, his record on turf is not very convincing

runners.name: Rockbury Lad
runners.spotlight: Placed twice at Dundalk during the winter; confidence is somewhat dented by 2-28 record on turf; placed only once from seven runs at this track.
runners.comment: Placed twice at Dundalk during the winter, confidence dented by 2-28 record on turf
runners.owner: P E Ryan & J G Shanahan

runners.name: Sheamus Seimhiu
runners.spotlight: Never closer than sixth in eight starts; the booking of Nicola Burns is the main positive.
runners.comment: Never closer than sixth in eight starts, the booking of Nicola Burns is the main positive

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap

runners.name: Star Mind
runners.spotlight: Was fit from Dundalk when winning an amateur riders' race over 1m1f here a month ago; 9lb rise offset by rider's claim; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Was fit from Dundalk when winning well here a month ago, 9lb rise offset by rider's claim

runners.name: Inthesmallhours
runners.spotlight: Easy winner of 1m4f AW maiden just before Christmas 2024; did not appear in 2025; soft ground was probably against her on recent comeback at Sligo, eased after her chance had gone.
runners.comment: 1m4f Dundalk winner in December 2024, soft ground was against her on recent comeback

runners.name: Casheldale Lass
runners.spotlight: Won Gowran and Naas handicaps last year; needs to step up from a low-key return at Navan; held by Tofino.
runners.comment: Won twice last summer, needs to step up from a low-key return at Navan, held by Tofino

runners.name: Great Mover
runners.spotlight: Won on heavy ground at Gowran last September; has failed to retrieve her best form in three outings this term, though she was aimed high in the Irish Lincoln and was not beaten far in sixth at Limerick on latest.
runners.comment: Has been unable to match the form she showed in the final months of last season

runners.name: Tofino
runners.spotlight: Won a Curragh handicap over 1m on good ground last year; put in a satisfactory effort at Navan last month when tackling this trip for the first time; worth considering.
runners.comment: Satisfactory run at Navan when tackling this trip for the first time, worth considering

runners.name: Diamond Exchange
runners.spotlight: Second in a small-field C&D maiden in May; well beaten in third behind a useful filly at Down Royal; winning favourite in a Dundalk maiden in January; made a satisfactory start to this season when third in a C&D event on yielding-to-soft; better ground will suit; in season when taken out of a race in a higher grade here last Sunday.
runners.comment: Ran out of steam on soft ground here a month ago, leading chance now on a quicker surface
runners.owner: Bronsan Racing & H Redknapp & John Breslin

runners.name: Tatum
runners.spotlight: Very reliable in the first half of last season, notching two wins at this venue and three places elsewhere between May and July; wins were over 1m4f and 1m5f, and this trip is shorter than ideal for her seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Two wins and three places between May and July last year, this trip is shorter than ideal

runners.name: Summer Snow
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicaps at 7f/1m last season; possibly a bit high in the ratings now; plenty of weight here for her seasonal debut, 19lb higher than when winning the apprentice handicap on this card 12 months ago.
runners.comment: Progressive in handicaps at 7f/1m last season, possibly a bit high in the ratings now

runners.name: Chica Guerrera
runners.spotlight: Tramore 1m4f winner last August and finished the season well with placed efforts in big fields on heavy ground at the Curragh; recent Sligo second was on very soft ground; would probably benefit from an ease.
runners.comment: Good form in testing conditions late last season, recent Sligo second on very soft ground

runners.name: Timeless Charm
runners.spotlight: Two wins in Britain were on AW; ground should suit but might prefer a longer trip; hard-earned Newcastle win last September was over an extended 1m4f.
runners.comment: Two wins in Britain were on AW, ground should suit but might prefer a longer trip

runners.name: Eastern Peace
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner, unplaced on handicap debut; not much experience on turf; stable's current form is a positive.
runners.comment: AW maiden winner, unplaced on handicap debut, not much experience on turf
runners.owner: Martin W Walsh & Tony Carey & Brian Murray & Mark Hopkins

races.race_name: Saval Beg Levmoss Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Scandinavia
runners.spotlight: Won four of his five races at three; improved with cheekpieces after Queen's Vase fifth, won a 1m5f Group 3 at Newmarket by almost 9l and mastering older stablemate Illinois in the Goodwood Cup (2m, good); dug deep to hold off Rahiebb on soft ground in the St Leger; readily coped with Dallas Star at Navan last month and now continues on the Kyprios route to the Gold Cup.
runners.comment: Goodwood Cup/St Leger winner, good start to the season at Navan, this is straightforward
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Happy Pharoah
runners.spotlight: Strong form at this venue last season, winning three times here, including a C&D Listed race and a 1m5f premier handicap; never a factor in the Irish Cesarewitch; probably unsuited by very soft ground on his seasonal debut at the Curragh six weeks ago.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner last year, probably unsuited by very soft ground on his return

runners.name: Dallas Star
runners.spotlight: Won the 1m2f Group 3 Ballysax Stakes here in April 2024 (1m2f, heavy); last win was at Dundalk early last year; no match for Leinster in a St Leger trial at the Curragh last August; twice placed in small-field AW events during the winter; stronger form in defeat behind Scandinavia at Navan; not certain to uphold form with Leinster.
runners.comment: Had a fitness advantage at Navan, may struggle to uphold form with Leinster

runners.name: Le Destrier
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained in Poland and France; finished fifth of seven in a 1m4f Group 1 in Germany in 2023; last seen when second to a useful Andre Fabre-trained horse (later Group 2-placed) in a 1m4f Listed race at Lyon Parilly in June 2024; same ownership as stable's winning hurdler and Ebor sixth Hipop De Loire; interesting with the future in mind.
runners.comment: First run since second in a 1m4f Listed race at Lyon Parilly in June 2024, best watched

runners.name: Mont St Michel
runners.spotlight: Ex-Ballydoyle; 2m1f Killarney winner last August; out of depth in Group 2 Prix Foy and well below form at the Curragh on final start; ran twice over hurdles for this stable before a below-par run on AW/handicap debut last month; up against it here.
runners.comment: Ex-Ballydoyle; 2m1f Killarney winner last August,
runners.owner: Patrick John O'Brien & J Carthy & M G Hogan

runners.name: Leinster
runners.spotlight: Fourth in Chester Cup and Ascot Stakes last season, won a four-runner St Leger Trial at the Curragh in August, readily brushing aside front-running Dallas Star; failed to do himself justice when tailed off in the Irish St Leger, made late progress into fourth behind Scandinavia at Navan on return; should be sharper now and may reverse form with runner-up Dallas Star.
runners.comment: Should be better for his Navan run; last year's form makes him superior to Dallas Star

races.race_name: Mervue Equine Supplements Handicap

runners.name: Trishuli River
runners.spotlight: Has a fine record at Dundalk, with four wins and seven places from 18 appearances; 0-9 record on turf is a distinct negative, and never placed.
runners.comment: Has a fine record at Dundalk (four wins, seven places), 0-9 record on turf is a negative

runners.name: Marians Gal
runners.spotlight: Worth considering on her winter AW form; made the frame in four of her five races after a breakthrough win in November; needs to step up on modest turf form; her regular rider claims a valuable 7lb.
runners.comment: Worth considering on her winter AW form, her regular rider claims a valuable 7lb

runners.name: Jurality
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden after 22 starts (six over hurdles); fair fourth over hurdles at Bellewstown last month before another respectable run on the Flat at Limerick; an each-way proposition with the champion jockey aboard.
runners.comment: Remains a maiden after 22 starts (six over hurdles), Limerick fifth offers some hope

runners.name: Amavasya
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; has slipped in the ratings from an opening mark of 67 to 55; made no impact on seasonal debut in a Limerick race in which Jurality was fifth.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden, slipping in the ratings but made no impact at Limerick on seasonal debut

runners.name: Maxwell Smart
runners.spotlight: 1-28 record is a concern; compiled a sequence of seconds; placed seven times in all, including at Sligo 12 days ago; senior rider aboard, with a very capable apprentice on the stable's other runner Eastern Wind; hard to be sure which one will prove best.
runners.comment: 1-28 record is a concern, placed seven times including at Sligo 12 days ago
runners.owner: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Synd'/D O'Leary/J McNally

runners.name: Autocrat
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner; no success from 17 turf attempts; down the field when quite well fancied at Sligo 12 days ago; held by Maxwell Smart on that running.
runners.comment: Six-time AW winner, no success from turf 17 attempts, down the field at Sligo recently

runners.name: Tassarolo
runners.spotlight: Three wins last year, two of them over hurdles; third in a 1m4f Curragh handicap in March; poor over hurdles since then, run may have come too soon.
runners.comment: In rear over hurdles at Cork since third in a 1m4f Curragh handicap in March

runners.name: Eastern Wind
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner, also one win over hurdles; a frequent Leopardstown runner with two wins and three places from 17 visits; fit from AW; could go well with 7lb claimed.
runners.comment: A frequent Leopardstown runner with two wins and three places from 17 visits, fit from AW

runners.name: Masego
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise when fourth in a juvenile hurdle at Listowel last September; two fifth placings over different trips at Gowran last month were mildly encouraging; needs to find more.
runners.comment: Two fifth placings at Gowran last month were mildly encouraging, needs to find more
runners.owner: Barrow Vale Syndicate/A D Mullins/Ms Margaret Mullins

runners.name: Bang Po
runners.spotlight: Nothing of any substance in his form since a 2023 Killarney win; struggling this year on AW; same at Gowran last time.
runners.comment: Nothing of any substance in his form since a 2023 Killarney win, struggling this year

runners.name: Numidia
runners.spotlight: Has a much better record on AW; three-time Dundalk winner, most recently over 2m last November; 1-28 on turf; towards the rear at Gowran last month.
runners.comment: Has a much better record on AW, 1-28 on turf, towards the rear at Gowran last month

runners.name: Church Mountain
runners.spotlight: Winner of nine races; form has been uneven since a 1m4f Dundalk success in January; appears held by Jurality on running at Limerick last time.
runners.comment: Winner of nine races, form has been uneven since a 1m4f Dundalk success in January

runners.name: Creative Dancer
runners.spotlight: Recent Sligo third, on his 16th outing, was his first time in the frame; ground was soft then; his younger rivals have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Recent Sligo third was his first time in the frame, younger rivals have stronger claims

runners.name: Miners Cabin
runners.spotlight: Very modest form in Flat maidens after starting his career over hurdles; transformation needed to feature on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Very modest form in maidens, transformation needed to feature on handicap debut

runners.name: Evening's Empire
runners.spotlight: On a losing run since 1m5f Down Royal win in July 2024; badly out of form (Flat and hurdles) last year.
runners.comment: Won over 1m5f at Down Royal two years ago, badly out of form last year (Flat and hurdles)

runners.name: Shakita
runners.spotlight: Weak form over hurdles when last seen; 50-1 when in rear at Thurles last October on only handicap start on Flat.
runners.comment: Weak form over hurdles when last seen, 50-1 when in rear on only handicap start on Flat

runners.name: Felicity Smoak
runners.spotlight: Poor form over hurdles on winter ground; showed promise when a long-priced second of 18 over 1m2f at Fairyhouse last June; on the same mark now.
runners.comment: Poor form over hurdles on winter ground, showed promise over 1m2f at Fairyhouse last June
runners.owner: E Dillon & B Power & J J Whelan & K R Waplington

runners.name: Voice Of Northeast
runners.spotlight: Placed in a claimer early last season; has failed to make his presence felt in four handicap attempts.
runners.comment: Placed in a claimer, has failed to make his presence felt in four handicap attempts

runners.name: Real Petite
runners.spotlight: Out of her depth in juvenile hurdles last season after an unpromising Flat campaign; little apparent chance unless cheekpieces make a big difference.
runners.comment: Out of her depth in juvenile hurdles last season after an unpromising Flat campaign

runners.name: Vivacious Lady
runners.spotlight: Fair form on AW in 2024, not at best at Dundalk last winter; weak turf credentials; lacks a recent outing.
runners.comment: Fair form on AW in 2024, not at best at Dundalk last winter, weak turf credentials

races.race_name: Women In Racing Lady Riders Handicap

runners.name: Drucker
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a good winter AW campaign, winning twice over this trip, including last month when rallying well to hold off an in-form rival; runs off 12lb lower turf mark here and surely major claims given the form of the yard (and rider).
runners.comment: Recent Dundalk winner runs off 12lb lower turf mark and major claims for in-form yard

runners.name: Jabbar
runners.spotlight: Four turf wins and last won on the AW over 2m in January 2025; beaten a neck off 89 at the Curragh (1m6f, good) last summer, more recently testing ground probably didn't suit at Navan in late March; trip/ground more in his favour here.
runners.comment: Testing ground probably didn't suit at Navan last time; trip/ground more in his favour

runners.name: Star Harbour
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner, on a long losing sequence now, although soft/heavy ground not ideal of late; handicapper relenting so don't rule out with ground more in his favour; rider/trainer had a winner at Roscommon on Monday.
runners.comment: Regressing but handicapper relenting and ground more to suit now

runners.name: Nakasero
runners.spotlight: Three wins have been over shorter trips, including here last summer (1m1f, good); only caught late over this trip on AW over the winter so has to be considered on turf return.
runners.comment: Won here last summer at 1m1f; second over this trip on AW so good prospects of staying
runners.owner: Mark E Phelan & Paul W Phelan

runners.name: Touch The Moon
runners.spotlight: Twice a winner over slightly shorter in Bahrain winter before last; bought for 55,000gns last summer and campaigned over hurdles so far by new connections notably touched off in Navan maiden (yielding), too keen and didn't get home on soft at Cheltenham when last seen in November; yard going well, not one to rule out.
runners.comment: Back from hurdling; yard going well, not one to rule out

runners.name: The Real Screamer
runners.spotlight: Was gaining his third AW win when ready winner over extended 1m2f in January when last seen; career-high mark now and unproven over this longer trip.
runners.comment: AW winner in January on latest; career-high mark now and unproven over this longer trip

runners.name: Autumn Twist
runners.spotlight: Ballinrobe maiden win last summer sandwiched by two hurdle sucesses, all on good ground; form this spring below-best under both codes however and others hold stronger claims.
runners.comment: Form this spring below-best under both codes and others hold stronger claims

runners.name: Soaring Sun
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced 5yo a steady improver on the AW over the winter, getting off the mark over this trip in January under today's rider; 3lb rise looks workable and should go well on turf return; rider/trainer combination won this last year.
runners.comment: Absent since AW win in January under this rider; 3lb higher and likely big player.

runners.name: Derry Lad
runners.spotlight: Four-time Flat winner at up to 1m4f but not for nearly three years; tried over various trips of late without success; handicapper giving him every chance but others preferred.
runners.comment: Long losing run; handicapper giving him every chance but others preferred

runners.name: Poetic Sound
runners.spotlight: Won twice over this trip in 2024 and began last season in form of his life over 1m6f at Navan, touched off in handicap and and fine third in Listed contest; tried over 2m afterwards to no great effect and below form on heavy ground final start; goes well fresh and top-weight respected.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo goes well fresh and top-weight respected

races.race_name: SIS Supporting Irish Racing Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Try Them
runners.spotlight: Flat-bred ran with credit on debut in an academy hurdle; well held when falling at the last at Haydock (2m, soft); creditable 7.5l fifth of 11 at Limerick (2m, soft) next time but didn't improve at Bellewstown last time when fifth; top-rated and leading contender in a weak race.
runners.comment: Flat-bred has posted some positive efforts over hurdles; leading contender in a weak race

runners.name: Fabhcun
runners.spotlight: Best of three maiden hurdle outings came on debut at Gowran on heavy ground in January when fifth; not at same level since; chance if back to his best; never raced on better ground.
runners.comment: Best of three m'den hurdle runs came on debut on hvy; chance of repeating that on better

runners.name: Poet's Duke
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 6-1 on debut at Naas (2m, soft to heavy), and while only seventh it wasn't a run devoid of promise; returned from break when fading into a 6.25l fifth over 2m4f at Wexford for his hurdling debut (good to yielding); could improve for that run and big player if so.
runners.comment: Could improve for return from break on hurdles bow last time and big player if so

runners.name: Diamond Secret
runners.spotlight: Decent sixth of 11 on hurdling bow at Thurles (2m4f, yielding); out of his depth in novice company at Naas next time; respectable efforts, although well beaten in two recent bumpers; tongue tie goes on now; place chance.
runners.comment: Decent sixth on hurdling bow; respectable efforts in bumpers recently; pkace chance

runners.name: Cornaroya Rocco
runners.spotlight: Nothing shown in opening bumper nor in two subsequent hurdle starts; much better at Tramore last time when challenging turning in before fading into a 6l fifth under a claimer; O'Keeffe takes over now.
runners.comment: Nothing shown in three starts until a 6l fifth last time; O'Keefe aboard for first time

runners.name: Lenko
runners.spotlight: Remote sixth on debut at Navan and didn't improve here last time.
runners.comment: Remote sixth on debut at Navan and didn't improve here last time

runners.name: Georgie's Pearl
runners.spotlight: First reserve; never closer than fifth in five bumpers; tailed off on hurdling bow at Navan.
runners.comment: Reserve; never closer than fifth in five bumpers; tailed off on hurdling bow

runners.name: Rosie's Wings
runners.spotlight: Beaten under 20l in his two bumpers and again ran a decent race when a 21l sixth at Roscommon for his hurdling debut last August; makes stable debut now in a moderate race and his middling form puts him thereabouts.
runners.comment: Consistent but middling form in bumpers/m'den; however, that form puts him thereabouts

runners.name: Butterfly Mountain
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much in either point-to-point start; weakening when falling on hurdles debut at Limerick; tailed off at Clonmel last time.
runners.comment: Didn't show much in either point; weakening when fell on hurdles bow and tailed off latest

runners.name: Codger's Rock
runners.spotlight: Slight glimmer of promise when a 35l seventh of 19 on debut at Down Royal at 200-1; still in touch when falling a three out at Fairyhouse; tailed off at Tramore upped to 2m5f last time.
runners.comment: Slight glimmer of promise in first run but not enough to make him appealing as a bet

runners.name: Jolly Potter
runners.spotlight: Moderate French hurdles form in 2023; didn't make the track for Gordon Elliott; tailed off at Naas for belated Irish debut.
runners.comment: Moderate French hurdles form; tailed off on belated Irish debut at Naas

runners.name: Solar Generation
runners.spotlight: Decent pedigree but yet to show it; faded from two out on Gowran debut and no impact at Clonmel last time.
runners.comment: Decent pedigree but yet to show it; no impact in two maiden starts

runners.name: Figero
runners.spotlight: Green when well held in two bumpers over the winter; raced keenly when tailed off for hurdles bow at Clonmel.
runners.comment: Green when well held in both bumpers; keen when tailed on hurdles bow at Clonmel

runners.name: Installment
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; nothing shown in two bumpers nor on hurdling debut last time.
runners.comment: Reserve; nothing shown in two bumpers nor on hurdling debut last time.

runners.name: Bravemans Bounce
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure odds for both maidens and ran like it.
runners.comment: Triple-figure odds for both maidens and ran like it

runners.name: Secret Order
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; weak form in points; triple-figure odds for both maidens, tailed off in first and UR latest.
runners.comment: Reserve; weak form in points; triple-figure odds for both maidens, tailed off and UR

runners.name: Sonny Fella
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden and yet to trouble the judge; failed to complete last two starts since tailed off at Clonmel in December.
runners.comment: 13-race m'den and yet to trouble judge; failed to complete last two starts

races.race_name: BOMAG Construction Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Malbay Madness
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner gained both of his hurdle wins consecutively on better ground at this track last season, including in this race; just 3lb above winning mark and his seasonal return here (2m1f, good to yielding) when an 11l third was encouraging.
runners.comment: Back-to-back wins here last term inc' when 3lb lower in this race; latest run promising

runners.name: Dairy Force
runners.spotlight: C&D maiden hurdle winner for Liam O'Brien (good to yielding); nothing since for this yard but good runs in defeat; runner-up at Downpatrick before falling at the fourth at Down Royal next time; again took the runner-up spot at Bellewstown last time; rising in the weights, but in the mix again.
runners.comment: C&D m'den winner has run well in defeat for new yard; rising in weights but in mix again

runners.name: Must Meet Cecil
runners.spotlight: Five-time winning hurdler hasn't been at his best for some time, but his two runs at this track last autumn were some of his better efforts.
runners.comment: Hasn't been at his best for some time but ran okay here twice last autumn

runners.name: Katherine
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; three-time hurdle winner has been disappointing in Flat/hurdle starts recently; more needed
runners.comment: Reserve; three-time hurdle winner has been disappointing in Flat/hurdle starts recently

runners.name: Metamorpheus
runners.spotlight: First reserve; Punchestown maiden hurdle winner broke his three-year winless streak when successful on the Flat at Sligo 12 days ago (1m3f, yielding to soft); not certain to back it up.
runners.comment: Reserve; broke three-year winless streak with a Flat win last time; not certain to back up

runners.name: Bhean Saibhre
runners.spotlight: Emphatically off the mark over hurdles at the ninth attempt at Ballinrobe in August (2m3f, good); form in behind not worth much; unable to justify favouritism in three Flat runs since then but they were creditable all the same; not discounted.
runners.comment: Emphaticall off the mark in a m'den last Aug'; three creditable Flat runs since then

runners.name: Shinnhill
runners.spotlight: Downpatrick maiden hurdle winner in July 2024; considering he was returning from 552 days off, it wasn't a bad effort seventh of 13 at Thurles when badly hampered by a faller in sole start for Natalia Lupini; returns to original trainer now.
runners.comment: M'den hurdle winner didn't run badly at Thurles considering circumstances

runners.name: Premier Rogue
runners.spotlight: Some promise in bumpers/maidens but offered little on switch to handicap at Leopardstown (14-1).
runners.comment: Some promise in bumpers/m'dens but offered little on h'cap debut

runners.name: Fort Randall
runners.spotlight: Subject of a gamble here last time (2-1 fav') when gaining first win back from a 458-day absence on second start (2m3f, good to yielding) for this stable; up 14lb but it was an easy win so he might be up to going in again.
runners.comment: Returned from 458 days off here last time with easy win; up 14lb but chance again

runners.name: High Notions
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; Fairyhouse maiden hurdle winner on her second start in October (2m4f, yielding); struggled in a Down Royal Grade 3 next time and similarly underwhelming in three handicap starts.
runners.comment: Reserve; m'den hurdle winner has struggled in four starts since inc' three h'caps
runners.owner: I'm Going To No Home Syndicate

runners.name: Kasper Hauser
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles; okay run at Punchestown on handicap debut in January (2,4f, soft to heavy) but his run back there next time was a step backwards; good run back in maiden company at Bellewstown last time when runner-up (2m1f, yielding); chance if building on that run.
runners.comment: Chance if building on runner-up finish in a Bellewstown m'den last time
runners.owner: Trevor Payne/One In A Million Syndicate

runners.name: Mister Vic
runners.spotlight: Returned from a 326-day absence at Ballinrobe last July to make the breakthrough at the seventh time of asking over hurdles (2m6f, good) with easy win; took a mammoth 16lb rise for that and duly struggled at Roscommon in final start for Noel Kelly in August (3m, good to yielding); down 3lb for stable debut.
runners.comment: Returned from absence last summer for easy first win; struggled off new mark next time

runners.name: Centreofattention
runners.spotlight: Debut bumper winner for Nicky Henderson in 2024 (good to soft); fair sixth on stable debut at Galway in October and ran better than finishing position suggested in a rated novice chase at Punchestown; tailed off in a Navan maiden hurdle last time at 80-1 (2m, soft to heavy); check market for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Bumper winner has shown signs of promise over hurdles but tailed off in a m'den last time

runners.name: Long Gone
runners.spotlight: Signed off his tenure with a second hurdles win for Declan Queally when landing a Bellewstown claimer last August (2m5f, good); never travelled when pulled up at Tramore on stable debut in November; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Won a claimer in Aug' but never travelled on stable debut in Nov'; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Desert Cave
runners.spotlight: Well beaten over fences and in point-to-points recently and didn't improve for reversion to hurdles in a Tramore claimer last time; new headgear tried.
runners.comment: Out of form in points/chases and return to hurdles in a claimer didn't help last time

runners.name: Titanium
runners.spotlight: Eight-time Flat winner (all turf wins on soft or worse); small signs of promise five hurdle runs; took third in a Musselburgh maiden hurdle in November (2m, good to soft) but last of 16 on handicap debut at Naas (2m, soft to heavy) when last seen in this sphere; recent Flat runs have been middling.
runners.comment: Eight-time Flat winner; signs of promise over hurdles but recent Flat runs middling
runners.owner: D C Nolan & D P Kilmurray & Mrs S Gavin & K Clarke

runners.name: Dontgowithjoe
runners.spotlight: Signed off a summer campaign last year with back-to-back 2m1f wins on good ground at Ballinrobe and Bellewstown; up 7lb for that latest win; could have more to give, but may need this first run back.
runners.comment: Back-to-back winner last summer when last seen; may need this first run back

races.race_name: CK Interiors Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Springhill Warrior
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in all three maiden hurdle starts and well clear of the field on all metrics; disappointed at 2-5 when last seen in February, but that would be good enough here; only slight concern is that he has never raced on this ground; both tongue tie and cheekpieces go on; declared for 5.20 at Clonmel on Thursday.
runners.comment: Clear of these on all metrics following runner-up finishes in all three m'dens

runners.name: Midnight Musical
runners.spotlight: Maiden has been unable to repeat the form of her runner-up finish in a Galway festival bumper last season, although highly tried at Listed and Grade 2 level; these are calmer waters for her hurdling bow.
runners.comment: Highly tried in bumpers after second at Galway festival; these are calmer waters

runners.name: Facile Ocean
runners.spotlight: Favourite for four-runner bumper on debut in sole start for Tom Mullins but too keen when third; similarly enthusiastic at Ballinrobe in first run for this yard; towards rear throughout at Clonmel last time; tongue tie on for hurdles bow now.
runners.comment: Keen when third of four on bumper debut; well beaten in two runs for this yard

runners.name: Look Sport
runners.spotlight: Purchased for 25,000 as a 3yo; dam French 2m1f hurdle winner; worth a market check; hooded for debut.
runners.comment: Purchased for 25,000 as a 3yo; dam French 2m1f hurdle winner; worth a market check

runners.name: Backinbusiness
runners.spotlight: 95,000euros 3yo; pulled up in first point-to-point but took a 6l third next time; O'Keeffe is an interesting jockey booking.
runners.comment: 95,000euros 3yo; pulled up in first point-to-point but took a 6l third next time

runners.name: Scotty Joe
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 2m3f hurdle winner Thegraveyardbookie; dam unraced sister to 2m4f/2m5f hurdle winner Resolute Bay, out of bumper-winning sister to useful 2m6f-3m hurdle/chase winner The Sliotar; led close to home at Ballyvodock in January and runner-up won twice afterwards.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 2m3f hurdle winner; led close to home in third point start in Jan'

runners.name: Carajillo
runners.spotlight: First reserve;  dam placed 6f AW (RPR 55), half-sister to 10.6f/1m4f Flat and 2m hurdle winner One For Joules, out of 1m3f/1m4f winning half-sister to winners Orpington (1m6f Listed), Good Of Luck (1m2f Flat and 2m/2m1f hurdle) and Whoopsie (1m4f-2m); best watched
runners.comment: Reserve; dam placed 6f AW (RPR 55); best watched

runners.name: Capri Gear
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; dam well beaten in bumper, half-sister to winners Red House Hill (bumper/11.3f Flat), All Clenched Up (bumper/2m hurdle) and Sand Fly (bumper/2m4f hurdle), out of bumper and useful 2m-3m hurdle/chase winner.
runners.comment: Reserve; dam well beaten in bumper, half-sister to three winners

runners.name: Joe Cool
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; runner-up last twice in Flat maidens at the Curragh and Bellewstown on heavy/soft; player if getting a run.
runners.comment: Reserve; runner-up in Flat m'dens last twice; player of getting a run

runners.name: Majestic Mayhem
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on bumper debut here last month; tremendous improvement required now over hurdles.
runners.comment: Tailed off on bumper debut here last month; tremendous improvement required

runners.name: Seekthisfairland
runners.spotlight: Last of 12 and tailed off in a 2m4 Wexford maiden hurdle on debut; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Last of 12 and tailed off in a Wexford maiden hurdle on debut; hard to fancy
runners.owner: Kevin Sexton & James McIlwee & James Convery & M G Byrne

runners.name: Colesberg
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten at huge odds in three maiden hurdle runs; others more appealing.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten at huge odds in three maiden hurdle runs; others more appealing

runners.name: Beacon Bypass
runners.spotlight: Beaten 39l in sole point-to-point and 56l on bumper debut; that form won't be enough.
runners.comment: Beaten 39l in sole point-to-point and 56l on bumper debut; that form won't be enough

runners.name: Me Wee Nanny Fran
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three maiden hurdle starts, the latest at 80-1; in good hands but best watched.
runners.comment: Down the field in three maiden hurdle starts; in good hands but best watched

runners.name: The Poet's Way
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in three maiden runs to date; hood on now.
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential in three maiden runs to date; hood on now

runners.name: Dorie Miller
runners.spotlight: Competed in quality bumper company for Noel Meade, notably in races won by The Wager and Boycetown who have contested Grade 1s; tailed off in three bumpers for this stable.
runners.comment: Competed in quality bumper company for Noel Meade; tailed off in last three bumpers

runners.name: Lanzhou
runners.spotlight: Leap of faith required to back him after four moderate runs in maiden hurdles.
runners.comment: Leap of faith required to back him after four moderate runs in maiden hurdles

runners.name: Casual Water
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to top-class Altior and several other smart performers; maybe telling that his debut last month came over 3m at Limerick when tailed off and shorter trip/better ground didn't improve him at Sligo; not a contender on form to date.
runners.comment: Half-brother to Altior has been tailed off in his opening two maiden runs

runners.name: Not So Serious
runners.spotlight: Midfield finishes in both bumpers for Andrew Slattery (beaten at 5-2 on both occasions); tailed off in both hurdling starts for new yard.
runners.comment: Okay runs in bumpers for former yard; tailed off twice over hurdles for new stable

races.race_name: Winning Post Service Station Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Faceman
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins last year, a maiden here (good) and a three-runner novice event at Tramore; not disgraced in a Cork Listed novice hurdle in November; fell at five out on chasing debut at Tramore last month; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Two hurdle wins last year inc' here; fell at five out on chasing debut; not dismissed

runners.name: Ad Caelum
runners.spotlight: Four wins over hurdles including in this race last year off 110 (good); recent form over fences not too encouraging but she's better over timber.
runners.comment: Four wins over hurdles inc' this race last year; recent runs over fences not encouraging

runners.name: Mystic Malina
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; won a weak Clonmel maiden on her second hurdles start and relished the step up to 3m to land a Cork handicap in March, appreciating better ground under a hold-up ride to win going away; ground possibly on the soft side but still a positive run when seventh of 20 in a Fairyhouse Listed contest (3m, yielding to soft) last time; player.
runners.comment: Won a 3m h'cap and positive run when ground softer in a Listed constest last time

runners.name: Mordor
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner was last seen over timber finishing eighth of 24 in the 2025 Martin Pipe (2m5f, good to soft); kept on Flat since then and added a third win in that sphere when successful at Navan last time (1m6f, heavy); most of his wins came on good ground, so leading contender back over obstacles fit and in form.
runners.comment: Dual winning hurdler is fit and in form after winning on Flat last time; leading contender

runners.name: Zam Bobbler
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 3yo was having only third start when landing ordinary 2m Wexford maiden in March, coming home strongly to beat a joint-favourite (pair clear); kept on for decent fourth of 20 on handicap debut at Fairyhouse (2m4f, yielding to soft); this further trip may get more from him.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 3yo won a Wexford m'den and form suggests this trip may get more from him
runners.owner: Paul Dillon/Pearse Mehigan/Michael Hannon/P J Rothwell

runners.name: Ringdufferin
runners.spotlight: Two-time hurdles winner for Gordon Elliott in 2023; out of form for former yard, but there were definite signs of life pn stable debut when fourth of 12 over fences at Tramore, making a bad mistake at the fifth; that is something to build on back over timber now.
runners.comment: Definite signs of life in stable debut last time; something to build on back over timber

runners.name: Ceanndana
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner under rules (three over hurdles); took second behind progressive rival on return from 87-day break at Punchestown in October (2m7f, good to yielding); another positive run when third over fences last time at Down Royal (3m, good to yielding); consistent sort but there may be better handicapped contenders.
runners.comment: Consistent sort who has placed last two runs but mark doesn't underestimate him

runners.name: Ottoman Style
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back 2m4f handicap hurdles on testing ground in early 2023; struggled in a light campaign in 2024-2025 and pulled up in sole run last season; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Lightly campaigned since dual 2023 wins, but poor in what he has shown

runners.name: Digby
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdles winner had been expected (9-2) to leave recent poor form behind upong encountering better ground at Down Royal over fences 11 days ago but the revival never arrived; questions to answer now.
runners.comment: Expected revival upon encountering better ground didn't arrive last time; questions now

races.race_name: Cooke's Bar And Restaurant Caragh Beginners Chase

runners.name: In The Trenches
runners.spotlight: A long-standing 32-race maiden who has proved frustrating but consistent, finishing runner-up 13 times; 12 chase runs, beaten 5l into fifth at Wexford eight days ago in handicap off 112; one of his best efforts came over this C&D last year; capable of winning a contest of this nature.
runners.comment: Long-standing maiden; good run when fifth at Wexford in h'cap chase lto; should go close

runners.name: Shellrunforbriggs
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over hurdles, landed handicap 29 days ago off 102 decisively by 5.5l; two runs over fences, distant fourth in good beginners' chase at Thurles before runner-up in Listed mares company, albeit well held; big run anticipated back up in trip.
runners.comment: Second in Listed mares company at Thurles in Feb; won h'cap hurdle off 102 lto; big chance

runners.name: Black Soul
runners.spotlight: Point winner whose sole win in 23 starts under rules came in a Down Royal maiden hurdle last May; not shown much this year until chase debut at Tramore last time, falling two out when still in contention (Drop A Threat fourth); should build on that.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner last year, encouraging start over fences lto before falling two out

runners.name: Kate Knows Best
runners.spotlight: Sligo maiden hurdle winner last year (2m2f, good); fine effort when beaten 9.5l into third by the smart I'll Sort That at Galway after; didn't make it past the first on chase debut at Clonmel 164 days ago; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Decent hurdles form in 2025 on good; fell at first on chase debut 164 days ago; ew chance

runners.name: Rockstown Girl
