runners.spotlight: Gained third hurdles victory when landing handicap off 100 at Down Royal in March; good effort off same mark over fences when 3.5l second to Butty O Brien at Wexford 35 days ago; needs improvement back in a beginners', but in good form.
runners.comment: Won h'cap hurdle in March; good effort over fences second at Wexford lto; needs more

runners.name: Juke Hill
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; ran to just a fair standard in three maiden hurdles; seemingly unsuited by heavy ground when beaten favourite on handicap debut at Thurles 106 days ago; chase debut.
runners.comment: Second reserve; three okay runs in maiden hurdles; unsuited by heavy on h'cap debut lto

runners.name: Phoenix Arizona
runners.spotlight: Placed in two small-field maiden hurdles last year; beaten a long way in point-to-point last month, but fared better when sixth here last time; more needed on chase debut up in trip.
runners.comment: Encouraging run here in maiden hurdle 21 days ago; needs much more on chase debut, however

runners.name: Warriors Wood
runners.spotlight: Relished good ground last summer when winning over hurdles at this course and Ballinrobe and ran well in handicap hurdle at Downpatrick off 110; unseated when in contention three out at Tramore 25 days ago on chase debut; definite chance again for in-form trainer.
runners.comment: Relishes good ground, won twice last summer; unseated on chase debut lto, running well

runners.name: Following Orders
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden over hurdles but some good efforts in defeat last year in maidens, most notably when second at Roscommon in August; returned from break at Bellewstown 27 days ago, pulled up before last; needs improvement on chase debut up in trip, but not out of it on pick of form.
runners.comment: Some good efforts in maiden hurdles in 2025; pulled up on return 27 days ago; chase debut

runners.name: Gunnery Sergeant
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two maiden hurdles this year, the form of which is questionable; ran okay when fifth on chase debut at Tramore 25 days ago, 4.75l behind Drop A Threat; needs a lot more but lightly raced.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two maiden hurdles; ran just ok on chase debut lto; needs plenty more

runners.name: Strange Currency
runners.spotlight: Standout effort in six starts under rules came in Punchestown maiden hurdle in February when beaten 15l into third by Ksar Fatal (form working out well); didn't build on that in three starts after, but ran better when well held in fifth at the same track last month; can be involved if rediscovering best form now over fences.
runners.comment: Form of Punchestown third in Feb working out well; in-and-out since but can be thereabouts

runners.name: Drop A Threat
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin point winner who hasn't fulfilled that potential in seven starts, six of them over hurdles; fourth on chase debut at Tramore 25 days ago, arguably flattered; similar standard race here down in trip; likely to come good at some point for top connections, but becoming expensive to follow.
runners.comment: Point winner but disappointing in seven starts; fourth on chase debut lto; needs more

runners.name: Quiet In Bresil
runners.spotlight: First reserve; two okay runs in maden hurdles either side of a poor effort this year; needs plenty more to figure on chase debut.
runners.comment: First reserve; two okay runs in maden hurdles either side of a poor effort this year

runners.name: Better Times
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in 2m6f handicap hurdle at Downpatrick in March, decent effort off 5lb higher at Clonmel next time before unseating after three out 16 days ago at Punchestown; enjoys good ground and being patiently ridden; chase debut; has the hurdle rating to be on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Downpatrick h'cap hurdle winner in March; unseated early lto; chase debut; not out of it

runners.name: Leni's Law
runners.spotlight: Acquitted himself well in three maiden hurdles earlier this year but cut out quickly on handicap debut 57 days ago, finishing tailed off; needs to bounce back, but early days still on chase debut.
runners.comment: Ran below par on h'cap debut 57 days ago, cut out quickly; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Driveonwill
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; 13-race maiden, ran a few decent races when in the frame in handicaps this year; unseated at fourth on chase debut seven days ago; would need a big step forward.
runners.comment: Third reserve; in the frame in h'cap hurdles this year; unseated on chase debut lto

runners.name: Jerisk Star
runners.spotlight: Winner of handicap hurdle at Gowran Park in October off 94; held when unseated last on chase debut in November; again failed to complete when fell two out at Tramore next time; needs improvement but not discounted after a 171-day layoff.
runners.comment: H'cap hurdle winner last year; in process of running ok on both chase runs; off 171 days

races.race_name: Shanette Superior Space Handicap Chase

runners.name: Cave Court
runners.spotlight: Two-time chase winner; career-best when second off 131 at Cartmel last June; well beaten four times since; pulled up over longer trip at Fairyhouse 39 days ago; down in trip and dropped 2lb.
runners.comment: Fine runner-up effort at Cartmel last June; below form since, down in trip; needs more

runners.name: Littlefoot
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner who ran a cracker when second off 120 in handicap hurdle at Tipperary in October (2m, good); form tailed off then but relished the switch to fences when scoring at Downpatrick in March, beating Dee's Lady by 4.25l, below that when fifth behind Coral River but will appreciate this sound surface.
runners.comment: Relished switch to fences when winning at Downpatrick; below form lto but likely improver

runners.name: Three In A Row
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 19 starts came in Naas handicap hurdle in April 2024; 0-12 over fences but placed five times and produced another solid effort when 0.5l second to James The Brave at Wexford 35 days ago; nudged up 2lb.
runners.comment: Consistent 12-race maiden over fences; solid second at Wexford 35 days ago; up 2lb

runners.name: Marv Michael
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner for Henry de Bromhead, including when bolting up by 12l over C&D last May; finished last season with two below-par runs; off 230 days and dropped 4lb on stable debut.
runners.comment: Four-time winner for Henry de Bromhead, below form last twice; off 230 days; stable debut

runners.name: Nas Na Riogh
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner over hurdles; 0-10 over fences, best runs when third in pair of handicaps at Wexford and Galway last summer; struggled since but not beaten far when sixth at Tramore 26 days ago and return to good ground will suit.
runners.comment: Two-time hurdle winner, 0-10 over fences; positive run lto; return to gd ground will suit

runners.name: Tell Us This
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; won claiming hurdle by 10l last time out; lenient mark over fences back on good ground.
runners.comment: Third reserve; won claiming hurdle by 10l lto; lenient mark over fences back on gd ground

runners.name: Whateys Quest
runners.spotlight: First reserve; sole win over fences came at Limerick (2m6f, yielding) in December 2024; ran well over hurdles off 2lb higher mark at Limerick on penultimate start; well held at Punchestown 124 days ago but badly hampered; chance after 124 days off.
runners.comment: First reserve; 1-13 over fences; better than result lto having been hampered; off 124 days

runners.name: Champagne Mahler
runners.spotlight: Sole win over fences came in valuable Gowran Park handicap in March 2025 (2m, soft); good second at Naas in January in similar conditions; tailed-off fourth 39 days ago at Cork; up in trip, probably needs softer ground.
runners.comment: Best efforts have come over shorter and on softer ground; not discounted over longer trip

runners.name: Gaelic Arc
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner, twice over fences; enjoyed a productive summer last year, scoring over C&D in June before finishing a nose second in valuable event in Killarney; too much on his plate backend of last year and returns on an okay mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner in June; not enjoyed a productive winter but mark has slid for return
runners.owner: Mark R D Fleming / T Westropp Bennett

runners.name: Grange Walk
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; four-time chase winner, out of form on soft the last year; needs revival.
runners.comment: Second reserve; four-time chase winner, out of form on soft the last year; needs revival

runners.name: A Wave Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: A Grade 1-winning juvenile hurdler in his younger days, far from the force of old but still well capable; latest win came in handicap chase over C&D in September; down the field at Leopardstown and in veterans' event at Wexford last twice; dropped 3lb; 3-6 at this track and each-way player.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner; below form last twice; dropped 3lb and claimer takes back over

runners.name: Torquay Road
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner who has fared well in three starts over fences, beaten a neck by Kilbrin Rocco at Tramore 26 days ago; step up in trip and ground should suit.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdle winner, taken to fences well; second at Tramore 26 days ago

runners.name: Lisleigh Lad
runners.spotlight: Two-time chase winner, encouraging run when beaten 0.5l behind Harbour Highway at Leopardstown (2m6f, yielding) in March before well-held fourth at Fairyhouse 41 days ago; ground should suit and place claims.
runners.comment: Good run when second at Leopardstown, similar effort lto; ground will suit, place claims

runners.name: Park That
runners.spotlight: Won maiden hurdle at this track last year; well held in two beginners' chases before faring better on handicap debut when third at Naas in January; followed that up at Navan last time when 2.5l second to Moudan, who went on to win valuable contest at Ayr since; up just 1lb and steps up in trip; leading player for in-form trainer.
runners.comment: Good second to subsequent Ayr scorer Moudan lto; up 1lb and steps up in trip; big player

runners.name: Milan Forth
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner under rules, two hunter chases and a handicap chase at Cork off 116 in January; beaten favourite at Gowran Park in March, seemingly unsuited by testing conditions; ran well when beaten 9l over hurdles at Punchestown 16 days ago; definite chance back over fences with ground to suit.
runners.comment: Three-time chase winner; ran well over hurdles at Punchestown lto; big chance

runners.name: Pray Tell
runners.spotlight: Won maiden hurdle at this course in September 2024, went on to score at Grade 3 level; encouraging start to chasing career when second to Argento Boy at Punchestown in December; below that last time out when sixth here; down in trip and tried in cheekpieces on handicap chase debut off 3lb lower mark than over hurdles.
runners.comment: Course winner; took to fences well, second at Punchestown; below that lto; h'cap debut

runners.name: Tonaghneave Well
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler, latest win came off 124 in handicap at Downpatrick in June; nothing of real note in five chase starts since, well beaten over 3m at Down Royal 195 days ago; needs a lot more after break, but hasn't been out of the first three in as many starts here.
runners.comment: Course winner; useful hurdler but hasn't matched that form over fences; 196 days off

races.race_name: Racing Again May 31st (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Returning Officer
runners.spotlight: No Risk At All gelding who cost 100,000euros as 3yo; shaped nicely when beaten 8l into sixth in strong Navan bumper 460 days ago, the form of which has worked out well; leading player if ready to go after long lay-off.
runners.comment: Good debut when sixth in strong race at Navan; off 460 days; leading player if ready

runners.name: Little Ginger
runners.spotlight: Well beat on debut at Punchestown in February but took a step forward at Limerick 29 days ago, beaten 5.5l into fourth by Sparkling Wit, keeping on well; needs more but trip will suit and can progress again.
runners.comment: Improved from debut to be beaten 5.5l into fourth at Limerick lto, keeping on well; chance

runners.name: Treadonmydreams
runners.spotlight: Yeats mare who hinted at ability when fourth in small field on debut at Naas; tailed off in Graded race at Punchestown; drops in class and a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Okay debut when fourth at Naas but tailed off lto; bit to prove but drops in class

runners.name: Grand Karak
runners.spotlight: Karaktar gelding who cost 4,000Euros as a foal; half-brother to French winners Grand Akbar (prolific 1m4f-2m) and Grand Beau Fort (2m1f); dam lightly raced, second at 11.5f France, half-sister to French 10.5f-1m6f winner Grand Beau Temps; tough ask on debut.
runners.comment: Cost 4,000Euros foal; Karaktar gelding; half-brother to two French winners (1m4f-2m1f)

runners.name: Jurassic Bob
runners.spotlight: Sholokhov gelding; dam bumper and useful 2m2f-3m hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to useful winners Brackloon High (bumper and 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase) and Native Robin (2m3f-2m5f chase), out of bumper winner; second in point-to-point in October and makes debut under rules.
runners.comment: Sholokhov gelding; dam bumper and useful 2m2f-3m hurdle/chase winner; second in ptp in Oct

runners.name: Minella Cyclone
runners.spotlight: Harzand gelding cost 62,000euros as 3yo; dam dual point winner, sister to winners Glenwood Prince (2m7f chase) and Royal Moll (bumper/2m hurdle), out of unraced half-sister to smart 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Calling Brave and smart staying chaser Ottowa; yard gets winners in this sphere and market check essential.
runners.comment: Cost 62,000euros as a 3yo; unseated in ptp 20 days ago; yard gets bumper winners

runners.name: Jack Owen Jack
runners.spotlight: Jukebox Jury gelding; half-brother to two winners on the Flat, including the smart Rubaiyat; made a very encouraging debut when beaten 2l by the useful Minella Emperor at Cork 215 days ago, travelling well and pulling clear with the winner; big player on that run.
runners.comment: Second on debut at Cork 215 days ago; pulled clear with useful winner; likely player

runners.name: World As One
runners.spotlight: Waldgeist gelding, half-brother to the smart Colonel Mustard; green and shaped well on debut when beaten 6.5l into second by High Kick Kev at Clonmel 43 days ago; hard to gauge the form but should improve plenty and good ground likely to suit even better.
runners.comment: Beaten odds-on fav when second at Clonmel 43 days ago; should improve for leading yard

runners.name: Tea Biscuit
runners.spotlight: Had a tough ask on rules debut in a competitive maiden hurdle at Gowran Park 90 days ago but acquitted himself well when beaten 23l into fourth, shaping better than the result having been keen; definite claims switched to a bumper for a small yard who has been excelling in this sphere with three winners from six runners last season.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut when fourth in maiden hurdle 90 days ago; chance now switched to bumper

runners.name: Suresuresure
runners.spotlight: Has shown nothing of note in four starts in bumpers; difficult to make a case for.
runners.comment: Has shown nothing of note in four starts in bumpers; difficult to make a case for

runners.name: Last Apache
runners.spotlight: Berkshire gelding who didn't make much of an impression when well beat into sixth on racecourse bow at Navan 55 days ago; bred to appreciate this longer trip, but needs a big step forward.
runners.comment: No impression when sixth on racecourse bow at Navan 55 days ago; needs big improvement

runners.name: S T Hamo
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much when down the field on debut at Naas 151 days ago; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Didn't show much when down the field on debut at Naas 151 days ago; hard to fancy

races.race_name: Huws Gray Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Classy Clarets
runners.spotlight: Versatile sort whose form since March comprises a win over 6f on AW and creditable third-place finishes over 5f/7f, latest at Musselburgh returned to turf; 0-17 in this sphere, however.
runners.comment: Versatile and in form but is 0-17 on turf; ran creditably at Musselburgh last time

runners.name: Wee Mary
runners.spotlight: AW winner; 0-9 on turf but has largely consistent form in handicaps in this sphere, placed at Thirsk and Ayr the last twice; again likely to give her running.
runners.comment: 0-9 on turf but has largely consistent form in handicaps in this sphere

runners.name: Thornaby Pearl
runners.spotlight: Suited by ground softer than good; five-time winner, most recently off current mark over C&D last August; ran respectably at Doncaster on reappearance; in the mix.
runners.comment: Five-time winner, most recently off current mark over C&D last August; in the mix

runners.name: Ski Angel
runners.spotlight: Has gained all wins in Scotland, including over C&D; respectable fourth at Ayr on seasonal debut; it's still two years since she scored away from Musselburgh, however.
runners.comment: All wins in Scotland but it's still two years since she last won away from Musselburgh

runners.name: Beerwah
runners.spotlight: Prevailed at Redcar (5f, good to firm) most recently, building on reappearance effort and taking record in blinkers to 2-6; may have more to offer in the retained headgear; respected.
runners.comment: Redcar success took his record in blinkers to 2-6; respected in the retained headgear

runners.name: Until Dawn
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner who finished 1l third over C&D (good) most recently, posting her best turf effort; place possibilities off same mark.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner; finished 1l third over C&D last time, posting her best turf effort
runners.owner: F Reay, S McMillan & S Pedersen

runners.name: Thunderstorm Katie
runners.spotlight: Scored five times, including over C&D, last year; made some late headway back here last time on return from break and may be back in top form with that outing under her belt.
runners.comment: May be back in top form with recent C&D effort (back from break) under her belt

runners.name: Kyber Crystal
runners.spotlight: Respectable third to Beerwah at Redcar on seasonal debut; back on last winning mark and has form over C&D, notably a 2024 success; difficult to dismiss.
runners.comment: Respectable third to Beerwah at Redcar on reappearance; back on last winning mark

runners.name: Tarlac
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly over 6f, winning once (off 8lb higher); however, attractively weighted and has possibilities if taking well to first-time blinkers, combined with new trip.
runners.comment: Possibilities if taking well to first-time blinkers, combined with new trip; on handy mark
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Sixcor
runners.spotlight: Registered most recent success in C&D event last July; not in the same form back here on reappearance but perhaps needed the run.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last July; not in same form back here on seasonal debut

races.race_name: British EBF Supporting Racing Welfare #mentalhealthawarenessweek Restricted Maiden (GBB/IRE Inc')

runners.name: Ricky Punting
runners.spotlight: From a speedy Italian family; sluggish in early stages but kept on pretty well for fourth at Bath (5.7f, firm) 16 days ago; should build on that performance here.
runners.comment: Sluggish in early stages but kept on pretty well for fourth at Bath last month

runners.name: Wild Terrain
runners.spotlight: £50,000 yearling; second foal; dam unplaced 5.5f-8.5f in France, half-sister to winners Chameur (French 1m Listed), Only The Brave (German 1m2f Group 3) and Cashman (German 9.5f Listed); stable's 2yos have made a good start to the season.
runners.comment: Second foal from a well-connected mare; stable's 2yos doing well this season

runners.name: Harley
runners.spotlight: £22,000 yearling; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Bomb Proof (5f including 2yo; RPR 95), Beauty By My Side (7f 2yo/1m AW; 79) and Astimegoesby (Spanish 6f-1m); dam French 1m2f winner, half-sister to 1m Group 3 winner Spoil The Fun; stable's first 2yo runner this season.
runners.comment: £22,000 yearling; half-brother to two 2yo winners; stable's first 2yo runner this season

runners.name: North Star Kodi
runners.spotlight: £30,000 yearling; fourth foal; brother to 1m 2yo winner Bearin Up (RPR 72), half-brother to Qatari 7f winner Acclaim To Royalty; dam unraced half-sister to winners Rare Ransom (Group-placed 7f/1m) and Ransomed Bride (Listed-placed 1m2f/10.6f); market instructive.
runners.comment: £30,000 yearling; brother to a fair 1m 2yo winner; market instructive

runners.name: Waasil
runners.spotlight: 70,000euros yearling; 12th foal; brother to 6f 2yo winner Lady Of Andros (RPR 93), half-brother to four winners including Mitraad (7f 2yo/1m; 107) and Nisreen (1m-1m2f including Listed; 106); dam 6f/7f winner (81), closely related to 9.7f Listed winner Haatheq; appeals on paper.
runners.comment: Brother to a useful 6f 2yo winner; with good stable; appeals on paper

runners.name: Boutblummintime
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 8l when sixth of 15 in the Brocklesby (5f, good to soft) at Doncaster in March, but was denied a clear run there and ought to have learnt from the experience.
runners.comment: Beaten about 8l when sixth in the Brocklesby but ought to have learnt from that experience

races.race_name: Prime Secure William Wallace Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Gaelic Approach
runners.spotlight: Improved form sent handicapping over 1m4f for both starts this term, taking second at Wolverhampton (AW) and getting off the mark at Leicester (good); both were in small fields but the former race has had its form boosted substantially and the latter ended up as a clearcut win after he went on over 2f out; latest 3lb rise probably underestimates him.
runners.comment: Improved in 1m4f handicaps this term and latest 3lb rise probably underestimates him
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xxiii & M Hulin

runners.name: Harriet Quimby
runners.spotlight: Two minor honours over about 1m last October, before battling her way to a novice win at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) five weeks ago; the latter form has taken some knocks and improvement is needed on this handicap debut, but her pedigree (dam won the Oaks) reinforces the already strong impression that 1m3f+ will suit.
runners.comment: Won 1m2f Yarmouth novice; pedigree and performance suggest strongly that 1m3f+ will suit

runners.name: My Ballyquinn
runners.spotlight: Went close at Thirsk (1m, good) on reappearance; failed to improve on that when fourth of six at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) in another novice event three weeks later, but he still looks capable of better and his dam was a 2m4f winner, so this extra 3f for handicap debut will surely help.
runners.comment: His dam was a 2m4f winner, so this extra 3f for handicap debut will surely help

races.race_name: Precon Braveheart Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Humble Spark
runners.spotlight: Held up; exposed 6yo who is 6-27 on AW surfaces but 1-13 on turf; however, that solitary turf win was over C&D (2024) and he wasn't far away back on turf for last three outings; case can be made.
runners.comment: Solitary turf win was C&D (2024) and he wasn't far away back on turf for last three starts

runners.name: Elsass
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent in his seven handicaps but he's won two of them; gelded before his successful return at Southwell (1m3f, AW) three weeks ago, when asserting by about 2l in six-runner race; 4lb higher today but probably okay on turf and has to be considered seriously.
runners.comment: Inconsistent in his seven handicaps but he's won two of them, including 2026 reappearance

runners.name: Urban Road
runners.spotlight: Often slowly away; last won in February 2025, with 14 races since; eight of nine wins were on AW, but easily best turf form came on last two starts (minor honours over 1m2f at Redcar) which gives hope; 2lb out of handicap.
runners.comment: Eight of nine wins were on AW but easily best turf form came on latest two outings
runners.owner: S McMillan, F Reay & A D Brown

runners.name: Antrim
runners.spotlight: Often a front-runner; won at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in October and December and at Meydan (1m3f, dirt) in January; merely mid-division back in Britain at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) six weeks ago, but that was a hot race; turf form (first three starts) is nowhere near his handicap mark.
runners.comment: Three wins October-January; turf form (first three runs) is nowhere near his handicap mark

runners.name: Ouro Preto
runners.spotlight: 3-15 when trained in France, the latest win in a claimer at Longchamp (1m4f, very soft) last September; well handicapped judged on peak French efforts and the market should be instructive, including against a stablemate.
runners.comment: 3-15 trained in France; well handicapped on peak efforts; market should be instructive

runners.name: Tiernan
runners.spotlight: Five races, all last term and ridden by Paul Mulrennan in four; easily best form when he came from last to be a close third of seven in Listed event at Hamilton (1m3f, good; 150-1) in July; 25-1 and tongue tied when tailed off in the November Handicap at Doncaster (1m4f, heavy) and he's also now had wind surgery; this is just his second handicap and there's a strong sense of unfinished business.
runners.comment: 0-5 but came from last to be a close 3rd of seven in C&D Listed event; unfinished business

runners.name: Majestic
runners.spotlight: Went close when returned to Britain at Goodwood (1m4f, good to firm) last August but towards the rear on all four starts since, latest when back on good ground 13 days ago; coming down the weights but best watched at present.
runners.comment: Went close at Goodwood (1m4f) last August but towards the rear on all four starts since

races.race_name: Luddon Construction Handicap

runners.name: Austrian Theory
runners.spotlight: On a losing run but he wasn't disgraced on his reappearance when fifth of ten in 1m2f Ripon handicap 29 days ago; this course winner can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on reappearance with Ripon fifth; this course winner can't be ruled out

runners.name: Sanafi Zabeel
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced ex-German 9f winner; off 11 months and not disgraced for his new yard when running on late into fifth in 1m handicap here 12 days ago; can build on it now.
runners.comment: Off 11 months and not disgraced for new yard when fifth here 12 days ago; can build on it

runners.name: Obito
runners.spotlight: A dual winner in 2025 (including here) who also finished runner-up three times in the autumn; yard is going well so he must enter calculations on his return.
runners.comment: Dual winner in 2025 (including here); yard going well so must enter calculations on return

runners.name: Qitaal
runners.spotlight: It's now 15 starts since his last victory and he was tried in cheekpieces when a below-par seventh in 1m2f handicap at Nottingham last month; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: It's now 15 starts since his last victory and he needs to take a big step forward

runners.name: Draupnir
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage son of Golden Horn who got off the mark at Thirsk (7f) in September; resumed from seven months off with a good third of 13 in another 7f handicap there 27 days ago; could do better still back up in trip with a hood fitted and rates a player.
runners.comment: Good third at Thirsk on return; back up in trip with hood on now and he rates a player

runners.name: Tee Aitch Aye
runners.spotlight: Gained his first win over C&D in September; has failed to last home over 1m4f on his last two runs, coming in last of eight at Newcastle in March, and not totally dismissed back in trip.
runners.comment: C&D winner; failed to last home at 1m4f last two runs; not totally dismissed back in trip

runners.name: Flying Fletcher
runners.spotlight: Ended a long losing sequence at Newcastle (1m) in January and largely in good form since, set a lot to do when fifth of 13 at Musselburgh (1m) 19 days ago; considered.
runners.comment: Ended long losing run at Newcastle in January and largely in good form since; considered

runners.name: Salam Dubawi
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m winner in France in 2025; looked rusty after seven months off for his new yard when last of 11 in 7f Musselburgh handicap 19 days ago and can take a step forward here back up in trip.
runners.comment: Looked rusty when last at Musselburgh 19 days ago; can take a step forward back up in trip

runners.name: Quiet Resolve
runners.spotlight: Made light of an eight-month absence for his new yard with victory in 1m handicap here 12 days ago; not taken lightly under a 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: Off eight months, winning start for new yard here latest; not taken lightly under 5lb pen

runners.name: Naturalia
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with a good 1m4f Catterick second but only ninth of 12 in 1m1f handicap at Musselburgh 19 days ago; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Only ninth of 12 in 1m1f handicap at Musselburgh 19 days ago; she needs to bounce back

races.race_name: Patersons Of Greenoakhill Handicap

runners.name: Welbury
runners.spotlight: Followed a stylish 1m Southwell novice win in March with an excellent second on his handicap debut at Doncaster (1m) three weeks ago, despite going freely; cheekpieces added now and capable of further progress; big shout.
runners.comment: Very good second on h'cap debut at Doncaster; cheekpieces on and better to come; big shout

runners.name: Saxophonist
runners.spotlight: Gelded and off six months before posting an improved third of seven on his handicap debut at Doncaster (1m) three weeks ago; can do better still, so can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Improved third at Doncaster three weeks ago; can do better still, so can't be ruled out

runners.name: Delinquent
runners.spotlight: Landed 1m Southwell handicap in April; only sixth under an aggressive ride at Haydock (1m) six days ago but no surprise to see him bounce back with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell; sixth at Haydock since but ridden aggressively there and can bounce back

runners.name: Unchartedterritory
runners.spotlight: Has been brought along steadily, going freely when fourth of eight in 1m Wetherby novice 19 days ago; looks to have more to offer now going into handicaps.
runners.comment: Brought along steadily, fourth at Wetherby 19 days ago; capable of better on h'cap debut

runners.name: Picture Palace
runners.spotlight: Son of Pinatubo who took a big step forward on his turf debut when third of eight in 1m Wetherby novice 19 days ago; he may do better still now going handicapping.
runners.comment: Big step forward when third at Wetherby 19 days ago; may do better still now handicapping

runners.name: Parole Officer
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m juvenile maiden for Sean Woods; beat only one for his new yard on his return in 1m Musselburgh handicap though; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Beat only one for new yard on return in 1m Musselburgh handicap; others appeal more

runners.name: Born A Star
runners.spotlight: A fair 7f juvenile scorer but yet to build on that in 2026, very free when only sixth of seven in 7f Wolverhampton handicap last month; more is required.
runners.comment: A fair 7f juvenile scorer but yet to build on that in 2026; more is required

runners.name: Deep Sleep
runners.spotlight: Signed off as 2yo with a good 7f Catterick fourth but he came in a well-held 12th in 7f Thirsk maiden on his return last month; lots more needed on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well-held 12th at Thirsk on his return last month; lots more needed on his handicap debut

runners.name: Angela's Baar
runners.spotlight: A promising debut 6f second at Pontefract last June but has failed to build on it, fifth of nine in 6f Wolverhampton maiden in February; this switch to handicaps needs to spark improvement.
runners.comment: Below-par fifth at Wolverhampton in February; switch to h'caps needs to spark progress

runners.name: Horu Kanu
runners.spotlight: Headstrong sort but he's progressing with experience and posted a very good fifth of nine on his first go in handicap company at Doncaster (1m) 13 days ago; not out of things.
runners.comment: Headstrong but very good fifth at Doncaster latest; not out of things

races.race_name: Molton Brown At Hamilton Park Handicap

runners.name: Hashtagnotions
runners.spotlight: Scored twice (around 1m) at Ayr and Beverley last summer; back in decent nick in 2026, fifth in 8.5f Beverley handicap 22 days ago; ought to be in the shake-up again from an easing mark.
runners.comment: In decent nick, fifth at Beverley 22 days ago; ought to be in the shake-up again

runners.name: Uncle Liam
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 in excellent form when runner-up on his last three runs; shaped encouragingly after seven months off when eighth of 11 in 1m1f handicap at Ayr ten days ago, leading until 1f out; should strip sharper now and this low-mileage 4yo merits serious consideration in his bid for a breakthrough victory.
runners.comment: Encouraging Ayr eighth on return, leading until 1f out; merits serious consideration

runners.name: Nakatomi
runners.spotlight: Yet to score but posted his best effort for current yard dropped back markedly in trip when third of 14 in 8.5f Beverley handicap 11 days ago; possibilities.
runners.comment: Yet to score but good third of 14 in 8.5f Beverley handicap 11 days ago; possibilities
runners.owner: F Reay, S McMillan & S Pedersen

runners.name: Pisanello
runners.spotlight: Useful C&D winner at his best, but this veteran is without a victory since June 2024 and has offered little in a pair of handicaps (around 1m) at Doncaster and Beverley this spring; cheekpieces are retried.
runners.comment: Offered little in handicaps at Doncaster and Beverley this spring; cheekpieces retried

runners.name: Perfidia
runners.spotlight: Reliable sort who comes here on the back of a good second of 14 in 7f Redcar handicap last month; firmly in the picture off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Reliable sort; good second at Redcar last month; firmly in the picture off same mark

runners.name: Velvet Skies
runners.spotlight: Fair ex-Irish 6f winner; yet to hit top form in four runs for his current yard, tried in a visor when fourth of seven in 6f Southwell handicap 15 days ago; needs to step forward despite a falling mark.
runners.comment: Yet to hit top form for his current yard; needs to step forward despite falling mark

runners.name: Millbuie
runners.spotlight: 0-24 but returned to form when sixth of 11 in 1m1f handicap at Ayr ten days ago; others still preferred though.
runners.comment: 0-24 but returned to form when sixth of 11 in 1m1f handicap at Ayr ten days ago
runners.owner: N J Dalgarno & W R Ellis

runners.name: Approaching Dawn
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who has yet to offer much, last of seven in 7f Ayr handicap ten days ago.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 6yo who has yet to offer much, last of seven in Ayr handicap ten days ago
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Pallas Lord
runners.spotlight: Ended a long losing run at Newcastle (7f) in February but below par both runs since, sixth in 8.5f handicap at Wolverhampton last time; has a rare run on turf now.
runners.comment: Ended a losing run at Newcastle in February but below par both runs since; rare turf run

races.race_name: Inkerman London Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Road To Wembley
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner on the Flat for Richard Hughes and it's been a promising start over hurdles, winning a Southwell maiden last time without too much fuss (good); penalised accordingly but sets the standard.
runners.comment: Penalised for his comfortable Southwell win but that form sets the standard

runners.name: Maskarvel
runners.spotlight: Promise in Irish bumpers and ran well to be second in a Huntingdon maiden, for all that only three got round; looks mildly progressive.
runners.comment: Ran well to be second in a Huntingdon maiden, for all that only three got round

runners.name: Into Battle
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat, of similar ability to Road To Wembley; made hurdle debut in a race won by Sober Glory, after which he had wind surgery; chased home a promising horse at Kempton and shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Chased home a promising horse at Kempton and shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Phantom Gold
runners.spotlight: Won two bumpers, one in the pointing scene and the other under rules; however, nothing positive to take out of his two runs over hurdles.
runners.comment: Bumper winner but nothing positive to take out of his two runs over hurdles

runners.name: Jet Renegade
runners.spotlight: 0-3 in points and has the same strike-rate in both bumpers and on the Flat; he's by no means devoid of ability but needs to step up.
runners.comment: 0-3 in points and has the same strike-rate in both bumpers and on the Flat

runners.name: Red Nile
runners.spotlight: Had two years off since finishing 14l behind the winner in a 2m maiden at Market Rasen; opposable on a few counts.
runners.comment: Had two years off since finishing 14l behind the winner in a 2m maiden at Market Rasen

races.race_name: Ha Civils Franny Blennerhassett Memorial Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Masked Dance
runners.spotlight: Won this two years ago and was only a short-head away last year; higher in the weights now but had been in career best form until last run which was in a strong handicap; has to be feared.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024 and was only a short-head away last year; running well until last time

runners.name: My Chiquita
runners.spotlight: Runner-up to Masked Dance in this race two years ago and her final two runs of last year were right up there with her best; could easily be a factor if in the same form for new stable after a break.
runners.comment: Could easily be a factor if in top form for new stable after a break

runners.name: Atlantic City
runners.spotlight: Has hit the ground running for Olly Murphy and dug deep off 5lb lower at Perth last month (2m4f, good); looks progressive.
runners.comment: A winner at Perth last month and looks progressive; 5lb higher here

runners.name: I C U In My Dreams
runners.spotlight: Progressed in winning a Newcastle maiden (2m4f, good to soft) but didn't look on that lenient a mark for handicap debut at Bangor and was tailed off; down 3lb.
runners.comment: Didn't look on that lenient a mark for handicap debut at Bangor and was tailed off

runners.name: Magical Maggie
runners.spotlight: Very closely matched with Masked Dance on their two Ludlow encounters in February, over 2m5f (soft) and 3m (good to soft); she's fine after a break and should have a part to play.
runners.comment: Very closely matched with Masked Dance on their two Ludlow encounters in February

runners.name: Roaring Home
runners.spotlight: Quickened up best off a slow pace to prevail in a small field at Chepstow (2m7f, good); unlikely that represented any improvement on previous best efforts but only went up 4lb.
runners.comment: Quickened up best off a slow pace to prevail in a small field at Chepstow (2m7f)

runners.name: Mermaids Cave
runners.spotlight: Won by 10l last week at Huntingdon (2m3f, good) in a race she also won the previous season, when beating Masked Dance on similar terms to today's; clear claims.
runners.comment: Penalised for last week's comfortable win at Huntingdon and can deal with this mark

runners.name: Golden Glance
runners.spotlight: Won a Haydock maiden hurdle in 2023 (2m3f, good to soft) and lightly raced in the meantime; tailed off in her last three handicaps and had wind surgery since the latest; risky.
runners.comment: Tailed off in her last three handicaps and had wind surgery since the latest; risky

races.race_name: #hameansmore Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Southoftheborder
runners.spotlight: Finally came good over fences at Kempton (2m2f, good) and nearly followed up at Market Rasen, albeit when odds-on for a four-runner race; nudged up another 3lb for that defeat but still one to consider.
runners.comment: In form; nudged up another 3lb for recent defeat but still one to consider

runners.name: My Noble Lord
runners.spotlight: Back down in grade with blinkers tried he registered a second chase win when 6l to the good at Bangor (2m4f, good); 6lb rise has elevated him to a career-high mark but it was comfortable last time.
runners.comment: 6lb rise has elevated him to a career-high mark but it was comfortable last time

runners.name: Genois
runners.spotlight: Had lost his way but very much rejuvenated in his last four races and was closing on the winner, whom he beat the time before, over 2m4f at Haydock last time (good); edging up the weights but firmly in the reckoning.
runners.comment: Had lost his way but very much rejuvenated in his last four races; pretty solid

runners.name: Breizh River
runners.spotlight: Not as good as he was but has still won two of his last four races and has only raced six times for this yard; up 5lb for Kelso but couldn't dismiss.
runners.comment: Not as good as he was but has still won two of his last four races; raised 5lb for Kelso

runners.name: Fringill Dike
runners.spotlight: Continues to hurtle down the weights but latest 13l defeat at Perth was another unconvincing run.
runners.comment: Mark continues to tumble but latest 13l defeat at Perth was another unconvincing run

runners.name: Coastguard Station
runners.spotlight: Enjoys good ground; bumped into the enigmatic Numitor on a good day at Hereford last month (2m5f) but this 10yo also struggles with his consistency; he's capable but risky enough.
runners.comment: Enjoys good ground; bumped into the enigmatic Numitor on a good day at Hereford last month

runners.name: The Flier Begley
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Haydock last time (behind Genois) in a race he won the previous season; now below par in his last three chases and belatedly tries cheekpieces at the age of 11.
runners.comment: Below par in his last three chases and belatedly tries cheekpieces at the age of 11

runners.name: Bleu d'Enfer
runners.spotlight: Back from wind surgery he bumped into one well handicapped at Bangor (2m4f, good) and it was a strong race in which he struggled at Haydock; he's no forlorn hope on that penultimate effort.
runners.comment: Bumped into one at Bangor and then contested a strong handicap at Haydock

runners.name: Parisencore
runners.spotlight: Made the most of a reduced mark to snap his losing run over 2m4f at Newcastle (good) and a 4lb rise should be workable if he can repeat that performance; has held his form in the past.
runners.comment: Made the most of a reduced mark to snap his losing run over 2m4f at Newcastle; up 4lb
runners.owner: Mrs I C Sellars And Miss D C Sellars

races.race_name: Hatfields Jaguar Land Rover Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Brewin'upastorm
runners.spotlight: Veteran who finds winning hard these days but on last two starts he held his own in Grade 2s at Fontwell and Fairyhouse, races he had won previously; retains plenty of enthusiasm and can go well back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Admirable veteran who retains plenty of enthusiasm and can go well back in a handicap

runners.name: Ike Sport
runners.spotlight: Ran quite well in the Pertemps Final and then runner-up in a big field handicap here in April (2m4f, good to soft; in this new headgear combination), improving on his fifth place the previous season; also ran second to Fingal's Hill in this 12 months ago; has nothing in hand off his mark but every chance of being involved.
runners.comment: Good record here; has nothing in hand off his mark but every chance of being involved

runners.name: Rambo T
runners.spotlight: It's been a struggle in some competitive handicaps but he ran well enough here at the National meeting (behind Ike Sport) to merit some attention; others still preferred.
runners.comment: Ran well enough at the National meeting to merit some attention

runners.name: Fingal's Hill
runners.spotlight: 5lb higher than when a 6l winner of this last year, beating Ike Sport; returns on the back of two solid efforts at Newcastle; chance.
runners.comment: Last year's winner who returns on the back of two solid efforts at Newcastle

runners.name: El Galactico
runners.spotlight: Won two of his first three races over hurdles; the wheels have fallen off this year and he pulled up on his last two runs, latest in first-time cheekpieces (retained); a hood is now on for the first time.
runners.comment: Career started well but threatening to lose his way and a hood is now tried
runners.owner: Feilim O' Muiri And Brendan Barrett

runners.name: Lightningupourdays
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 7yo whose form has substance and it was a good handicap at Hereford in which he was third in December; went off favourite in a big field at Ascot in February but never gave his running; could be interesting back from a break.
runners.comment: Had a break since poor run in February but previous third at Hereford reads well

runners.name: Smokeringinthedark
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and improving; beat Fingal's Hill by 2l at Newcastle (2m4f) before finding only one too good upped to Class 2 level at Haydock (3m, good); didn't fail for stamina that day but he'll be fine back in trip; player.
runners.comment: Lightly raced and improving having produced career bests in last two races (2m4f/3m)

runners.name: Static
runners.spotlight: Won at about 2m3f at Haydock (good to soft) and Bangor (good) and the combination of a deep race and the drop back to 2m found him out at Aintree; better expected here off what should still be a realistic mark.
runners.comment: 2m too sharp last time and better expected here off what should still be a realistic mark

runners.name: Luttrell Lad
runners.spotlight: Won at Exeter and then a close fourth at Sandown (2m4f, good); could put a line through his subsequent races on account of heavy ground and a trip too far; respected.
runners.comment: Can put a line through his subsequent races on account of heavy ground and a trip too far

runners.name: Last Kingdom
runners.spotlight: Both hurdles wins when trained by Willie Mullins; has shown little in four starts for current yard and hard to be at all confident.
runners.comment: Ex-Willie Mullins; shown little in four starts for current yard

runners.name: Break Point
runners.spotlight: Has only won a maiden hurdle when trained by Olly Murphy; two modest runs in low-grade AW races since leaving that yard.
runners.comment: Two modest runs in low-grade AW races since leaving Olly Murphy for whom he won a maiden

races.race_name: Huyton Asphalt Franny Blennerhassett Memorial Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Taxus Baccata
runners.spotlight: Has improved since undergoing wind surgery for her new trainer this year and went back-to-back at Leicester (2m4f, good to soft) and Wincanton (3m1f, good); last run was too bad to be true but she did go up 7lb for that last win in a race that fell apart.
runners.comment: Ran flat last time but did go up 7lb for winning previous race that fell apart

runners.name: Bumpy Evans
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who is 5-10 over fences but the handicapper is beginning to look in charge and he worryingly refused to race last week at Market Rasen; cheekpieces are soon turned to.
runners.comment: 5-10 over fences but refused to race last week and cheekpieces are soon turned to

runners.name: Calimystic
runners.spotlight: Didn't hang about over hurdles and now 2-2 over fences, the latter win gained at Sandown (3m, soft) in March; withdrawn three times since then with good ground deemed unsuitable but his first two wins (one over hurdles) were gained on good; probably still has more improvement to come; must be considered.
runners.comment: Didn't hang about over hurdles and now 2-2 over fences; likely that he has more to offer

runners.name: Loughderg Rocco
runners.spotlight: Started well over fences for a different yard in 2022 but, following a mammoth absence, has struggled to get competitive in 2m4f/3m handicaps for this one; current ability is hard to gauge and a tongue-tie is added after wind surgery.
runners.comment: Current ability is hard to gauge and a tongue-tie is added after wind surgery

runners.name: Jacks Parrot
runners.spotlight: Has performed to a similar level in his last four handicaps, from 3m1f to 3m5f; did get outpaced over 3m1f at Haydock last time before staying on admirably to be hot on the heels of My Bobby Dazzler; he could see this out stronger than some.
runners.comment: Competitive and performer to a similar level in his last four handicaps, from 3m1f to 3m5f

runners.name: Aboutdamntime
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-6 over fences and when beaten at Kelso last time that was at the hands of an improver (3m2f, good to soft); gets in off the same mark here and could be a factor
runners.comment: 2-6 over fences and when beaten at Kelso it was at the hands of an improver

runners.name: Regarde
runners.spotlight: Has had three wind operations and tends to go well when fresh, so might not improve markedly on his latest second at Haydock (3m4f, good) after 144 days away; still ran well though and assured stamina will come in handy.
runners.comment: Second over much further at Haydock last time and assured stamina will come in handy

runners.name: My Bobby Dazzler
runners.spotlight: Went up 5lb for winning a Class 4 at Hereford (3m1f, soft) and raised another 1lb for his second in a better race at Haydock (3m1f, good), finishing just ahead of Jacks Parrot; this 11yo is thriving and entitled to play a part in the finish.
runners.comment: Second at this level last time; this 11yo is thriving and entitled to play a part

runners.name: Erne River
runners.spotlight: Still runs well now and again but this 11yo is not as good as he was and become inconsistent.
runners.comment: Runs well now and again but this 11yo is not as good as he was and become inconsistent

runners.name: Fortunedefortunata
runners.spotlight: Had smart form when with Gordon Elliott before losing his way and selling for £3,000; outran odds of 100-1 when just behind Regarde at Haydock in April but then finished tailed off at Perth; others safer.
runners.comment: Penultimate effort a reminder than he has still has it but has disappointed since

races.race_name: Mental Health Awareness Week Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Jamada
runners.spotlight: Delivered on earlier promise when winning four-runner Fakenham maiden (2m, good) last month, his fifth start over hurdles; contests much deeper race on this handicap debut but probably still has potential; one to consider.
runners.comment: Switches to a handicap after winning four-runner maiden last month; still has potential

runners.name: Spartan Times
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer who battled well to win 16-runner handicap hurdle at Uttoxeter (2m, good) this month, after a break, and is now 2-4 in this sphere; 5lb claimer William Shanahan is enlisted today; high on the list.
runners.comment: Battled well to win big-field Uttoxeter handicap this month and is now 2-4 over hurdles

runners.name: Book Of Secrets
runners.spotlight: Ran quite well when fourth at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) in March and remains on a good mark but his last win came in summer 2023 and others in this line-up have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Ran quite well last time and remains on a good mark but last win came in summer 2023

runners.name: A Good Yomp
runners.spotlight: Caused 28-1 surprise in first-time hood at Huntingdon (2m3f, good to soft) in November and remained in good form until end of last season (2m-2m7f); edging back down the weights and holds each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Has remained in good form since his win in November and holds each-way claims at least

runners.name: Lively Citizen
runners.spotlight: Long in the tooth and no longer the force of old but bounced back from two poor chase runs with respectable effort over hurdles at Haydock (1m7f, good) last month and is now 5lb lower than when going close in this race a year ago; could have a say.
runners.comment: Ran okay when back over hurdles last month; now 5lb lower than when second in this in 2025
runners.owner: Mark E Smith And Keith M Hawes

runners.name: Juan Bermudez
runners.spotlight: Gained all three hurdle wins at Stratford; returned to form with close second there (2m2f, good) last month and can probably cope with subsequent 2lb nudge but he's away from his favourite track today.
runners.comment: Returned to form with close second at favourite track Stratford last month

runners.name: Uhtred Ragnarson
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who returned from layoff with clear win at Warwick (2m, good) in November; absent since last-flight fall at Newbury in January but has possibilities if fully tuned up after latest break.
runners.comment: Off since falling in January but won when fresh in November and is still lightly raced

runners.name: Land Of Punt
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who might have been stretched by 2m5f when third of nine on handicap debut at Haydock (good to soft; wore cheekpieces) in March; contender if combination of drop back in trip and switch to visor helps.
runners.comment: Might have been stretched by 2m5f when placed on handicap debut; remains unexposed

runners.name: Promethean
runners.spotlight: Quite useful on the Flat at his best; always behind on first two hurdling starts and didn't achieve huge amount when well-held third in slowly run Southwell maiden (2m, good) last month but is still worth a glance in the betting before this handicap hurdle debut; tongue-tie refitted.
runners.comment: Made quiet start to hurdling career but may yet transfer some of his Flat ability

runners.name: Grey Skies
runners.spotlight: Won twice in blinkers on good ground last summer (2m/2m1f) but was given a break after two disappointing runs in the autumn and made a low-key return without blinkers last month; has blinkers back on today but needs to get back in the groove.
runners.comment: Made low-key return from break last month and needs to get back in the groove

runners.name: Theophilos
runners.spotlight: Fair on the Flat when based in Ireland but unplaced all five hurdling starts there; made low-key stable debut (on the Flat) last month and has quite a bit more to prove than some of these.
runners.comment: Made low-key stable debut on the Flat last month; others have much less to prove

races.race_name: Mental Health Awareness Week Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Guet Apens
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat when trained in France and, after a long absence, has made solid start to British hurdling career this year; followed March's Carlisle novice win (2m1f, soft) with second behind subsequent Scottish Champion Hurdle winner Dedicated Hero in Kelso handicap (2m5f, good) last month; drops back in trip with cheekpieces/tongue-tie added here; good chance.
runners.comment: Second to subsequent Scottish Champion Hurdle winner on last month's handicap debut

runners.name: Caelan
runners.spotlight: Just 1-8 over hurdles but returned from break with creditable second behind in-form stablemate at Ffos Las (2m, good) last month and remains on a workable mark; respected.
runners.comment: Returned from break with good second last month and remains on a workable mark

runners.name: Inappropriate
runners.spotlight: Failed to win during rather mixed campaign last season but last month's keeping-on second at Newcastle (2m1f, good) was a good effort and the form has been well advertised by the winner since.
runners.comment: Had mixed record last season but last month's Newcastle second makes him a big player

runners.name: Disguisedlimit
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in four of his six chases for the Greenall/Guerriero stable last season and reverts to hurdling for new yard on a workable mark but others appeal more all the same; tongue-tie/cheekpieces removed.
runners.comment: Fair chase form last season; back hurdling on workable mark for new stable

runners.name: Emerald Time
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who made the frame in both bumpers and showed hurdling ability in his three qualifying runs in February/March; open to improvement on this handicap debut; market may guide.
runners.comment: Showed ability in bumpers and in his qualifying races; improvement possible in handicaps

runners.name: Mojo Ego
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice at Cartmel (2m1f) last summer and made the frame over C&D in November but absent since below-par run on Boxing Day and entitled to benefit from this outing.
runners.comment: Absent since below-par run on Boxing Day and entitled to benefit from this outing

runners.name: Zephlyn
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner on the Flat but 0-5 over hurdles; just a respectable fourth on recent handicap hurdle debut at Perth (2m4f, good) and may not be ideally suited by this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Respectable fourth over 2m4f on handicap hurdle debut; drop back in trip may not be ideal

runners.name: Mr Lincoln
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles and has a tendency to race too freely but ran by far his best race for Iain Jardine when very close second at Carlisle (2m1f, good to soft) in March, and remains well handicapped on older Irish form.
runners.comment: 0-8 over hurdles but went very close at Carlisle in March; can't be ignored

runners.name: Roarin' Success
runners.spotlight: Quite useful at his best on the Flat and displayed promise in three novice/maiden hurdles during the winter; returned to the Flat with two underwhelming runs last month but remains open to improvement in this sphere.
runners.comment: Quite useful at best on the Flat and has displayed promise in three novice/maiden hurdles
runners.owner: Hale Racing, Lyle Farming Company & Ptnr

runners.name: Maple
runners.spotlight: Scored over hurdles at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) last month and also ran well on the Flat three weeks ago; likely to give another good account here but might find others better handicapped.
runners.comment: In good form over hurdles/on the Flat but may find others better handicapped
runners.owner: R Whitelock, L Clemitson & R Jefferson

races.race_name: "Point To Point" Bumper National Hunt Flat Race (Amateur Jockeys' Race) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Call Me Ken
runners.spotlight: Won a point bumper on soft ground in early November and followed up on good at Exeter; had Timmie Two some 9l behind on the latter occasion and that horse was close up behind Island Jetaway the run before.
runners.comment: Won a point bumper on soft ground in early November and followed up on good at Exeter

runners.name: Well This Won't Do
runners.spotlight: Won a point bumper by 7l in December but pulled up since then at 3m and 2m5f.
runners.comment: Won a point bumper by 7l in December but pulled up since then at 3m and 2m5f

runners.name: Maiden Over
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in one of these at Milborne St Andrew but trailed the winner by 30l.
runners.comment: Runner-up in one of these at Milborne St Andrew but trailed the winner by 30l

runners.name: Wise Maiden
runners.spotlight: Respected connections and made light work of Pass The Port in a point bumper in March.
runners.comment: Respected connections and made light work of Pass The Port in a point bumper in March

runners.name: Island Jetaway
runners.spotlight: Won narrowly at Larkhill (2m); a line through the second suggests Call Me Ken may have her measure.
runners.comment: Won narrowly at Larkhill; one formline suggests Call Me Ken may have her measure

runners.name: Briskalo
runners.spotlight: 2m4f maiden point winner who has held his form with recent seconds over 3m; respected connections.
runners.comment: 2m4f maiden point winner who has held his form with recent seconds over 3m

runners.name: Carrillo
runners.spotlight: Three point wins at 2m4f (twice) and 3m, and was favourite for all of those; clearly held in some regard and James King knows him well.
runners.comment: Three point wins at 2m4f (twice) and 3m, and was favourite for all of those

runners.name: College Road
runners.spotlight: Made all and sauntered home by 20l in a small-field point bumper at Sandon last month.
runners.comment: Made all and sauntered home by 20l in a small-field point bumper at Sandon last month

runners.name: G N Jefferson
runners.spotlight: Beaten 24l and 15l in two points, at 3m and 2m4f respectively.
runners.comment: Beaten 24l and 15l in two points, at 3m and 2m4f respectively

runners.name: Laugusto
runners.spotlight: Kept it simple from the front when winning a seven-race point bumper by 7l last month.
runners.comment: Kept in simple from the front when winning a seven-race point bumper by 7l last month

runners.name: Pass The Port
runners.spotlight: Ran in a point bumper two months ago and was no match for Wise Maiden, going down by 12l.
runners.comment: Ran in a point bumper two months ago and was no match for Wise Maiden

runners.name: Via Romano
runners.spotlight: Partnered by James King in his two points but not this evening; ran second in the first but was pulled up in the second.
runners.comment: Partnered by James King in his two points but not this evening; pulled up in the latest

runners.name: Handsome Stan
runners.spotlight: Has two runs in point bumpers, proving much more competitive in the second which he won by just over 5l; positive jockey booking.
runners.comment: Won his latest point bumper by over 5l and has a positive jockey booking

runners.name: Oxo Called
runners.spotlight: One of the favourites for both his point bumpers and was a lot closer in the second, beaten by his market rival by just under 3l; finished some way behind Island Jetaway on his debut.
runners.comment: One of the favourites for both his point bumpers and was a lot closer in the second

runners.name: Run Happy
runners.spotlight: Another runner to have hinted at winning potential with competitive runs in defeat in point bumpers, last time getting outpaced by still only 3l off the winner.
runners.comment: Has hinted at winning potential with competitive runs in defeat in point bumpers
runners.owner: The Hon A Vestey, Sangwin & J Scutt

runners.name: Silver Salver
runners.spotlight: Led over the last fence in his 2m4f point only to be reeled in late on; only five ran.
runners.comment: Led over the last fence in his 2m4f point only to be reeled in late on; only five ran

races.race_name: Sky Bet Fillies' Stakes (Registered As The Michael Seely Memorial Fillies' Stakes) (Listed Race)

runners.name: Act Of Kindness
runners.spotlight: Won at Newmarket (7f, good) last October on second start and kept on for third on reappearance in the Group 3 Nell Gwyn there (7f, good) last month; this half-sister to Group 1 Fillies' Mile winner Ylang Ylang is open to further improvement now tackling 1m and could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Kept on for third in the 7f Nell Gwyn and she's open to improvement now tackling 1m

runners.name: Pacific Mission
runners.spotlight: Won at Kempton (7f, AW) last August on second run and, despite slow starts, posted fine efforts when runner-up on next two outings, in the Group 2 May Hill at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) and the Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies Turf at Del Mar (1m, firm); leading form claims on return.
runners.comment: Runner-up at the Breeders' Cup last October and has leading form claims on her return

runners.name: Botagoz
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Kempton (7f, AW) last December and made it 2-2 with 2l win in Nottingham novice (8.3f, good) last month; this is a much stiffer test of her credentials but she's a half-sister to a Listed winner, and her dam won a Group 2, and it remains to be seen where her limitations lie.
runners.comment: 2-2; up in grade today but bred for the task; remains to be seen where her limitations lie

runners.name: So Regal
runners.spotlight: Promising third on sole 2yo start (7f, AW) and 2-2 this year following wins in a maiden at Newmarket (1m, good) and novice at Ascot (1m, good to firm); this well-bred filly is in excellent hands to continue to progress (one of two for the stable) and may well take this rise in grade in her stride.
runners.comment: 2-2 this year and this well-bred filly may well take today's rise in grade in her stride

runners.name: Fashion People
runners.spotlight: Odds-on for both starts and impressive each time, at Newcastle (7f, AW) last November and at Yarmouth (7f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; she could do with settling better now at 1m and others have more substance to their form, but she's in top hands (one of two for the yard) and it's hard to identify where the ceiling of her ability will sit; not ruled out.
runners.comment: 2-2; others have stronger form but not ruled out for top stable (one of two for yard)

runners.name: Lilt
runners.spotlight: 7-2, set a steady pace when making all on debut at Doncaster (7f, soft) last October; prevailed by just a neck and is bottom of this pack on RPRs but she's a half-sister to a 7f/1m Listed winner for connections, and her dam was a US 9.5f/1m2f Grade 1 winner; she could have lots more to offer.
runners.comment: Had just a neck to spare on sole 2yo start but well bred and could have lots more to offer

runners.name: Synchronicity
runners.spotlight: 900,000gns yearling who is a sister to 1m1f/1m2f Group 1 winner Ombudsman and she was sent off the 6-4 favourite when winning a newcomers' race at Newbury (7f, good) last month; she drew nicely clear in the closing stages and the runner-up, fourth and fifth have all won since; looks set to be suited by the step up to 1m and she's an exciting prospect.
runners.comment: Sister to Group 1 winner Ombudsman; won on debut last month and she's an exciting prospect

races.race_name: Titan Wealth Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Al Durry
runners.spotlight: Has raced on only the AW, with her wins at Southwell (1m) in October and on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (9.5f) in March both coming after five-month layoffs; it's unclear how she will cope with turf but she is undoubtedly progressive.
runners.comment: Has raced on only AW; unclear how she will cope with turf, but undoubtedly progressive

runners.name: Clouds Hill
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed the run of the race when making all to beat four rivals on his Yarmouth return (1m2f, good) 15 days ago; has a 5lb penalty to carry but remains unexposed after just five starts, so makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: 5lb higher than for his successful Yarmouth reappearance but still unexposed; shortlisted
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Alcarath
runners.spotlight: Bolted up from the front in an Epsom novice (8.5f, good) last September but twice unplaced in handicaps, including on his reappearance at Newmarket (1m1f, good to firm) 13 days ago; should be better for that run, but more is needed.
runners.comment: Should be better for his recent Newmarket reappearance but more is needed

runners.name: High Degree
runners.spotlight: Narrowly landed skinny odds in a Ffos Las maiden (1m, good) last July and although out of the first three in three big-field handicaps (1m/1m2f) subsequently, he was far from disgraced; gelded since last seen seven months ago but looks the type with more to offer this year.
runners.comment: Gelded since last seen seven months ago but looks the type with more to offer this year

runners.name: Something Splendid
runners.spotlight: 2-13, his latest success coming off this mark at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) last September after the first past the post was disqualified for a banned substance; off seven months but has gone well fresh before; each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Off seven months but has gone well fresh before; each-way possibilities

runners.name: Celeborn
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who was returning from seven months off when winning at Epsom (8.5f, good) in April last year; off again since and 9lb higher, but he is clearly proven fresh and his sire should ensure that this longer trip is within range; respected.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo; off for over a year but is proven fresh and should stay this far; respected
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Hand Of God
runners.spotlight: Talented 5yo, but has endured some lengthy absences in his career and was returning from another 280 days off when seventh of 24 in the Spring Cup here (1m, good) last month; return to this trip is not a problem and he's interesting down in grade.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo; return to this trip is not a problem and he's interesting down in grade

runners.name: Oursin
runners.spotlight: Has raced only three times, all on the AW and winning two 1m novices in the autumn; makes his handicap/turf debut after 200 days off, but the longer trip should suit on breeding.
runners.comment: AW record reads 211; handicap/turf debut after 200 days off; the longer trip should suit

runners.name: Al Aali
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in France (9.5f, AW) as a 2yo; didn't show enough on his stable debut at Goodwood (1m2f, good; first start since being gelded) 13 days ago to make him of immediate interest.
runners.comment: Didn't show enough on his stable debut at Goodwood 13 days ago to make him of interest

runners.name: Mustazeed
runners.spotlight: 4-9 over C&D including landing this race three years ago, but his starts have increasingly become a problem, flatly refusing to do so here in October; too risky.
runners.comment: 4-9 over C&D including this race in 2023, but his starts have become a big problem

runners.name: Parole d'Oro
runners.spotlight: Sole success came at Epsom (7f, good to firm) as a 2yo, though he twice finished runner-up last year including at York (1m2f, good; first start since being gelded) in October; off since but he has gone well fresh in the past; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Off since finishing second at York in October but has gone well fresh in the past

races.race_name: Support Macmillan Charity Raceday On 13 June Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Financer
runners.spotlight: Runner-up three times last season, including in a big field over C&D (good), and he could improve for last month's reappearance run at Ripon; however, this is a tough contest in which to try to snap a 13-race losing run.
runners.comment: Some good runs last season, including over C&D, but his losing sequence is up to 13
runners.owner: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor

runners.name: Thunder Run
runners.spotlight: Disappointing in the Cambridgeshire at Newmarket on final outing last season and same story in Listed race at Kempton on last month's reappearance; however, he's back down to the same mark as when winning this last year (good to firm) and can have a big say if he's back on song.
runners.comment: A return to form is needed but he races off the same mark as when winning this last year

runners.name: Cadarn
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row at Redcar (1m, good to firm) last spring for George Boughey and he's just 1lb higher than when last successful; effective at 1m2f earlier in career; however, he hasn't been at his best on his last four starts, for this yard on the last two occasions, and he has to raise his game.
runners.comment: Won two in a row last spring but hasn't been at his best on his four starts since

runners.name: Warrant Holder
runners.spotlight: Built upon his previous promise (including on turf) to win in good style in a small field at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) last October on his second handicap start; gelded since and this lightly raced 4yo could have lots more to offer this season for his top stable; wears a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Impressive when last seen and this lightly raced 4yo could have lots more to offer
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Regal Ulixes
runners.spotlight: Two novice wins on turf and he made it 3-6 when scoring at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in February on his second handicap start; solid fourth to The Glen Rovers at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) last month and, while improvement is necessary today, this is just his ninth run and he may have more left in the tank.
runners.comment: Solid fourth on AW last time; lightly raced and he may have more left in the tank

runners.name: Have Secret
runners.spotlight: 6yo who is on a career-high mark but he looked better than ever when winning by just over 2l at Pontefract (1m2f, good) last month; close third in this last year (good to firm) and could again be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Better than ever when winning at Pontefract latest; third in this last year; thereabouts

runners.name: Double Parked
runners.spotlight: Prominent-racer who won twice during last season's 3yo campaign (1m, good to soft/good to firm) and returned to form when third at Redcar (1m, good) last Thursday on his second start of the season; it's possible this new trip will be within range and he has an each-way shout if seeing it out.
runners.comment: Good third over 1m at Redcar last week; each-way shout if seeing out this new trip

runners.name: Castle Stuart
runners.spotlight: Close third of 15 on stable debut over C&D (good to firm) last August when 3lb higher; he hasn't kicked on subsequently but last month's reappearance sixth at Doncaster gives him something to build on; not discounted.
runners.comment: Has failed to kick on since his good run over C&D last August but he's not discounted

runners.name: Per Contra
runners.spotlight: Neck second of 15 over C&D (good to firm) last August and his seventh of 12 at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) last month can be upgraded as he was slowly away in a race which favoured those ridden more prominently; he's one to consider back up in trip.
runners.comment: Neck C&D 2nd last August and last month's Musselburgh run can be upgraded; one to consider

runners.name: Empire Of Light
runners.spotlight: Made all at Chester (1m2f, heavy) last September and did likewise to follow up on reappearance at Haydock (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago; he had just a head to spare last time and is up 4lb in a higher grade today, but this 5yo is clearly in fine heart and could make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Front-runner who has won his last two; up in grade but this 5yo is clearly in fine heart

runners.name: Ashnak
runners.spotlight: French 2yo winner in 2024 and remains lightly raced; ran well when third of six at Ripon (1m4f, good) last month on his second stable start, when shaping as though this drop back in trip would be a plus, and he could have more to offer for a yard that has excelled with similar types over the years.
runners.comment: Ex-French 4yo; shaped when third over 1m4f at Ripon as though this shorter trip would help

runners.name: Zryan
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins in the winter (9.4f/1m2f); it's possible he's gone off the boil but when beaten 8l in ninth of 13 at Newmarket a fortnight ago he was denied a clear passage before keeping on nicely; not ruled out each-way back up in trip.
runners.comment: It's possible he's gone off the boil but the step back up in trip could prove a positive

runners.name: The Glen Rovers
runners.spotlight: 7yo who is lightly raced for his age and made it 6-12 on AW when winning a competitive 1m2f handicap at Newcastle last month; achieved very little on turf at the start of his career but he's a different model now and he could be in the shake-up if the return to grass isn't an issue.
runners.comment: Won competitive AW h'cap last time; could be in shake-up if return to grass isn't an issue

runners.name: Tony Montana
runners.spotlight: Beaten on his last 12 starts going back to May 2024 but he's run some big races in the meantime, including when beaten a head in this last year (good to firm) off the same mark as today; reappeared with solid sixth of 24 on stable debut in the Spring Cup at Newbury (1m, good); can be in the mix.
runners.comment: Has the same mark as when a head runner-up in this last year and he can be in the mix

runners.name: Altareq
runners.spotlight: Improved upon his first two runs when going close for the Gosdens in Newbury maiden (1m2f, good) last May; gelded subsequently and sold for 95,000gns last October; this well-bred 4yo is a very interesting recruit for Jim Goldie but he makes his handicap/stable debut in a hot race following a year off, and it's hard to know what to expect.
runners.comment: Ex-Gosden 4yo; sold for 95,000gns; makes handicap/stable debut in hot race after absence

runners.name: Chillingham
runners.spotlight: Promoted to first in 1m4f handicap here (good to firm) last June and he races off 2lb lower today; however, he wasn't at his best last year on his final few runs for Edward Bethell, and failed to threaten over hurdles in the winter on his first two starts for this yard; may be best watched.
runners.comment: Course winner who is on a handy mark but he failed to shine over hurdles in the winter

races.race_name: Design Tonic Out Of This World Spaces At The Races EBF Marygate Fillies' Stakes (Listed) (IRE Inc')

runners.name: Lover Girl
runners.spotlight: Amo runner who is two from two, namely 5f wins in Wolverhampton maiden (AW) and Salisbury conditions race (good to firm); leading player on the figures and may continue to make hay while the iron's hot.
runners.comment: Amo runner who is two from two, latest win in Salisbury conditions race; leading claims

runners.name: Princesse d'Orange
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism with quite an impressive 4l success from the front in Beverley contest (5f, good to soft) and may well prove Listed class or even better; promising contender who commands respect.
runners.comment: Quite impressive at Beverley and may well prove Listed class or even better; respected

runners.name: Lady Blanche
runners.spotlight: Persian Force filly who made all for an authoritative success at Bath (5.7f, firm) to build on her Beverley debut (5f, good to soft); looks potentially useful and helps to give Amo Racing a strong hand.
runners.comment: Recorded an authoritative win at Bath last time and helps to give Amo Racing a good hand

runners.name: Angels Passing
runners.spotlight: 130,000euros yearling; No Nay Never half-sister to six winners, notably Duneflower (7f/1m including 2yo/Listed; RPR 107); dam Group-placed 7f 2yo scorer (101); sole debutante in the field; check the betting.
runners.comment: 130,000euros yearling; by No Nay Never; sole debutante in the field; check the betting

runners.name: Lake Muritz
runners.spotlight: Backed up 50-1 effort behind Niewiadoma to go one better at Beverley (5f, good to soft) where she beat four rivals; however, looks short of Listed standard and is unlikely to follow up.
runners.comment: Went one better at Beverley but looks unlikely to follow up in this grade

runners.name: Lauralynn
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly whose consistent but modest form comprises placed efforts at Beverley and a success at Southwell (5f, AW); has the worst chance on the figures upped in grade.
runners.comment: Has the worst chance judged on her consistent but modest form
runners.owner: Ger O'Neill & Unique Retreats Stays Ltd

runners.name: Love A Giggle
